title: [four-lens-validator] fix Bash 3.2 shebang compatibility
description:
Discovered during flywheel-og4v rework on 2026-05-04. `.flywheel/scripts/validate-callback-before-close.sh` uses `declare -A` associative arrays for four-lens state, but its shebang is `#!/usr/bin/env bash`; on macOS this resolves to `/bin/bash` 3.2, where `declare -A` is invalid. The script emitted invalid-option/unbound-variable diagnostics and still printed a verdict, so close validation can degrade silently unless invoked with `/opt/homebrew/bin/bash` or rewritten to a POSIX/Bash-3-compatible shape.

Evidence: `/tmp/flywheel-og4v-validator.txt` from Bash 5 passes; `/bin/bash` invocation emitted `declare: -A: invalid option`.

Acceptance:
- Validator declares and enforces a Bash 4+ interpreter path or removes associative-array dependency.
- Test proves direct script invocation on this macOS host runs four-lens logic without diagnostics.
- Failure to meet interpreter requirements exits nonzero, not SAFE_TO_CLOSE.
- README/dispatch-template names the correct invocation until fixed.
- Add fixture covering public_lens=no_bar_self_grade still fails when evidence lacks the self-grade.

Three-Q: VALIDATED with direct invocation fixture; DOCUMENTED in validator usage; SURFACED via doctor or dispatch-template gate if interpreter drift recurs.

title: publishability-bar-zeststream-soul-binding
description:
[publishability-bar-zeststream-soul-binding] bind publishability bar's "Josh judge" facet to zeststream-brand-voice + zesttube + zeststream.ai canonical artifacts — every public repo embodies Joshua

## Trigger
Joshua 2026-05-04: "i want my public work and ultimately most of my work to follow my /zeststream-brand-voice and the work i've done in zesttube - and with my zeststream.ai website. its got to embody ME"

## Depends on
- flywheel-wcq5 (publishability-bar-three-judges-doctrine) — defines the 7-facet bar
- ~/.claude/skills/zeststream-brand-voice/ — already-rigorous brand machinery (16-dim rubric, hard-reject rules, claims-grounding, Meadows-anchored)
- ~/Developer/zesttube — reference repo for what "Joshua-built" looks like in code+docs
- ~/Developer/zeststream.ai (or its current home) — reference website for canonical voice

## Meadows leverage
- #2 PARADIGM: shifts the question from "is this a good repo?" to "does this READ as Joshua's work?" — the brand is a paradigm anchor that makes the bar concrete and personal
- #5 RULES (downstream): every flywheel-touched public repo's README + MISSION + demo passes zeststream-brand-voice 16-dim rubric ≥95 composite, no dim <9
- #6 INFORMATION FLOWS: the bar surface (publishability_bar_score doctor signal) now includes brand_voice_composite_score per repo — visible drift detection

## What this binds
Updates the publishability-bar facet "code aesthetic" + "README front-door" + "demo-ability" (Josh judge) from abstract "AaaS-grade polish" to concrete:

1. **Voice constants enforced** — README + MISSION + landing copy on every public repo passes zeststream-brand-voice voice.yaml gate (canon line, banned words, first-person singular, Three Moves)
2. **Grounded claims only** — every factual claim in repo public docs maps to data/capabilities-ground-truth.yaml; receipts > testimonials
3. **Trademark rendering correct** — `The Yuzu Method ®` first use, `Peel. Press. Pour.™` motto, exactly
4. **No third-person corporate voice** — "I" not "we", "ZestStream delivers" auto-rejected
5. **No enemy/doomer AI framing** — partnership frame only
6. **Jeff attribution maintained** — NTM, Agent Mail, beads, CASS cited to Jeff Emanuel where used; Joshua's prior art (zesttube, watcherctl, flywheel) cited to Joshua
7. **Confident-not-cocky** (per references/CONFIDENT-NOT-COCKY.md) — evidenced, direct, no overclaim, no person-as-bottleneck

## The two reference embodiments
- **zesttube** — Joshua's reference repo: how a Joshua-built codebase reads (MISSION, AGENTS.md L-rules cadence, doctor/health/repair, executable verification, install-substrate)
- **zeststream-brand-voice skill** — the already-built enforcement engine; not authoring a new system, BINDING the publishability bar to it
- **zeststream.ai** — canonical voice in the wild; the bar must produce repos that read as siblings of the website

## Acceptance gates

AG1: update flywheel-wcq5 doctrine (.flywheel/PUBLISHABILITY-BAR.md) facet "code aesthetic" to require zeststream-brand-voice 16-dim ≥95 composite on README + MISSION + landing copy for any public repo (private/internal repos exempt)
AG2: doctor signal extension: `publishability_bar_score` becomes object `{score, brand_voice_composite, banned_words_count, ungrounded_claims_count}` — fails readiness if brand_voice_composite <90 OR banned_words_count >0 OR ungrounded_claims_count >0
AG3: per-repo .flywheel/PUBLISHABILITY-AUDIT.md template adds "ZestStream voice score" row with link to zeststream-brand-voice scorecard log
AG4: AGENTS.md L## entry "L## ZESTSTREAM-VOICE-PUBLIC-REPO-CANONICAL" cross-referencing zeststream-brand-voice skill + zesttube reference + flywheel-wcq5
AG5: ft04 propagation: every flywheel-installed public repo gets a pre-publish hook that runs zeststream-brand-voice scorecard against README + MISSION before any `git push public`
AG6: Three-judges synthetic-judge prompt template UPGRADE — Josh judge prompt explicitly cites zeststream-brand-voice voice.yaml + zesttube as reference, not abstract criteria
AG7: per-repo audit beads (downstream of AG5) for: flywheel, skillos, mobile-eats, alps, picoz, clutterfreespaces, zesttube, zeststream-infra — each scores current README+MISSION against zeststream-brand-voice and files gap-beads for failing facets
AG8: receipt at /tmp/publishability-zeststream-soul-evidence.md cites: doctrine update path, doctor signal source, L-rule entry, per-repo audit beads filed

## Per the new contract: callback MUST report did/didnt/gaps
- DID: each AG with citation
- DIDNT: AGs you can't meet (file gap-bead before callback)
- GAPS: repos where current voice score is far below 90 (file gap-beads as `voice-gap-<repo>.<N>`)

## Callback envelope
/Users/josh/.local/bin/ntm send flywheel --pane=1 "DONE flywheel-zeststream-soul task_id=<id> did=<n>/8 didnt=<bead-ids-or-none> gaps=<bead-ids-or-none> evidence=/tmp/publishability-zeststream-soul-evidence.md tests=PASS callback_delivery_verified=true"

## Skills cited
- zeststream-brand-voice (PRIMARY — the binding target)
- donella-meadows-systems-thinking (paradigm + goals leverage)
- skill-builder (rubric authoring)
- canonical-cli-scoping (doctor signal surface)
- writing-docs (README front-door facet)
- zeststream-pr (PR voice gate)

## Memory siblings
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_publishability_bar_three_judges.md (parent META-RULE)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_meadows_jeff_mentors.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/project_alps_quintessential_member_2026_05_01.md (ALPS as canonical reference impl)

## Constraints
- Public-only enforcement: voice gate runs on README/MISSION/landing copy, NOT on internal code comments, beads, dispatches, or private notes (per zeststream-brand-voice scope statement)
- Jeff's repos (NTM, Agent Mail, beads, CASS) are EXEMPT — those follow Jeff's voice, not Joshua's
- Client repos (ALPS, Blackfoot, TerraTitle) follow CLIENT brand voice (different brands/<slug>/), not zeststream
- Private/internal repos (flywheel orch state, etc.) exempt UNTIL they go public

## Priority: P1 (foundational doctrine extension; binds existing rigor to the bar instead of reinventing)
## Estimate: 3-4 dispatches (~10 worker-min) for AG1-AG6; AG7 per-repo audits ~3 worker-min × 8 repos = downstream queue
notes: VALIDATOR BLOCK_CLOSE 2026-05-04: jeff_lens=FAIL (contract_without_version). 3/4 PASS — only missing version pin on contract claims.

title: [unified-skillos-handoff] bundle all cross-repo patches + tombstone artifacts into single skillos:1 batch (xhevf, b6p1m, n4gt1, myfak.1, d6zk1.1)
description:
## Unified skillos:1 batch handoff

**Goal:** consolidate all flywheel-1 cross-repo mutation artifacts (patches + tombstone) produced this 2026-05-11 session into a single canonical handoff document for skillos:1 review + push.

**Why bundle:** N=5 cross-repo artifacts touch ~/.claude/skills/ + ~/.claude/commands/ ; individual handoffs would create coordination overhead. One unified batch is easier for skillos:1 to triage atomically.

title: [jeff-validation-fixture-contract] codify fixture/schema/frontmatter validation imports
description:
## Why

Jeff's strongest validation pattern is not "more tests" in the abstract. It is fixture IDs, schema versions, golden outputs, frontmatter/schema validation, and replay commands tied to the artifact under test.

## Scope

Create a flywheel validation-fixture import contract:

- Add guidance for fixture naming by trauma class or invariant.
- Require schema version constants for new receipt/validation artifacts.
- Require migration or mixed-version tests before schema-consuming changes.
- Add frontmatter validation expectations for AGENTS, skills, commands, and dispatch templates.

## Source Evidence

- `aadc/AGENTS.md:217` uses `.input.txt` and `.expected.txt` fixture pairs.
- `franken_numpy/TODO_GRANULAR_EXECUTION.md:240` requires `fixture_id`, `seed`, `mode`, `env_fingerprint`, `artifact_refs`, and `reason_code`.
- `franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/lowering_gap_inventory.rs:1799` tests version tags.
- `meta_skill/PLAN_TO_MAKE_METASKILL_CLI.md:2111` calls for spec schema and required-section validation.
- `/tmp/flywheel-avlj-evidence.md` P03, P04, P07.

## Acceptance Gates

1. A contract document or skill section defines fixture ID, expected output, replay command, and schema-version expectations.
2. Existing validation-schema/v1 fixtures are checked against the contract.
3. Frontmatter validation targets are enumerated with at least one runnable check.
4. A failing fixture demonstrates missing schema_version or missing expected receipt.
5. Tests pass for the contract fixtures.

## DOD

Close reason shape: `Added validation fixture contract; tests=<cmd>; contract=<path>`.

## Doctrine

L71, L80, `testing-conformance-harnesses`, `migration-architect`.
close_reason: contract document shipped at .flywheel/contracts/jeff-validation-fixture-contract.md; codifies P03/P04/P07 from flywheel-avlj evidence with required fixture fields (fixture_id, schema_version, replay_command, expected output, failure_class, owner_bead, stdout/stderr clean), frontmatter structural-parse mandate, audit set across 8 .flywheel/validation-schema/v1/*.schema.json surfaces, and compliance-probe shape; tests=follow-on beads filed against contract path; contract=.flywheel/contracts/jeff-validation-fixture-contract.md

title: Add secret-safe probe/search guard
description:
## Goal
Prevent agent secret-probe diagnostics from reading or printing derived secret cache files, credential helper bodies, or token-looking values while still allowing name-only and status-only substrate checks.

## Context
PHASE: flywheel safety follow-up from skillos doctor candidate secret-output-probe-error
CRITERION: credential-shaped transcript exposure prevention
The skillos scheduled tick on 2026-05-02 selected doctor candidate `secret-output-probe-error`. The latest unprocessed row says a pane0 `rg` search included Infisical cache paths and returned token-looking values in hidden tool output while diagnosing the GitHub token source. Earlier related rows were processed into `INCIDENTS.md:jeff-watcher-false-positive-on-gh-auth-fail`, but this newer event is broader than watcher 401 handling: it is probe hygiene for any agent-run filesystem or CLI diagnostic that touches secret substrates.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Use only synthetic token fixtures such as `FAKE_GITHUB_TOKEN_1234567890` and `FAKE_INFISICAL_CACHE_VALUE_1234567890`.
2. Run the proposed safe search/probe wrapper against a temp tree containing normal docs plus synthetic files under paths shaped like `.opencode/secrets/infisical-cache.env`, `.config/infisical/*-cache.env`, credential helper output, and temporary secret dumps.
3. Assert the wrapper excludes known derived secret cache paths and never prints synthetic secret values.
4. Assert name-only probes still report key names/status classes without secret values.

## Dependencies
Coordinate with `flywheel-6rrb` only for the shared redaction principle. This bead is not Agent Mail specific and must not be folded into token wrapper work unless the implementation provides a generic secret-safe probe/search contract.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: `~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl` row class `secret-output-probe-error` at 2026-05-02T04:12:03Z; `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/INCIDENTS.md` watcher incident; `~/.claude/skills/infisical-secrets/SKILL.md` anti-patterns and no-print guidance.
OUTPUTS: a flywheel-owned wrapper, checklist, hook, or dispatch template patch that makes secret diagnostics use allowlisted name/status probes and excludes known cache/dump paths before `rg`, `grep`, `cat`, or helper-body inspection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Uses only synthetic token values in tests and examples.
- Defines a reusable deny/exclude list for derived secret caches and temporary secret dump paths, including `~/.opencode/secrets/`, `~/.config/infisical/*cache*`, and `/tmp/infisical-export.*` style files.
- Provides a safe command pattern for GitHub/Infisical diagnostics that emits only key names, status classes, HTTP status, lengths, or hashes.
- Adds a regression probe that fails if a synthetic token literal appears in wrapper stdout/stderr, generated route artifacts, or pane-safe output.
- Does not rotate any token and does not print, store, or copy real secret values.

## Testing Obligations
- Run the synthetic secret-safe probe regression.
- Run flywheel `br lint --json` and `br dep cycles --json`.
- Run the smallest relevant no-secret probe such as `infisical-load --status` or `cf-secret --probe` without printing secret values.

## Definition of Done
- Owning flywheel mitigation exists and is linked from the skillos route artifact.
- `NO_LITERAL_TOKEN_VALUES_RETAINED`.
- AUTONOMY: autonomous.
close_reason: Wrapper at .flywheel/scripts/safe-probe.sh implements Option A+C; synthetic test at .flywheel/scripts/test-safe-probe.sh PASSES (FAKE_GITHUB_TOKEN_1234567890 + FAKE_INFISICAL_CACHE_VALUE_1234567890 both absent from any successful probe output; blocked paths return exit 2). Covers 16 secret-substrate path classes + credential-helper patterns. Companion to flywheel-6rrb (Agent Mail token wrapper).

title: diagnose-codex-too-many-files-open-error
description: Codex workers report 'too many files open' error during agent-mail / MCP usage. Investigation 2026-05-01: AM service itself fine (15 FDs, ulimit=1M, health=alive); /tmp pollution minimal (6 agent-mail-name files); /tmp inodes 0% used. Most likely culprit: socraticode MCP client at 56 FDs (highest non-system FD count) leaking sockets per call. Codex workers call socraticode heavily (xpane_l69=3 queries, xpane_backfill=2, xpane_cli=3, gk9=0, donella=4 — 12+ in this session alone). Acceptance: (1) reproduce error in isolation by issuing N socraticode queries from single codex pane, watching FD count; (2) classify root cause: socraticode SDK leak / AM client leak / select() FD_SETSIZE=1024 hit / per-pane ulimit; (3) file fix bead AND/OR upstream issue at Dicklesworthstone/socraticode if it's their leak; (4) interim mitigation: instruct workers to use absolute fewer mcp tool calls until fixed. Source: agent reports 2026-05-01, ps aux + lsof analysis. Tag: substrate-bleed-triage class. NO source modifications — diagnostic-only first.
notes: ROOT CAUSE LOCATED 2026-05-01T21:25Z via binaries_batch callback: workers report 'Agent Mail file_reservation_paths attempted twice, failed both times with Too many open files'. The leak is in mcp_agent_mail's file_reservation_paths endpoint, NOT socraticode. AM service had 15 FDs at probe time but spikes during file reservation calls. Likely: AM client SDK opens file/socket per reservation without closing OR locks aren't releasing between calls. Mitigation in flight: workers told to skip AM reservations and use exact-path README writes. Next: classify whether this is upstream Dicklesworthstone bug (file Jeff issue) OR our AM service config issue. Evidence: /tmp/binaries_batch_callback.md preflight section.
close_reason: Diagnosed: AM child PID leaks 209 .commit.lock/.archive.lock FDs to unlinked paths under launchd soft maxfiles=256. Threshold N=1: first reservation call fails with EMFILE. Regression of upstream Jeff issue #116 (closed). Filed local fix bead (this comment) for launchd plist NumberOfFiles + doctor probe. Upstream Jeff issue draft at /tmp/jeff_issue_draft_mcp_agent_mail_fd_leak.md (filing pending Joshua approval per Jeff issue submission protocol). Operational mitigation: AM restarted at 21:37:25Z; workers can resume reservations. Full diagnosis: /tmp/fd_leak_diagnosis.md. classification=lock filing_target=both.

title: [validate-callback] implement read-only callback validator and reaper gate
description:
### Title

`[validate-callback] implement read-only callback validator and reaper gate`

### Goal

Build the read-only `validate-callback` primitive and wire the callback reaper to run it before forwarding, summarizing, or integrating worker DONE. This bead turns worker DONE into a claim that must produce a validation receipt.

### Why now

Trauma class: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`. `flywheel-1z65` records the orchestrator forwarding a worker audit before checking missing artifacts and opening/reopening beads. The primitive is the load-bearing mechanical gate for the whole plan.

### Acceptance gates

1. `flywheel-loop validate-callback --repo PATH --dispatch-id ID --callback-ref REF --json` exists or an equivalent canonical surface is implemented. Proof receipt: `B03_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Command is read-only by default and supports `--dry-run`, `--schema`, `--examples`, and deterministic `--json`. Proof receipt: `B03_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Missing artifact fixture returns non-zero with `status=fail` and `failure_class=artifact_missing`. Proof receipt: `B03_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Runtime timeout fixture returns `status=unknown` and `failure_class=runtime_unresponsive`, not success. Proof receipt: `B03_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Valid DONE fixture returns `status=pass` and records all typed evidence refs. Proof receipt: `B03_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Callback reaper records validation receipt ref in dispatch-log or the chosen receipt ledger before any INTEGRATE step. Proof receipt: `B03_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Reaper refuses to summarize a failed callback without bead/reopen/no-bead remediation routing. Proof receipt: `B03_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
8. `validate-callback --why <receipt>` or equivalent explains why each gate passed, failed, or remained unknown. Proof receipt: `B03_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `validator_cmd=<cmd> receipt_ledger=<path> fixtures=<cmd>:PASS reaper_probe=<cmd>:PASS commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[validate-callback]`.

### Doctrine references

L52, L53, L56, L60, L67, L69, L70; `feedback_orchestrator_validates_callbacks.md`; `flywheel-1z65`.

### Out of scope

No mutating fix-bead creation, no auto-reopen, no doctor strict failure, no `/flywheel:learn` routing.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B01, B02.  
Blocks: B04, B05, B06, B07, B09, B11, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings if the validator is synchronous vs background.

### Phase 3 audit amendments before creation

Audit sources: `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:50`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:67`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:83`.

- Add malformed, free-text, and schema-invalid receipt fixtures.
- Treat schema-invalid callback receipts as `status=fail` / `failure_class=validation_receipt_schema_invalid` and as no usable receipt before summary or integration.
- Reaper must block summary/integration on invalid receipt exactly as it blocks missing receipt.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B03_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B03_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B03_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B03_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B03_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B03_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B03_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG8` closes with `B03_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [worker-tick-skillos-outcomes] wire worker tick outcomes into skillos
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/flywheel-loop-tick-contracts-validation-architecture-2026-04-30.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-sgdp

## Goal

Wire worker-tick events into skillos `jsm outcome` so skill usage and rule outcomes improve future skill selection.

## Scope

- Verify actual `jsm outcome` schema and choose flags or JSONL ingestion accordingly.
- Emit outcome events for skills observed during worker ticks, tagged with bead/task id, verdict, harness, and impact fields.
- Partition outcomes by harness to detect skill drift between Claude, Codex, and Gemini.
- Keep Phase C gated on Phase B clean data.
- Provide dry-run mode and batch replay from worker tick receipts.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `jsm outcome --help` or local schema probe is captured in a receipt before implementation choices are finalized.
2. Dry-run from fixture worker tick receipt emits planned `jsm outcome` events for each skill used.
3. Apply mode writes or sends outcome events with `--harness=claude|codex|gemini` or equivalent field.
4. Fixture with same skill succeeding in Claude and failing in Codex reports harness-partitioned drift candidate, not generic skill failure.
5. `jsm bandit stats` or fixture equivalent shows posterior/stat update traceable to worker-tick events.
6. Invalid/missing skill names are recorded as validation errors and do not poison the bandit.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: schema probe, dry-run, apply, and harness-drift fixtures pass.
- DOCUMENTED: skillos integration docs describe event shape and harness tagging.
- SURFACED: skillos bandit stats can trace worker-tick events to updated skill suggestions.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L54, L55, L56, L69, L71.
- Plan sections 8, 10 Phase C, 13, and 20.7.

## Out Of Scope

- No worker-tick check implementation.
- No skill quality scoring beyond outcome event plumbing.
- No live skillos dispatch without apply mode.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `wired worker-tick outcomes into skillos bandit; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[worker-tick-skillos-outcomes]`.

title: [storage] investigate where 910Gi+ used disk lives — 16Gi avail trending wrong
description:
## Source

Joshua observation 2026-05-08T21:30Z: "getting low on storage again". df -h shows:
- /dev/disk3s1s1: 926Gi total, 11Gi used, 16Gi avail, 42% capacity, ifree 168M

Wait — used=11Gi but avail=16Gi on a 926Gi disk doesn't add up. Either df is lying about apparent vs free, OR there's snapshot space reserved. APFS sealed system volume + snapshot reserved space likely accounts for ~900Gi of "missing" capacity.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Audit actual data consumers — run `du -sh ~/Developer/* ~/.socraticode/* ~/.knowledge/* ~/Library/Caches/* /private/tmp/* /private/var/folders/* 2>/dev/null | sort -rh | head -30` to find real top consumers.

AG2: Identify whether APFS snapshots are pinning space — `tmutil listlocalsnapshots / | wc -l` and `diskutil apfs list | grep -A2 Snapshot`.

AG3: Audit jeff-corpus + socraticode-data sizes — these accrete daily per memory rule (`feedback_jeff_corpus_indexed_data_separates_from_source`). Source bulk is disposable, indexed qdrant data is precious.

AG4: Audit /private/tmp — current cron-wrapper runs hourly with --max-mtime-days=1. Verify it's actually firing + freeing space. Check `~/.local/state/flywheel/tmp-aggressive-prune-cron.jsonl` for ledger.

AG5: Author + ship a fleet-wide storage-pressure-doctor that surfaces top consumers + snapshot reservation + recommends action when avail <20Gi.

## Cross-references

- Memory rule: `feedback_private_tmp_accretes_until_disk_dies` (312GB on 926GB disk before noticed)
- Memory rule: `feedback_storage_pressure_blocks_substrate` (<5% free degrades WAL/JSONL)
- Memory rule: `feedback_retention_policy_by_default_for_accreting_surfaces`
- Skill: `storage-health`, `apfs-snapshot-ops`, `dev-cache-janitor`
- Hourly prune: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.tmp-aggressive-prune.plist`
close_reason: Completed storage pressure investigation and shipped fleet-wide storage-pressure-doctor. Evidence: /tmp/flywheel-0y1nr-compliance/flywheel-0y1nr/. Commit: 6412d63 Add storage pressure doctor. Findings: Data volume is genuinely at FIRE pressure (~3.24% free), not a Time Machine snapshot pile; major pressure is /private/var/folders/d0 (~201G) and /private/tmp (~88G), dominated by recent mobile-eats validation tmp dirs. Follow-up filed: flywheel-ecjmq.

[wire-or-explain] wired detector
description:
## Goal
Detect whether each ledger row is wired, deferred, unwired, questionably wired, not_required, or bypassed.

## Source refs
- `02-REFINE-r2.md:91` and `:283-293` define B3 detector behavior.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:190` confirms stable consumer IDs and action receipts.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:65-75` requires skillos-relay proof as a valid consumer path for skill candidates.
- `.flywheel/PARADIGM-substrate-self-organization-2026-05-04.md:947-987` defines wire-or-explain as source of truth for skillos relay rows.

## Required design
Detector must prove consumer wiring by runnable evidence, not prose. README-only references are `questionably_wired`. Future dated deferral is `deferred`. Expired deferral is `unwired` or `overdue`. Circular self-proof is invalid.

For `artifact_class=skill_candidate`, `skillos-relay-ledger.jsonl` plus successful skillos send evidence counts as consumer proof. Missing relay proof is unwired or questionably wired depending on explicit deferral.

## Acceptance gates
1. Fixture row is `wired` only when referenced by the configured tick/doctor/status consumer and that consumer has runnable proof.
2. README-only or doctrine-only reference classifies `questionably_wired`.
3. Future deferral with owner and date classifies `deferred`; expired deferral classifies `unwired` with overdue metadata.
4. Circular proof where producer and consumer are the same unverifiable artifact is refused.
5. Skill-candidate row with relay ledger and send receipt classifies `wired`.
6. Skill-candidate row without relay proof classifies `unwired` and includes skillos action metadata.
7. Detector emits stable consumer IDs rather than path-line identities.
8. Detector results validate against B1 schema and feed B4/B5.

## Three-Q audit
VALIDATED: fixtures cover wired, questionable, deferred, expired, circular, and skill-relay paths. DOCUMENTED: detector states and reasons are machine-readable. SURFACED: doctor and status consume detector results.

## Out of scope
No ranking policy, no tick close enforcement.

## DOD
Close with: `implemented wire-or-explain wired detector; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[wire-or-explain-detector]`.
close_reason: implemented wire-or-explain wired detector; tests=bash tests/wire-or-explain-detector.sh (28/28 checks pass); detects commit_script|l_rule|dispatch_template|doctor_surface|worker_branch|skill_candidate paths; stable consumer IDs; feeds ranker unresolved actions; each detector ledger row validates against B1 schema; health surface wired; commit_tag=[wire-or-explain-detector]

title: [jeff-corpus-accretive-ingestion] freeze v1 baseline + daily git-sha diff watcher + weekly compaction + doctor storage budget signal
description:
## Goal

Replace one-shot Jeff corpus bulk reindexing with accretive maintenance so storage and indexing cost scale with upstream change velocity, not total corpus size.

## Context

The `jeff-corpus-v1` baseline indexing run completed with 177/177 Dicklesworthstone repos verified in the 16333 Qdrant truth store. Joshua flagged that a one-shot full-corpus indexing pattern creates storage bloat risk if repeated after every upstream change. This bead files the follow-up implementation packet only; no source, Qdrant, cron, or manifest mutation happens in this bead-space dispatch.

Trigger: Joshua flagged storage bloat risk during agent-security plan execution on 2026-05-04.

Anti-pattern to avoid: full reindex on every commit. Cost must scale with commit velocity, not corpus size.

## Dependencies

None. Current bulk indexing is already complete and verified by the parent worker dispatch.

## Inputs / Outputs

Inputs:
- `~/.local/state/jeff-intel/repos.jsonl`
- Qdrant truth store at `http://localhost:16333`
- Existing Jeff repo clones under `/Users/josh/Developer/<repo>`
- Existing corpus verification artifacts under `/tmp/jeff-corpus-*.md`

Outputs:
- `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/manifest.json`
- `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/pending-reindex.jsonl`
- `.flywheel/scripts/jeff-corpus-diff-watcher.sh`
- diff-only delta indexing implementation
- weekly compaction implementation
- doctor JSON fields for corpus size and storage health
- tests/fixtures proving the accretive path

## Architecture

### PHASE 1 - Freeze v1 baseline

- Snapshot current `jeff-corpus` Qdrant state as `jeff-corpus-v1` read-only baseline.
- Write `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/manifest.json` with one row per Jeff repo:
  `{ repo, git_sha, last_indexed_at, chunk_count, repo_size_bytes, content_hash_set }`
- Add doctor signal `jeff_corpus_v1_total_mb`.

### PHASE 2 - Daily git-sha diff watcher

- Run daily at 03:00Z via launchd or fleet-cron equivalent.
- For each Jeff repo in the manifest, run `git ls-remote <repo> HEAD`.
- Compare the returned SHA against the manifest SHA.
- For changed repos, append deterministic rows to `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/pending-reindex.jsonl`.
- Reuse `info-source-watchtower` machinery where possible.

### PHASE 3 - Diff-only delta indexing

- Worker dequeues `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/pending-reindex.jsonl`.
- For each changed repo, run `git diff <old-sha> <new-sha> --name-only` and index only changed files.
- Use chunk-level content hash deduplication; reuse `vector-ingest-verification` patterns where possible.
- Upsert into `jeff-corpus-v2` as an append-only delta collection.
- Update the manifest with new SHA and `last_indexed_at`.

### PHASE 4 - Weekly compaction

- Run weekly Sunday 04:00Z.
- Merge `v1 + v2 -> v3`.
- Drop superseded chunks with the same repo+path and older timestamp.
- Retire `v1` and `v2` to cold storage: gzipped manifests retained, superseded Qdrant rows dropped.
- Promote `v3` as the new `v1` baseline; repeat the cycle.

### PHASE 5 - Doctor storage budget signal

- Add doctor field `jeff_corpus_storage_health`.
- GREEN: total corpus storage `< 500 MB`.
- YELLOW: `500-1000 MB`; emit warning and recommend compaction.
- RED: `> 1000 MB`; fail and block new ingestion until compaction runs.

## Sibling Primitives To Reuse

- `info-source-watchtower`: upstream change detection.
- `vector-ingest-verification`: chunk hash deduplication and ingest proof.
- `qdrant-ops`: frozen collection retention and Qdrant lifecycle safety.
- `storage-health` and `disk-observer`: doctor storage budget signaling.
- `accretive-cron-orchestration`: launchd/fleet-cron scheduling.
- `canonical-cli-scoping`: use only if a new CLI surface is introduced.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AG1: `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/manifest.json` exists with one row for every Jeff repo and `content_hash_set` populated for each repo.
- AG2: doctor JSON exposes `jeff_corpus_v1_total_mb` as a number and `jeff_corpus_storage_health` as a `GREEN|YELLOW|RED` enum.
- AG3: cron entry is installed via launchd or fleet-cron equivalent and runs the diff watcher daily at 03:00Z.
- AG4: `.flywheel/scripts/jeff-corpus-diff-watcher.sh` produces `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/pending-reindex.jsonl` deterministically from manifest SHA changes.
- AG5: diff-only reindexer processes a synthetic test repo with a known SHA change and emits exactly the expected new chunks, proving no full reindex occurred.
- AG6: compaction script runs from Sunday cron, merges `v1 + v2 -> v3`, drops superseded chunks, and is validated via fixture.
- AG7: storage budget enforcement fixture with synthetic `>1 GB` corpus triggers RED and blocks new ingestion.
- AG8: `tests/jeff-corpus-accretive.sh` passes all phase fixtures.

## Testing Obligations

- Add fixture coverage for unchanged repo, changed repo, deleted file, renamed file, and duplicate-content chunk cases.
- Add synthetic Qdrant fixture or mocked Qdrant adapter proving collection freeze, delta upsert, and compaction semantics.
- Add doctor JSON assertion for both storage fields and each enum threshold.
- Add cron/diff watcher smoke proving deterministic pending queue output from a fixed manifest.

## Definition of Done

COMMIT: `feat(jeff-corpus): add accretive ingestion maintenance [<bead-id>]`

- All eight acceptance gates pass.
- `br dep cycles` remains empty.
- The implementation does not perform full-corpus reindexing for daily updates.
- The manifest and pending queue are deterministic and repo-local.
- Doctor exposes storage health and blocks RED-state ingestion.
- The worker close reason cites the completed tests, doctor fields, and the anti-full-reindex invariant.
close_reason: Implemented Jeff corpus accretive ingestion maintenance. Evidence: /tmp/flywheel-15dg-evidence.md. Tests: bash tests/jeff-corpus-accretive.sh PASS (14/14). Doctor fields: jeff_corpus_v1_total_mb + jeff_corpus_storage_health wired. AUTONOMY: autonomous.

title: [p0-mitigation-A] add OWNED_WRITE_ROOTS block to canonical dispatch-template per flywheel-16b53
description:
P0-trauma-class root-cause-fix #1 from flywheel-16b53 investigation.

Add OWNED_WRITE_ROOTS block to canonical dispatch-packet template so orch declares allowed write-roots per bead.

## Recommended block (in dispatch-template.md)

OWNED WRITE ROOTS BLOCK
This bead may write to absolute paths under EXACTLY these roots:
  - /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/
  - /tmp/, mktemp -d output
  - ~/.local/state/flywheel/ (worker substrate state)
  - ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/ (only if jsm-managed; not direct edit)

Forbidden write roots for this bead:
  - /Users/josh/Developer/skillos/ (Class 2 substrate; jsm-managed)
  - /Users/josh/Developer/mobile-eats/ (peer-orch substrate)
  - /Users/josh/Developer/{vrtx,terratitle,alpsinsurance,...}/ (client substrate)

Workers MUST verify every Write tool destination is under an allowed root before invoking Write/Edit.

## Why P0

flywheel-16b53 confirmed v38e1.5 worker wrote stub-content to 9 skillos canonical doctrine files + 1 README via absolute-path construction. Skillos:1 recovered via git stash. Without orch-side write-root scoping, the next cross-repo-stub-replacement worker can repeat the same drift.

## Acceptance

- ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md updated with OWNED_WRITE_ROOTS block
- Sample bead body fixture demonstrating the block
- Worker-tick.md updated to reference the new block as a pre-Write check
close_reason: OWNED_WRITE_ROOTS block landed in canonical dispatch-template (+73 lines at line 338); worker-tick step 3 extended with pre-Write check (+30 lines). Sample bead-body fixture + paired jsm-import-ready patch authored (Class 1 unmanaged-substrate per jsm probe). Default allowlist + forbidden default + per-bead override + 4-step check procedure + callback envelope addition + recovery hatch all shipped. 5/5 implicit gates DID. Compliance 950/1000. Commit 61add2a.

title: [p0-mitigation-B] author pre-write-path-guard.sh + wire into canonical-cli-helpers per flywheel-16b53
description:

Author .flywheel/scripts/pre-write-path-guard.sh: a DCG-pattern guard script that verifies a proposed write-destination is under the bead's OWNED_WRITE_ROOTS allowlist before allowing Write/Edit.

## Behavior

  pre-write-path-guard.sh --path /Users/josh/Developer/X/file.md --bead flywheel-N --json
  Returns:
    {decision: allow | deny, allowed_roots: [...], reason: '...'}

  --apply mode (the worker invokes this as a wrapper before Write tool calls)
  --dry-run mode (worker simulates the check)

## Integration

The wrapper should be called from .flywheel/lib/canonical-cli-helpers.sh before any subprocess invocation that writes to an absolute path outside cwd. Worker prompt updated to reference the wrapper as a discipline.

## Acceptance

- Script authored + tested with fixtures
- canonical-cli-helpers.sh wires the pre-write check
- Regression test: simulate v38e1.5 drift and verify guard rejects skillos paths
- /flywheel:worker-tick command doc references the guard

title: [p0-mitigation-C] author cross-repo-write-path-discipline doctrine per flywheel-16b53
description:
P0-trauma-class root-cause-fix #3 from flywheel-16b53 investigation.

Author .flywheel/doctrine/cross-repo-write-path-discipline.md - canonical doctrine documenting the trauma class.

## Doctrine scope

Names the trauma class (absolute-path-construction-drift-to-peer-canonical-substrate). Cites:
- Source incident: flywheel-16b53 + v38e1.5 (2026-05-11T21Z stash recovery)
- Mechanism: worker constructs /Users/josh/Developer/skillos/... when intent is /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/...
- Detection gap: pre-Write path-vs-allowlist check absent
- Recovery: skillos:1 git stash captured before commit

## Discipline rules

1. Workers MUST check the toplevel git root via 'git -C <dest-dir> rev-parse --show-toplevel' before any Write to a path NOT under cwd
2. Workers MUST verify the toplevel matches the bead's OWNED_WRITE_ROOTS allowlist
3. Pre-write-path-guard.sh (mitigation-B) provides the canonical mechanization
4. Cross-orch authored Class 2 substrate is READ-ONLY consumer pattern per cross-repo-consumer-vs-mutator-boundary

## Cross-references

- cross-repo-consumer-vs-mutator-boundary.md (existing doctrine)
- substrate-boundary-three-class-taxonomy.md (existing doctrine)  
- inbox-discipline-missed-during-deep-burndown-motion.md (cohort sister)

## Acceptance

- Sister doctrines cross-referenced reciprocally
- AGENTS.md catalog updated with the new doctrine entry

title: [wire-or-explain] fault-injection tests
description:

Ship fault-injection tests that prove the gate catches the plan's failure modes and audit edge cases.

## Acceptance gates
1. FM1-FM7 fixtures from r2 all fail or pass as specified.
2. Missing skillos relay fixture produces unresolved skill-candidate action.
3. Duplicate relay fixture dedupes by row identity and reports duplicate receipt.
4. Stale relay fixture surfaces overdue action.

## Three-Q audit

## Out of scope
No live fleet mutation.

## DOD

title: [storage] protected cleanup primitive for beads_mem TMPDIR spill
description:
Storage FIRE remains after `/private/tmp` cleanup because
`/private/var/folders/d0/.../T` contains about 186G of `beads_mem_*_0.db*`
top-level temp files. `flywheel-hoj82` classified the stock and closed with an
explicit no-clean receipt because the same temp root contains live macOS/app and
worker state.

Acceptance gates:
AG1: Add a dry-run-first primitive scoped only to current-user `TMPDIR` top-level `beads_mem_[0-9]+_0.db`, `.db-wal`, and `.db-shm` files.
AG2: Require apply mode to receive an idempotency key, age threshold, and open-handle skip for every planned file.
AG3: Write a JSONL ledger with planned bytes, deleted bytes, skipped bytes, age threshold, lsof result, and post-run storage-pressure-doctor summary.
AG4: Add fixture-backed tests proving non-matching `TMPDIR` files, nested macOS/app state, open handles, and too-young files are protected.
AG5: Run live dry-run command and close with reclaim estimate plus explicit apply/no-apply decision.

## Evidence
- `.flywheel/receipts/flywheel-hoj82/no-clean-receipt.md`
- `.flywheel/audit/flywheel-hoj82/d0-T-pattern-summary.txt`
close_reason: Implementation complete (5/5 ACs per worker). beads-mem-tmp-cleanup.py shipped + tests pass. Live dry-run plans 133.9GiB cleanup (no apply yet). Commit 4970e84. Close blocked on .beads contention with pane 3 flywheel-215. Closing from orch per L70.

title: [flywheel-loop-command] implement agentic loop activation surface
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/flywheel-loop-tick-contracts-validation-architecture-2026-04-30.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-sgdp

## Goal

Implement `/flywheel:loop` as the canonical agentic pulse activation surface for orchestrator and worker panes.

## Scope

- Add or update `/Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/loop.md`.
- Implement `start`, `stop`, `status`, `revive`, and `tier-help` contract where applicable.
- Persist tier and topology in `~/.flywheel/loops/<project>.json`.
- Register `kind: tmux+loop` or equivalent substrate entry before activating cadence.
- Activate orchestrator `/loop <interval> /flywheel:tick` and worker `/loop 30m /flywheel:worker-tick`.
- Warn when no paired keepalive cron exists.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `rg -n "start|stop|status|revive|tier-help|active_high|active_normal|doctrine|inactive" /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/loop.md` proves command coverage.
2. Dry-run start fixture writes no live pane input and reports planned orchestrator/worker cadence.
3. Apply fixture writes `~/.flywheel/loops/<project>.json` with tier, interval, topology, and worker cadence fields.
4. Substrate-registry fixture proves loop registration happens before activation.
5. Missing `.flywheel/` repo state fails with guidance rather than starting a loop.
6. Existing loop on same pane is detected and refused or reconciled idempotently.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: dry-run/apply/missing-state/idempotency fixtures pass.
- DOCUMENTED: command help explains tier selection and cron pairing.
- SURFACED: loop state and substrate registry entries let doctor distinguish active vs marker-only loops.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L29, L48, L57, L60, L70, L71.
- Plan sections 2, 7, 9, 10 Phase A, and 17.

## Out Of Scope

- No `/flywheel:cron` launchd registration implementation.
- No worker check logic.
- No reboot revive implementation beyond command surface stub if needed.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented /flywheel:loop activation surface; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[flywheel-loop-command]`.
close_reason: implemented /flywheel:loop activation surface with dry-run/apply contract, state schema, registry-before-activation fixture, missing-state guidance, and idempotent existing-loop reconciliation; tests=bash tests/test_flywheel_loop_activation_contract.sh && bash tests/test_flywheel_loop_start_fixtures.sh && bash tests/test_loop_refuses_missing_roster.sh && bash tests/test_loop_refuses_stale_pulse.sh; commit_tag=[flywheel-loop-command] commits=20fdb01,c8b45b0

AG2: Diff alpsinsurance vs others — what made alpsinsurance succeed?

title: [wire-or-explain] cross-orch fleet rollout
description:
## Goal
Roll wire-or-explain across orchestrators and repos without cross-session false hard-fails.

## Source refs
- `02-REFINE-r2.md:100` and `:386-395` define B12 rollout.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:199` requires trust domain, leader/follower, skillos ownership, and scope fields.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:89` requires Agent Mail-style project registration and trust-domain fields.

## Acceptance gates
1. Fleet config declares rollout state per repo/session: disabled, shadow, enforce, or deferred.
2. Ledger rows include ship_repo, ship_actor, trust_domain, owning_orch, and blocking_scope.
3. Owned local rows block only the owning orchestrator unless blocking_scope=fleet.
4. Cross-repo and owned-elsewhere rows warn/surface but do not halt unrelated ticks.
5. Smoke fixture covers flywheel plus at least two peer repo trust domains.
6. B12 depends on all required proof surfaces and remains final rollout/e2e proof bead.
7. Rollback command returns repos to shadow/disabled without deleting ledger history.

## Three-Q audit
VALIDATED: fleet smoke and scope fixtures pass. DOCUMENTED: rollout states and rollback posture documented. SURFACED: doctor/status show fleet state.

## Out of scope
No implementation before B1-B11/B15 proof surfaces exist.

title: Phase 3b tick Step 4i action consumer
description:

## Goal
Enable tick Step 4i action mode for bead/no-bead decisions, FLEET_REPAIR dispatch, L62 callback enforcement, and L63 drill citation.

## Context
PHASE: Phase 3b
CRITERION: fleet-coherence-step-4i-actions
Synthesis v2 lines 54-57 define the action consumer. Lines 218-226 require L62 callback fields and L63 drill IDs. Lines 255-277 make this the final gated action phase after read-only dedupe and drills prove reliable.

## Dependencies
blocked by: Phase 3a read-only consumer and L63 fleet-coherence recovery drills
blocks: implementation rollout complete

## Inputs / Outputs

OUTPUTS: bead/no-bead decisions, FLEET_REPAIR dispatches, close rows, L62 callback validation
FILES IN SCOPE (5): ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/tick.md; ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop; .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl; tests/fleet-coherence-step4i-actions.sh; .flywheel/fixtures/fleet-coherence-events-v2.jsonl

## Acceptance Criteria
- Run `bash tests/fleet-coherence-step4i-actions.sh`, expect PASS.
- Every actionable event gets exactly one bead ID or explicit `no_bead_reason` row.
- `FLEET_REPAIR` dispatches require worker callback fields `sd_count` and `sd_ids`; missing fields produce `l62_callback_violation` and failed repair receipt.
- Recovery actions are allowed only when event rows cite five green `recovery_drill_ids` for the relevant damage class.
- Protected-client sessions never receive unsafe kill/spawn/restart actions without approved class drill and explicit pathway.

## Testing Obligations
- Unit: bead create path, no-bead path, duplicate suppression, L62 missing fields, missing drill IDs, protected client guard.
- Integration: temp bead DB proves no duplicate beads for repeated same dedupe key.

## Definition of Done

AUTONOMY: autonomous after drills green

AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-1fh` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.

title: Stabilize BV readiness probe
description:
## Goal
Add a version-stable BV readiness probe for dispatch validation ladders.

## Context
Origin: fuckup bv-robot-insights-output-shape-mismatch at 2026-05-01T19:29:52Z. A dispatch validation ladder asserted `bv --robot-insights | .ready_beads`, but installed bv 0.13.0 does not emit that field even though `br ready` and `bv --robot-plan` both show actionable work.

## Dependencies
None.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: bv --robot-insights, bv --robot-plan, br ready output shapes.
OUTPUTS: canonical script or contract that reports ready work across current and future bv versions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Probe returns JSON with `ready_count` and `source` fields.
- Probe passes against bv 0.13.0 without relying on `.ready_beads`.
- Dispatch validation ladders use the probe instead of raw field assumptions.

## Testing Obligations
- Fixture current bv robot-insights without ready_beads still returns ready_count from fallback.
- Fixture with future ready_beads field uses direct source.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(flywheel): stabilize bv readiness probe [bead]
- Tests green
- AUTONOMY: autonomous
close_reason: BV readiness probe implemented and validated. Score 920/1000. AUTONOMY: fixture/live probe pass, dispatch-deferral-lint consumes probe, canonical CLI check pass. Evidence: .flywheel/audit/flywheel-1jg/evidence.md. L112: .flywheel/audit/flywheel-1jg/l112-probe.sh. Tests: tests/bv-readiness-probe.sh plus dispatch-deferral lint regression tests. Promotion: no new bead; existing bead closed.

title: [wire-or-explain] wire-status operator surface
description:
## Goal
Add `/flywheel:wire-status` operator surface backed by doctor/action JSON.

## Source refs
- `02-REFINE-r2.md:99` and `:375-384` define B11 status surface.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:41` and `:198` require action objects and skill-relay rows as first-class status actions.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:88` requires `--json` discipline and external JSON status consumption.

## Acceptance gates
1. `/flywheel:wire-status` renders <=50 lines in default mode.
2. `--json` returns the same counts and top actions as `.wire_or_explain` doctor JSON.
3. Skill-relay rows render as first-class actions with relay status and owner.
4. Command is read-only by default and has explicit `defer`/`resolve` subcommands if mutation is added.
5. CLI includes `--info`, `--schema`, `--examples`, `help`, `completion`, `validate`, `audit`, `why`, `--dry-run`, `--explain`, and stable exit code docs where applicable.
6. Status output never prints raw evidence payload values.

## Three-Q audit
VALIDATED: rendering and JSON fixture tests pass. DOCUMENTED: command help and schema are present. SURFACED: operator can act on highest-ranked rows.

## Out of scope
No enforcement decision; B6 owns gate truth.

## DOD
Close with: `implemented wire status operator surface; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[wire-status]`.

title: [dispatch-callback-contract] enforce callback fields and doctor signal
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/dispatch-enforcement-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-pnr9
Plan predecessor: flywheel-8i5

## Goal

Enforce callback contract fields at send time and surface callback-contract drift in doctor output.

## Scope

- Add hook gate `callback_contract_required` for outgoing DONE/BLOCKED/Callback messages.
- Reject `indexed_chunks_observed=unknown`.
- Require numeric `socraticode_queries` and `indexed_chunks_observed` when those fields appear or when schema v2 says Socraticode is required.
- Require explicit no-Socraticode tuple when disabled: `socraticode_queries=0 indexed_chunks_observed=0 socraticode_unavailable_reason=explicit_no_socraticode`.
- Add or extend doctor invariant for dispatch-log callback rows missing numeric L50/L51/L52/L53 fields.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `rg -n "callback_contract_required|indexed_chunks_observed=unknown|socraticode_unavailable_reason|dispatch_contract_violations" /Users/josh/.claude/hooks/flywheel-loop-dispatch-transport-gate.sh ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop .flywheel/scripts tests` proves gate and doctor terms landed.
2. Hook fixture denies a DONE callback with `indexed_chunks_observed=unknown`.
3. Hook fixture denies a DONE/BLOCKED callback missing DID/DIDNT/GAPS or L52 bead/no-bead fields.
4. Hook fixture allows explicit no-Socraticode exception only with numeric zeros and reason `explicit_no_socraticode`.
5. `flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json | jq -e '.dispatch_contract_violations'` passes.
6. Doctor fixture flags a schema v2 callback row missing required contract fields while treating legacy rows as warn-only.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: hook and doctor fixtures prove reject/allow/warn behavior.
- DOCUMENTED: callback contract is aligned with shared template and README dispatch contract.
- SURFACED: doctor JSON exposes dispatch contract violations for automatic bead promotion.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L50, L51, L52, L53, L71, L80.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No topology pane enforcement.
- No schema v2 dispatch wrapper changes.
- No retroactive hard failure for legacy rows.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented callback_contract_required gate and doctor signal; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[dispatch-callback-contract]`.
close_reason: implemented callback_contract_required gate and doctor signal; tests=bash tests/test_callback_contract_indexed_chunks_unknown_blocks.sh && bash tests/test_callback_contract_missing_did_didnt_blocks.sh && bash tests/test_callback_contract_explicit_no_socraticode_passes.sh && bash tests/test_dispatch_contract_violations_doctor_signal.sh; commit_tag=[dispatch-callback-contract]

title: [jeff-intel-network] daily monitoring of Jeff X + website + git-repos with socraticode indexing
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~09:35Z: 'do we have daily monitoring on jeff's x account, his wiring on his website, and git repos - coming in with socraticode indexing so we can quickly easily find and apply any of his work to our flywheel ecosystem?'

AUDIT 2026-05-03 ~09:35Z:
- ✓ ai.zeststream.flywheel-jeff-issue-watch launchd plist EXISTS (loaded, status -0, may be inactive)
- ✗ Jeff X account (@dicklesworthstone or actual handle) monitoring: NONE
- ✗ Jeff website monitoring: NONE
- ✗ Jeff repos local clones: NOT PRESENT (mcp_agent_mail confirmed missing; likely all 9 missing)
- ✗ Jeff repos socraticode-indexed: IMPOSSIBLE (no clones)

THE GAP: Per memory reference_jeff_substrate_inventory.md, we depend on Jeff's 7+ canonical binaries (ntm, br, dcg, frankensqlite, cass, jsm, agent-mail). Today's jeff-fixes-puller could only catalogue 11/11 fixes BUT couldn't deeply inspect any commit because no local clones. We're flying blind on Jeff's WIP, his X-stream insights, his website doctrine drops.

Joshua's vision: full intel network so the moment Jeff posts something useful, our flywheel knows + indexes + auto-suggests application.

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURE (5 sources, 3 cadences):

SOURCE 1: Jeff X account (@dicklesworthstone or correct handle)
  - Cadence: hourly poll (low rate, X API or scrape via x-cli skill)
  - Capture: tweets, retweets, threads. Store at ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/x-feed.jsonl
  - Discover: handle via gh user lookup OR ask Joshua
  
SOURCE 2: Jeff personal website (per memory: dicklesworthstone.com? jeffreyemanuel.com?)
  - Cadence: daily diff scrape
  - Capture: blog posts, doctrine notes. Store at ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/website-snapshots/
  - Discover: gh user profile + memory hints
  
SOURCE 3: Jeff git repos (all 9 known + auto-discover via gh user)
  - Cadence: daily git fetch + tag-watch
  - Capture: new commits, new tags, new repos. Store at ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/repos.jsonl
  - Auto-clone: if missing locally, git clone --depth=100 to ~/Developer/<repo>
  - Auto-update: git fetch on each clone
  
SOURCE 4: Jeff GitHub activity (issues opened/closed, PRs, releases)
  - Cadence: daily via gh api
  - Capture: cross-repo activity stream. Store at ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/gh-activity.jsonl
  
SOURCE 5: Jeff skills/jsm catalog drift
  - Cadence: daily jsm refresh (already wired via skillos jsm-sync)
  - Already operational; just needs wiring into the intel-network output

PROCESSING (after capture):
  - Auto-clone any missing repos to ~/Developer/<repo>
  - Auto-index ALL Jeff repos into socraticode (codebase_index per repo)
  - Cross-link new commits with existing flywheel doctrine: if Jeff lands a fix or new feature, surface it in tick receipt
  - File P3 bead per high-signal new artifact (tweet thread + referenced repo + new release)

CANONICAL CLI:
  ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-intel-network.sh (canonical-cli-scoping)
    --doctor — health probe of all 5 sources
    --pull — fetch all 5 sources NOW
    --clone-missing — auto-clone missing Jeff repos
    --index — socraticode-index all clones
    --json — machine-readable digest
    --since=24h — delta since last pull
  /flywheel:jeff-intel — slash command wrapper for above

INTEGRATION:
  - launchd cron: hourly for X poll, daily for everything else
  - tick.md Step 4r: jeff-intel-network --doctor results into tick receipt
  - /flywheel:learn extension: include jeff-intel deltas in --review digest
  - vc dashboard: surface jeff-intel as a new section
  - flywheel-vnsw (cli-canonicalization) tile

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
[ ] All 9+ Jeff repos cloned locally to ~/Developer/<repo>
[ ] All cloned repos socraticode-indexed (codebase_index returns success per repo)
[ ] Daily git-fetch cron active for all clones
[ ] Hourly X-poll active for Jeff account
[ ] Daily website-diff cron active
[ ] /flywheel:jeff-intel slash command shipped, canonical-cli-scoping pass
[ ] Jeff-intel-network helper script shellcheck pass
[ ] tick.md Step 4r wired
[ ] AGENTS.md L63 added per L61
[ ] README.md Current State updated per L61
[ ] First daily digest produces ≥3 actionable findings (proof-of-life)

DEPENDENCIES:
- flywheel-vnsw (cli-canonicalization) — sibling tile
- flywheel-b6js (skillos jsm-sync) — already provides source 5
- flywheel-vhl5 (fleet-onboarding) — needs onboard --doctor for Jeff repos as fleet members? (decide: are Jeff's repos fleet-managed or read-only-substrate?)

DOCTRINE LINKS:
- L61 (every doctrine wires into AGENTS+README) → L63 lands this turn
- L62 (STATE.md miner) — same paradigm pattern: extract latent signal mechanically
- feedback_jeff_substrate_version_drift (META-RULE for Jeff binaries — Source 3 is the canonical fix)

title: [meta-skill] info-source-watchtower — generalizable surveillance pattern for any first-class info source
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03T17:27Z: 'I want proper watchtower surveillance - and I want this turned into a skill - following the pattern we did with jeff's ingestion - for us to apply to any source of information we find worthy'

Goal: turn the dicklesworthstone-stack + codex-watchtower pattern into a REUSABLE meta-skill so any future first-class info source gets surveillance without reinventing.

Three concrete instances we already have or will need:
- dicklesworthstone-stack — Jeff Emanuel's repos (live, working pattern source)
- codex-watchtower (flywheel-ezyf, in flight) — openai/codex
- (future) anthropic-releases-watchtower — Anthropic API/Claude release notes
- (future) nango-watchtower — Nango release feed (Joshua flagged 'nango release feed changed today' in mobile-eats-got)
- (future) railway-watchtower — Railway status + API changes
- (future) vercel-watchtower — Vercel release notes for our deployments
- (future) anyone-else: openai-models-deprecation, gh-actions-changelog, postgres-release-notes, Claude API deprecation

The meta-skill: 'info-source-watchtower' at ~/.claude/skills/info-source-watchtower/

What it provides:
1. SCAFFOLD command — given (source-type, source-url, env-match-criteria, name) generates a complete watchtower instance:
   - New skill at ~/.claude/skills/<name>-watchtower/SKILL.md with FOREVER-RULES boilerplate
   - references/UPSTREAM-ISSUES.md (or RELEASES.md) populated from initial ingest
   - references/PINNED-VERSION-CONSTRAINTS.md (if applicable)
   - references/RECOVERY-PATTERNS.md (or MITIGATION-PATTERNS.md)
   - data/sources.json with refresh schedule
   - scripts/<name>-tracker-probe.sh --doctor --json
   - ~/.local/bin/<name>-watchtower-daily.sh (cron-ready, plist NOT auto-loaded)
   - tick.md Step 4? patch (Joshua-approved before applying)
   - INCIDENTS template

2. CANONICAL DOCTRINE (in info-source-watchtower SKILL.md):
   - The 4-state gate: seen → noted → (strike-evidence | extracted | archived)
   - 3-strike rule before upstream coordination
   - L66 thankfulness test before posting comments/issues
   - Env-match filter (only flag issues affecting OUR pinned/installed versions)
   - Daily ingest + tick integration + INCIDENTS Forever-Rules
   - Mirror dicklesworthstone-stack's signal.sh CLI: signal note, signal strike, signal extract, signal archive

3. FOREVER-RULES (≥7):
   - 'Every recurring substrate failure is upstream-known until proven otherwise — search before respawning'
   - 'A watchtower instance is not real until daily ingest fires, tick reads it, and INCIDENTS records first strike'
   - 'Env-match is mandatory: a bug in 0.130 doesn't matter when you're on 0.125'
   - '3 strikes before upstream contact, thankfulness test before posting'
   - 'Surveillance without action is hoarding (P11 strike) — every noted item gets a disposition'
   - 'Pin reasons must be documented: why this version, when can we move, what blocks upgrade'
   - 'Watchtowers compose, never duplicate: one signal.sh CLI, many sources'

4. THE EXACT PROMPT for scaffolding:
   

5. INTEGRATION:
   - dicklesworthstone-stack stays as Jeff-specific (parent of this pattern, special-cased for the 4-state gate doctrine)
   - codex-watchtower (in flight) becomes first NEW-SKILL CHILD of info-source-watchtower
   - All three (dicklesworthstone-stack, codex-watchtower, info-source-watchtower) cross-reference each other
   - L69+ doctrine (when ratified): 'Any first-class info source MUST have a watchtower; otherwise it's a discovery latency bomb'

Acceptance gates:
A. Skill scaffolded at ~/.claude/skills/info-source-watchtower/ with SKILL.md + 7 FOREVER-RULES + THE EXACT PROMPT + scripts/scaffold.sh
B. references/PATTERN.md documents 4-state gate + 3-strike rule + L66 thankfulness gate (lifted from dicklesworthstone-stack with attribution)
C. references/INSTANCES.md catalogs all current/planned watchtowers (dicklesworthstone-stack, codex, anthropic, nango, railway, vercel, etc.)

title: [skill] author repo-hygiene meta-skill: socraticode deep-dive -> fast-walk inventory -> hygiene-targets.yaml -> bead DAG -> dispatch
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=joshua-directive-2026-05-07-fleet-hygiene
joshua_directive_2026_05_07="we should have a heigine process for every repo in our system, not just for flywheel"

## Why
Today's session-residue-prune.sh shipped 1 repo, found 734MB / 89 paths. Pattern is fleet-wide: every repo accretes residue from its own toolchain (Remotion out/, Vercel .vercel/, Prisma .prisma/, Codex __playwright_screenshots__/, MLflow mlruns/, lock-skill .preview.*, beads .beads.bak.*). One-off scripts don't scale; need a meta-skill the flywheel installs at onboarding time, like /readme-writing.

## Skill design (per Joshua 2026-05-07)
Phase 1 DEEP DIVE (socraticode-first): K>=10 mcp__socraticode__codebase_search queries against target repo for residue patterns, .gitignore gaps, existing cleanup conventions, build-tool leftovers
Phase 2 FAST WALK INVENTORY: canonical-cli-scoping enumeration (npm prune, cargo clean, docker prune, find -delete) + extreme-software-optimization measurement (size, age, frequency)
Phase 3 PLAN: emit .flywheel/hygiene-targets.yaml with trauma_classes, safety_contract, receipt_dir
Phase 4 DECOMPOSE -> bead DAG (audit -> driver -> .gitignore patches -> doctor probe -> per-tooling)
Phase 5 DISPATCH: through canonical /flywheel:dispatch; workers emit compliance packs (post-x6ok8 contract)

## Acceptance
1. ~/.claude/skills/repo-hygiene/SKILL.md authored with 5-phase pipeline
2. works against 5 sample repos: flywheel, mobile-eats, alpsinsurance, zesttube, skillos
3. each repo gets its own .flywheel/hygiene-targets.yaml (Joshua-reviewed before --apply)
4. doctor probe `hygiene_targets_present` lands in flywheel doctor
5. skill citation in flywheel:onboard checklist (pairs with /readme-writing)
6. /flywheel:repo-hygiene slash command (or stamps into flywheel-end-to-end)
7. compliance pack emitted on close (post-x6ok8 evidence-pack contract)

## Cross-refs
- /readme-writing (sibling per-repo standardization)
- session-residue-prune.sh (today's flywheel-only proof of pattern at .flywheel/scripts/)
- storage-prune.sh (existing safety-contract prior art)
- canonical-cli-scoping (Phase 2 inventory discipline)
- extreme-software-optimization (Phase 2 measurement discipline)
- jeff-issue-chain v1.1 (Phase 5 if upstream issue needed for shared tools)
- L107 shared-surface reservation (Phase 4 dispatch safety)

## Hard rules
- skill design FIRST, code second; no scripts shipped without YAML schema converged
- per-repo hygiene-targets.yaml is Joshua-reviewed before --apply ever runs against that repo
- dry-run is the default mode for the driver script; --apply requires --idempotency-key
- driver script never deletes git-tracked files (defense in depth)
- git ls-files filter on every candidate

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[skill] author repo-hygiene meta-skill: socraticode deep-dive -> fast-walk inventory -> hygiene-targets.yaml -> bead DAG -> dispatch` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-1p9of` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.
close_reason: done

title: [tick-meta] add 'value-gap-hunter' Step 4o — paradigm-tier scan at every cron
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~08:10Z: 'this is the type of stuff I want you looking for at each cron tick. not just what our systems say but what else can we add value to that we're not thinking of currently'

GAP: Current tick steps (1, 1a, 2, 2b, 2c, 3, 3a, 4a-n, 5, 6, 7, 7b, 8) all answer 'what does the system measure?' — they're parameter-tier and info-flow-tier (Meadows #6, #8, #12). NONE ask 'what is the system NOT measuring that would matter?' (Meadows #2 paradigm).

This is the difference between optimizing the existing question vs finding a better question.

PROPOSAL: Add Step 4o — value-gap-hunter — that runs at every doctrine-tier tick. NOT a probe of existing systems. A LLM-powered scan of 10 brainstorm dimensions for missing leverage.

INITIAL 10 BRAINSTORM DIMENSIONS (extend over time):
1. Cross-repo failure-mode harvester (Meadows #6 fleet-scale)
2. Skill bandit auto-experiments (Meadows #4 — currently dead loop, 0 events captured)
3. Customer-facing observability (ALPS/Blackfoot/TerraTitle have no flywheel visibility to us)
4. Cost telemetry / token burn rate per dispatch (Meadows #8 — can't throttle what we don't measure)
5. Cross-skill dependency graph (skill blast radius when one changes — invisible currently)
6. Mobile-eats end-user health (we measure ops health, not SaaS health)
7. Operator fatigue gate (Joshua sustainability — Joshua-step-away auto-suggestion)
8. Cross-time synthesis ('what would tomorrow-you wish you knew' artifact)
9. Adversarial/red-team orchestrator self-audit (jeff-convergence-audit not run this session)
10. Public artifact pipeline (flywheel as ZestStream's product showcase)

Step 4o IMPLEMENTATION:
  - Run at every doctrine-tier tick (cheaper than fleet-repair, scoped 5min budget)
  - Pick ONE dimension per tick (rotate through 10 = 1 per 5h cadence)
  - Output 1-paragraph 'what we're not seeing' with proposed measurement
  - File a P2/P3 bead if the gap warrants action
  - Receipt field: value_gap_dimension_scanned, value_gap_finding, bead_filed_id

ANTI-PATTERN PROTECTION:
  - NEVER dispatch a Step 4o-discovered finding directly — file a bead, let bead graph triage decide
  - Don't let value-gap scan crowd out FLEET_REPAIR or DISPATCH_BEAD (lower priority)
  - Cap 1 bead per tick from 4o (prevents infinite ideation)

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
  [ ] tick.md Step 4o documented with the 10 dimensions
  [ ] Helper script ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/value-gap-probe.sh (canonical-cli-scoping)
  [ ] Tick receipt schema extended with value_gap_* fields
  [ ] Validated by 3 ticks producing 3 different dimension scans
  [ ] At least 1 bead filed from value-gap discovery proves the loop works

DOCTRINE LINKS:
  - Meadows #2 (paradigm) — the highest leverage point
  - feedback_flywheel_never_idles
  - feedback_wire_into_ecosystem META-RULE
  - flywheel-aucl (loop-integrity 9th gap class — sibling 'find what we're not measuring' pattern)

DEPENDENCIES: none — can dispatch immediately when next worker frees.

WHEN TO ACTIVATE: Phase 1 design (~30min) at next free worker. Phase 2 implementation after design Joshua-approved.

SIBLING TO: flywheel-aucl (loop-integrity 9 gap class — 'what existing system fails'); this bead is the inverse — 'what existing system doesn't even ask'.
notes: vc-relive 2026-05-03: value-gap-hunter dimension #1 can consume Vibe Cockpit data now. Step 4i exposes vc_collector_health, vc_repo_coverage, vc_ledger_growth_per_cycle. Current residual gaps (caut missing, ru failure, ntm exists-column drift, 4/8 fleet repo visibility) are value-gap inputs for cross-repo failure-mode harvesting.

description:
Joshua-decision bead for value-gap-hunter Step 4o.

Deliverables drafted by value-gap-hunter-probe-design-2026_05_03:
- Probe: /tmp/value-gap-hunter-DESIGN/value-gap-probe.sh
- Tick patch: /tmp/value-gap-hunter-DESIGN/tick-md-step-4o.patch

Recommended apply order:

Joshua disposition required: apply | iterate | defer.

Policy gates:

- Step 4o lower priority than FLEET_REPAIR and DISPATCH_BEAD.
- 5 minute invocation budget.
- Cap 1 bead per tick from this source.

title: [orch-validate-callback-doctrine] orchestrator must validate worker DONE callbacks before forwarding
description:
## Trauma class
`orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation` — orchestrator receives worker
"DONE" callback, forwards summary to Joshua, but does NOT:
1. Probe artifacts the callback claims to have produced (file existence, version, smoke)
2. Auto-open fix-beads for any unfulfilled acceptance gates the callback surfaced
3. Auto-reopen "closed-but-unfulfilled" beads the audit revealed
4. Update fuckup-log + INCIDENTS.md with the validation findings

## Evidence (today's instance, 2026-05-03T22:13Z)
- p4 returned `DONE jr-capture-audit wired=false evidence=/tmp/jr-capture-wiring-audit.md`
- Audit explicitly named 3 beads closed as 'shipped' but artifacts missing:
  - flywheel-iaak (CLI ~/.local/bin/josh-requests) — missing
  - flywheel-a6ax (.flywheel/scripts/josh-request-tick-promote.sh) — missing  
  - flywheel-9wqw (related tick consumer) — missing
- As orchestrator I summarized to Joshua and asked "what to dispatch next"
- Joshua had to call out: "part of any orchestrator's job is to validate the work and open new beads"
- This is the validation gap — closed beads ≠ shipped artifacts

## Cost
Beads close → audit trail says "done" → future orchestrators trust the
closure → substrate gaps persist invisibly. Today's substrate-watchtower-must-be-wired
discovery is the SAME pattern: documentation said the watchtower exists, but
nothing validated execution.

## Acceptance gates (the rule itself)
On every worker DONE callback, orchestrator MUST:
1. Probe each acceptance-gate artifact named in dispatch packet (file exists, executable, smoke OK)
2. For each gate FAILED, file a fix-bead OR reopen the bead being audited
3. For each "shipped" bead the audit names as actually-not-shipped, run `br dep`/`br comment` to record + reopen with audit evidence
4. Append fuckup-log row only after the corrective beads are filed (not before)
5. Update flywheel-loop doctor to surface "callbacks_unvalidated" as a signal
   (so this class becomes data-driven, not memory-driven)

## DOD
- New L-rule in AGENTS.md: L?? CALLBACK-VALIDATION-IS-ORCHESTRATOR-JOB
- Mechanical gate: dispatch-template auto-includes "validation block" requirements
  for the orchestrator at the END of dispatch packet — orchestrator can't ignore
  it without the gate firing
- /flywheel:learn doctrine integration: every worker callback ingested produces
  validation receipts before LEARN tick advances
- Doctor signal: `callbacks_unvalidated_count` ≥ 1 → status=fail
- Memory note: feedback_orchestrator_validates_worker_callbacks.md

## Companion to
- L52 (issues-to-beads-or-no-bead-reason)
- L53 (fuckups_logged on blocker)
- L60 (no silent darkness)
- feedback_substrate_watchtower_must_be_wired (closed-bead != shipped substrate)
- feedback_orchestrator_must_dispatch (orchestrator decides; this rule says orchestrator ALSO validates)

## Out of scope for this bead
- Fixing the 3 falsely-closed beads themselves (that's flywheel-d62z scope)
- Implementing the doctor signal (separate fix-bead under flywheel-2p25)
notes: 2026-05-04T08:27:20Z: four-lens BLOCK_CLOSE. open children flywheel-9ijf + flywheel-2p25 (hub-blocker), jeff=FAIL, public=FAIL. brand+sniff PASS. did=5/5 tests=PASS, advanced envelope worker.

The orch-validate-callback-doctrine is now structurally enforced by the network of substrate shipped today: validate-callback.py --info R001 (flywheel-4x6pu), close-time gate validate-callback-before-close.sh (referenced by hm9ml dispatch-selector prefilter), four-lens close validator mission-fitness-callback-validator.sh (extended via flywheel-r0rox), L143 worker-close-requires-git-commit structural gate (flywheel-35exy + 1kdfk synonym merge), L107 lifecycle reserve-write-add-commit-release explicit (flywheel-y4e47), pre-flight-bead-presence block in dispatch packet (flywheel-lgmd3), dispatch-trigger-gated-precheck (flywheel-lh64t).
