title: [fleet-skill-discovery-doctor] surface fleet skill discovery in doctor
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/fleet-skill-reporting-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-xbuh

## Goal

Expose fleet skill discovery status in `flywheel-loop doctor --json` and flag suspicious long-running callbacks with no skill discovery receipts.

## Scope

- Add doctor section `fleet_skill_discovery` or equivalent.
- Report last-24h discovery count, top candidates by sightings, and pending skillos coordinator actions.
- Add warning when a worker has 3 consecutive >2h task callbacks with `skill_discoveries=0 sd_ids=none`, excluding explicit trivial/no-discovery classifications.
- Ensure doctor reads JSONL robustly if file is missing, empty, or has a malformed row.
- Add promotion path through `doctor-signal-bead-promotion.sh` when pending coordinator action or suspicious no-discovery count crosses threshold.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json | jq -e '.fleet_skill_discovery'` passes.
2. Doctor fixture with no discovery file reports zero counts and status `ok|warn`, not crash.
3. Doctor fixture with malformed JSONL row reports malformed count and continues processing valid rows.
4. Doctor fixture with 7 discoveries in 24h reports `last_24h_discoveries == 7` and top candidates by sighting count.
5. Doctor fixture with one candidate at 3 sightings reports pending coordinator action.
6. Doctor fixture with 3 consecutive >2h callbacks and no `sd-*` rows reports a warning class for skipped discovery duty.
7. `doctor-signal-bead-promotion.sh` or equivalent can promote the warning to a bead with candidate evidence.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: doctor tests cover empty, malformed, normal, threshold, and suspicious-callback cases.
- DOCUMENTED: README/canonical paths describe the doctor field and warning threshold.
- SURFACED: doctor JSON exposes counts and promotion-ready warnings.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L50, L52, L56, L60, L61, L71, L80.
- Plan lines 149-170 for doctor and acceptance criteria.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No coordinator mutation logic.
- No Agent Mail thread creation.
- No live dispatch to skillos.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `surfaced fleet skill discovery in doctor; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[fleet-skill-discovery-doctor]`.
close_reason: DID: surfaced fleet_skill_discovery in doctor JSON, added malformed-row resilience, top-candidate and pending coordinator action counts, skipped-discovery-duty warning, and auto-doctor promotion wiring. DIDNT: did not mutate skillos coordinator or notification wrapper; live doctor lib files are patched outside repo git. GAPS: none. evidence=/tmp/flywheel-5gyv-compliance-pack.md commit=c5c2af6 tests=PASS beads_updated=flywheel-5gyv:closed no_bead_reason=no_new_gap fuckups_logged=none

title: [fleet-skill-reporting-e2e] document and smoke-test enterprise reporting workflow
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/fleet-skill-reporting-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-xbuh

## Goal

Wire fleet skill discovery into docs, canonical paths, skill guidance, and final end-to-end smoke so the enterprise reporting layer is validated, documented, and surfaced.

## Scope

- Update README and `.flywheel/canonical-paths.txt` with `skill-discoveries.jsonl`, coordinator command, doctor field, and notification surface.
- Add or update skill guidance for workers discovering reusable patterns.
- Add end-to-end fixture: worker writes `sd-*` row, callback reports `skill_discoveries=1`, coordinator groups it, doctor reports it, and notification dry-run targets skillos.
- Ensure no source secrets or Agent Mail bearer tokens are written to discovery rows or notifications.
- Decide whether a new AGENTS L-rule is needed or whether existing L50/L55/L56/L61/L71 cover it; document the decision.

## Acceptance Gates

1. README references the fleet skill discovery workflow and the command(s) used to append, coordinate, and inspect discoveries.
2. `.flywheel/canonical-paths.txt` references `~/.local/state/flywheel/skill-discoveries.jsonl`, the coordinator surface, and doctor field.
3. Skill guidance or dispatch guidance tells workers when to report `pattern-emerged`, `skill-search-miss`, and `skill-found-but-incomplete`.
4. End-to-end smoke fixture proves append -> callback field -> coordinator dry-run -> doctor JSON -> skillos notification dry-run.
5. Secret-scrub fixture proves discovery snippets/notifications redact token-shaped values before storage or send.
6. AGENTS doctrine decision is recorded: either new L-rule landed with evidence, or explicit no-new-L-rule rationale references existing companion rules.
7. `br dep cycles` remains empty after all fleet skill reporting beads are wired.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: E2E smoke and secret-scrub tests pass.
- DOCUMENTED: README, canonical paths, skill/dispatch guidance, and optional AGENTS doctrine are updated.
- SURFACED: doctor, skillos notification, Agent Mail thread, and pulse row make findings visible.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L55, L56, L61, L71, L80.
- Plan lines 174-206 for enterprise reporting and four-pillar observatory.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No skill regression detection.
- No cross-fleet skill recommendation engine.
- No supervised discovery quality scoring.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `validated/documented/surfaced fleet skill reporting E2E; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[fleet-skill-reporting-e2e]`.

title: [pane-work-signal-fixtures] add Codex false-idle regression corpus
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/pane-work-signal-truth-source-2026-05-01.md
Parent bead: flywheel-5ktd
Plan phase/slice: Implementation breakdown 4, false-idle fixture and regression corpus.

## Goal

Create the regression fixture corpus for Codex pane false-idle detection so later helper, tick, status, and doctor changes can prove behavior against stable inputs instead of live pane state.

## Scope

- Files: `tests/fixtures/pane-work-signal/**`, `tests/pane-work-signal-fixtures-self-test.sh` or the nearest existing test harness.
- Fixture cases:
  - Codex pane tail containing `Working (...)` within the last 12 lines.
  - Codex pane where only a tail-3 sample would miss `Working (...)`.
  - `ntm health` idle/error paired with PWS visible-working evidence.
  - Fresh hash-delta sample without visible foreground text.
  - Stale sample older than 5 minutes.
  - Non-Codex pane where `ntm health` remains canonical and PWS is advisory.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `test -d tests/fixtures/pane-work-signal` proves the fixture directory exists.
2. `find tests/fixtures/pane-work-signal -type f | wc -l` reports at least 6 fixture files covering the listed cases.
3. `bash tests/pane-work-signal-fixtures-self-test.sh` passes and validates each fixture has expected truth state metadata.
4. `rg -n "Working \\(" tests/fixtures/pane-work-signal` proves visible Codex foreground work appears in a fixture.
5. `rg -n "ntm_health=(idle|error).*pws_truth=working|pws_truth=working.*ntm_health=(idle|error)" tests/fixtures/pane-work-signal tests/pane-work-signal-fixtures-self-test.sh` proves the false-idle case is represented.
6. `rg -n "tail-12|last 12|last_12" tests/pane-work-signal-fixtures-self-test.sh tests/fixtures/pane-work-signal` proves the tail-12 minimum is tested.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: fixture self-test runs locally and fails if any expected state is missing.
- DOCUMENTED: fixture names and metadata state which plan condition each file represents.
- SURFACED: downstream beads cite this corpus as the stable evidence source for helper, tick, status, and doctor behavior.

## Doctrine Refs

- AGENTS.md L50 Socraticode mandatory in dispatches.
- AGENTS.md L69 probe runtime context before deciding state.
- AGENTS.md L71 validate-and-redispatch discipline.
- Memory: feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more.
- Memory: feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: flywheel-5ktd parent decomposition.
- Blocks: pane-work-signal helper parser/classifier bead.

## Out Of Scope

- No production parser changes.
- No tick or status behavior changes.
- No direct pane capture outside test fixtures.

## Rollback / Dry-Run Posture

Fixture-only change. Revert by deleting `tests/fixtures/pane-work-signal/**` and its self-test if later implementation chooses a different fixture harness.

## DOD

Close with reason: `implemented false-idle PWS regression fixtures from pane-work-signal plan; tests=<command/output>; commit_tag=flywheel-5ktd-fixtures`.

## Callback Evidence Shape

Worker callback should include: `DONE <bead-id> evidence=<fixture paths + self-test receipt> tests=PASS fixtures=<N> callback_delivery_verified=true`.

title: [pane-work-signal-helper] extend Codex foreground parser and truth-state classifier
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/pane-work-signal-truth-source-2026-05-01.md
Parent bead: flywheel-5ktd
Plan phase/slice: Implementation breakdown 1, helper parser and Codex truth-state classifier.

## Goal

Extend `.flywheel/scripts/pane-work-signal.sh` so Codex pane activity classification uses foreground `Working (...)` evidence, hash deltas, stale-sample handling, and explicit truth-source fields from the plan.

## Scope

- Files: `.flywheel/scripts/pane-work-signal.sh`, helper-specific tests under `tests/**`.
- Add or verify JSON fields:
  - `foreground_working_state`
  - `foreground_working_evidence`
  - `truth_source`
  - `truth_state`
  - `truth_reason`
- Codex classification order:
  1. `Working (...)` in last 12 lines means `truth_state=working`.
  2. No visible work but hash changed within 90 seconds means `truth_state=working`.
  3. No usable sample older than 5 minutes means `truth_state=stale`.
  4. Otherwise `truth_state=idle`.
- Non-Codex panes keep `ntm health` as canonical unless explicit visible work text is present.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash -n .flywheel/scripts/pane-work-signal.sh` passes.
2. `.flywheel/scripts/pane-work-signal.sh --help` or documented usage includes `--lines` and the JSON fields above.
3. `bash tests/pane-work-signal-helper-self-test.sh` passes against the fixture corpus.
4. The Codex visible-working fixture returns `truth_state=working` and `truth_source=pane_work_signal`.
5. The tail-3 false-negative fixture returns `foreground_working_state=true` when sampled with the plan's tail-12 minimum.
6. The stale fixture returns `truth_state=stale` with a `truth_reason` naming stale or missing sample age.
7. The non-Codex fixture preserves `ntm health` as canonical unless explicit visible work evidence is present.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: helper tests prove each classifier branch with fixtures, not live pane timing.
- DOCUMENTED: `--help` or inline usage describes the new JSON contract.
- SURFACED: helper output fields are ready for tick receipt, status display, and doctor signal consumers.

## Doctrine Refs

- AGENTS.md L60 no silent darkness for long-running work.
- AGENTS.md L67 live truth source.
- AGENTS.md L69 probe runtime context before deciding state.
- AGENTS.md L71 validate-and-redispatch discipline.
- Memory: feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more.
- Memory: feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: pane-work-signal fixture corpus bead.
- Blocks: tick integration, status display, doctor signal beads.

## Out Of Scope

- No dispatch-capacity decision changes in the tick driver.
- No status UI changes.
- No bead promotion logic.

## Rollback / Dry-Run Posture

Helper changes must remain callable in fixture mode before live pane sampling. If behavior regresses, downstream tick integration can set `FLYWHEEL_PANE_WORK_SIGNAL_DISABLE=1` or `.flywheel/disable-pane-work-signal` to fall back to `ntm health`.

## DOD

Close with reason: `implemented Codex PWS truth-state classifier; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=flywheel-5ktd-helper`.

## Callback Evidence Shape

Worker callback should include: `DONE <bead-id> evidence=<helper test receipt> tests=PASS fields=foreground_working_state,truth_state,truth_source callback_delivery_verified=true`.

title: [pane-work-signal-tick] use PWS as Codex dispatch-capacity truth
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/pane-work-signal-truth-source-2026-05-01.md
Parent bead: flywheel-5ktd
Plan phase/slice: Implementation breakdown 2, tick Step 3 and receipt schema.

## Goal

Make the tick driver treat `pane-work-signal.sh` as the canonical Codex dispatch-capacity truth when it disagrees with `ntm health`, while preserving `ntm health` for topology and non-Codex activity state.

## Scope

- Files: `~/.claude/commands/flywheel/tick.md`, `.flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick`, receipt schema or validation files if present.
- Patch the Step 3 language that currently treats `ntm health` idle/active as true for Codex panes.
- For each `agent_type=="cod"` pane:
  - run `.flywheel/scripts/pane-work-signal.sh "$SESSION" "$PANE" --lines 80`;
  - if `ntm health` says idle/error and PWS says working, capacity is false;
  - log SOFT `pane_work_signal_disagrees_with_ntm_health`;
  - add receipt fields `pane_work_signal_sampled`, `pane_work_signal_by_pane`, `pane_work_signal_disagreements`, and `idle_capacity_source="pane_work_signal_for_codex"`.
- Respect rollback controls: `FLYWHEEL_PANE_WORK_SIGNAL_DISABLE=1` and `.flywheel/disable-pane-work-signal`.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash -n .flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick` passes if the tick driver is shell-backed.
2. `bash tests/validate-tick-phase.sh` passes after the Step 3 update.

description:

## Goal

- Doctor JSON field: `.pane_work_signal`.
- Required semantics:
  - codex PWS working + `ntm health` idle/error => warning, capacity false, class `ntm_codex_false_idle`.
  - same pane disagreement for 3 consecutive ticks or longer than 30 minutes => error.

title: Add supported fuckup-log mark-processed command
description:
## Goal

Add a supported `flywheel-loop fuckup mark-processed` (or equivalent) command so resolved fuckup-log rows can be marked without manual JSONL edits.

## Context

Repo-local skillos bead `skillos-3lv` found that `flywheel-loop fuckup list --unprocessed` still reports row 73 (`run-server-entrypoint-permission-drift`) after the actual follow-up bead `skillos-16t` was completed. The live `flywheel-loop` help and source expose only `fuckup log`, `list`, `triage`, and `harvest`; no supported mutation path exists for setting `processed_at` / `processed_into`. Manual editing of `~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl` is intentionally avoided.

## Dependencies

None known. Related but broader: `flywheel-2ha` (`fuckup-to-bead-pipeline-planning-convergence-required`) is blocked and does not provide this narrow mutation primitive.

## Inputs / Outputs

INPUTS:
- `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop`
- `~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl`
- Evidence row: line 73, trauma_class `run-server-entrypoint-permission-drift`, processed by skillos bead `skillos-16t`

OUTPUTS:
- A documented command such as `flywheel-loop fuckup mark-processed --class <class> --ts <timestamp> --processed-into <bead-or-artifact> [--json]`, or a safer equivalent using an immutable row selector.
- Tests or dry-run fixture proving only the intended row is mutated.
- Help text updated so agents discover the supported path.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Command can mark exactly one matching row processed, preserving every other JSONL line byte-for-byte or semantically equivalent with atomic rewrite.
- Requires an explicit row selector (timestamp + trauma_class, source_event_id, or line/id) and refuses ambiguous matches.
- Writes `processed_at` and `processed_into`; optionally records `processed_by` / `processed_note`.
- Supports `--dry-run` and `--json`.
- Unit/fixture test covers one success, one ambiguous match refusal, and one no-match refusal.
- `flywheel-loop fuckup list --unprocessed --class run-server-entrypoint-permission-drift` no longer reports the row after marking it into `skillos-16t` in a safe test or controlled live run.

## Testing Obligations

- Run an isolated temp-file test by setting `FLYWHEEL_FUCKUP_LOG` to a fixture JSONL.
- Run `flywheel-loop fuckup list --unprocessed --json` before/after on the fixture.
- Run relevant shell/static tests for the binary.

## Definition of Done

- Tool patch committed in the flywheel/tooling surface.
- Help text documents the new command.
- A controlled live use can close `skillos-3lv` without manual JSONL edits.
- AUTONOMY: autonomous.
close_reason: Plan-space spec /tmp/fuckup_mark_processed_plan.md: flywheel-loop fuckup mark-processed --row N --reason TEXT [--processed-into PATH] subcommand. ladder_passed=yes.

title: [shape-a-invertibility-wire-in] add classification_rule_id to validate-callback.py + quality-bar-close-gate.sh per audit-machinery-hygiene-discipline Shape A
description:
Follow-up bead for flywheel-3nsp1 audit pass. Surfaced 2 high-blast-radius validators in flywheel that emit failures WITHOUT Shape A invertibility (classification_rule_id field). Each currently emits 17-30 fail paths under coarse-grained categories — operators can't grep one rule ID to find the rule's premise.

Audit report: .flywheel/audit/flywheel-3nsp1/audit-report.md

Surfaces to fix:
1. .flywheel/scripts/validate-callback.py (880 lines) — gates every callback close (very high blast radius)
   - ~30 status=fail emit sites
   - Failures routed via failure_classes (correctness, transient, etc.) — these are CATEGORIES not specific rule IDs
   - Add classification_rule_id field to every errors.append() call
   - Document rule registry at top of file (e.g., VC-R001 through VC-R030)
2. .flywheel/scripts/quality-bar-close-gate.sh (1527 lines) — gates Phase 5 close (very high blast radius)
   - ~17 hardcoded required_evidence items at line 87
   - Failures emit string codes like 'evidence_pack_resolver_exec_failed:...' (inconsistent)
   - Add canonical rule_id field per required_evidence (QB-R001 through QB-R017)

Canonical reference instance: .flywheel/scripts/canonical-cli-lint.sh (556 lines) — Shape A reference instance with L1-L9 rule IDs emitted per violation row. Source documents each L<N> rule via comment header. This is the pattern to replicate.

Acceptance gates:
- AG1: every status='fail' emit in validate-callback.py carries a rule_id field (~30 sites)
- AG2: every failure path in quality-bar-close-gate.sh carries a rule_id field
- AG3: rule registry documented at top of each file (rule_id → premise → inversion path)
- AG4: audit verification predicate runs clean:
  for surface in validate-callback.py quality-bar-close-gate.sh; do
    count_fails=$(grep -cE 'status.*fail' $surface)
    count_ids=$(grep -cE 'rule_id' $surface)
    [[ $count_ids -ge $count_fails ]]
  done

Estimated effort: 3-4 hours combined (~30 + 17 = ~47 emit-site annotations + 2 rule registries).

This closes the audit-machinery-hygiene-discipline propagation for high-blast-radius surfaces. After this bead closes, fleet has 2 fully-invertible high-blast-radius validators + 1 canonical reference instance (canonical-cli-lint.sh) for the Shape A pattern.

Cross-refs: parent doctrine=.flywheel/doctrine/audit-machinery-hygiene-discipline.md; checklist=.flywheel/doctrine/audit-machinery-hygiene-author-checklist.md (flywheel-c5ovc); audit report=.flywheel/audit/flywheel-3nsp1/audit-report.md; canonical-Shape-A trauma instance=flywheel-iro0k→flywheel-03aca→flywheel-a33xj (cross-pane-git-probe arc); canonical-Shape-A REFERENCE instance=canonical-cli-lint.sh L1-L9.
close_reason: DONE 990/1000 - Shape A invertibility wire-in pilot shipped for validate-callback.py: FAILURE_CODE_REGISTRY constant (21 entries: premise+source+inversion) + --why-code CLI flag (list/known/unknown modes) + live verification all 3 modes + coverage 21 registered ≥ 18 emitted; quality-bar-close-gate.sh deferred to continuation flywheel-bg06b (1527 lines + ad-hoc fail-string refactor)

title: [skillos still LIMPING] 2 dead signals after mobile-eats-pattern rollback — need orchestrator-cc + fuckup-handler
description:
Filed 2026-05-03 ~09:20Z by orchestrator-cc following flywheel-b6js.1 ROLLBACK callback.

EVIDENCE: skillos mobile-eats-pattern apply went from 2/5 LIMPING → 3/5 LIMPING → rolled back per dispatch contract (5/5 verify gate not met). jsm_sync ITSELF worked (84 routed decisions, canonical receipt written 08:10Z) but loop-integrity contract requires all 5 signals.

DEAD SIGNALS POST-ROLLBACK (per L60 contract):
1. pane_state_changed_since_last_tick — likely skillos panes not poked by new launchd payload (jsm-sync writes ledger but doesn't dispatch worker tasks)
2. fuckup_decisions_made_since_last_tick — skillos has no orchestrator-cc to process fuckup-log between ticks

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS:
The mobile-eats pattern works because mobile-eats has BOTH (a) work payload AND (b) workers consuming the payload. Skillos got (a) jsm-sync as work payload but workers don't auto-consume — there's no orchestrator-cc to read jsm-sync output and dispatch follow-up tasks to skillos worker panes.

This validates Joshua's earlier 'dumb loop' framing more precisely: skillos needs an ORCHESTRATOR LAYER, not just a work-payload script. The original Option A I proposed (add CC orchestrator pane to skillos session) was directionally right; mobile-eats apparently has equivalent orchestration via its own session topology that I need to study.

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
[ ] Compare mobile-eats session topology vs skillos: identify orchestration mechanism mobile-eats uses (could be cc pane, could be product-specific)
[ ] Decide skillos orchestration shape: A=add cc pane, B=write jsm-sync-orchestrator helper that auto-dispatches to existing codex panes, C=cross-session L61 to flywheel orchestrator
[ ] File design bead OR dispatch immediately if scope allows
[ ] If A chosen: register cc pane in skillos session topology, wire FoggyBear identity
[ ] If B chosen: write skillos-orchestrator.sh as launchd post-tick step
[ ] Re-test loop-integrity after fix; target HEALTHY 5/5

DEPENDENCIES:
- flywheel-aucl (loop-integrity 9th gap class — defines the contract this satisfies)
- flywheel-b6js (skillos mission expansion — parent epic)
- flywheel-b6js.1 (the rolled-back attempt — sibling)

DOCTRINE LINKS:
- AGENTS.md L60 (5-signal contract — definition we're targeting)
- /tmp/apply-skillos-mobile-eats-pattern_findings.md (rollback receipt for evidence)
- /tmp/skillos-limping-diagnostic_findings.md (original 2/5 baseline)

title: [D] create launchd plist registry matrix
description:
## Goal
Create a registry and restart matrix for tentacle daemons managed by launchd or background processes.

## Context
ntm-fleet-health, vc-daemon, and future tentacle daemons need centralized expected-state checks.

## Dependencies
Depends on C1 and C7 initially; can expand as more daemon tentacles appear.

## Inputs / Outputs
Files in scope: launchd plist registry; doctor probe; restart matrix script or docs
Output artifact: committed implementation and/or evidence artifact named in acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria
- registry lists plist label, expected uptime, binary path, restart policy; probe reconciles launchctl output and returns JSON

## Testing Obligations
- Run launchctl list reconciliation against known labels and validate missing/stale daemon status.

## Origin
00-MASTER-PLAN.md §IV.6 lines 366-371.

## Out of Scope
Do not unload/reload services in audit mode.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: include [D3] in the commit message or close reason.
- Acceptance evidence is captured in the bead closeout.
- Dependency graph remains acyclic after close.
close_reason: [D3] Added read-only launchd plist registry matrix with JSON reconciliation, fixture stale/missing validation, and L112 evidence probe.

title: [skillos-substrate-broken] FoggyBear retired in fleet-mail + run-30m-loop.sh has 4 errors
description:
## Discovery
2026-05-03T20:55Z Joshua flagged skillos completely idle. Diagnosis from flywheel orch:

### Layer 1: Plist runs but tick body fails
- launchctl: state=running, stdout shows tick 4 dispatched 20:24:00Z (cron alive)
- stderr (~/Developer/skillos/state/launchd-loop-stderr.log): 4 distinct error classes
  - line 77 of run-30m-loop.sh: skillos-172 (bead ID) executed as command — quoting bug
  - line 77: state/ directory passed where file expected
  - .flywheel/STATE.md and last_closeout_receipt.json: Permission denied
  - jsm push <PATH>: missing PATH arg

### Layer 2: FoggyBear identity is RETIRED in fleet-mail
- Discovered when LavenderGlen (flywheel orch identity) attempted to send diagnostic message
- Error: "Agent 'FoggyBear' is retired and no longer accepts new messages"
- Means: NO cross-orch inbound coordination possible to skillos via canonical fleet-mail channel
- May be intentional (skillos orch retired their own identity) OR may be incident artifact (token loss → reregistration → old retired)

## Why this matters
This is not just a script bug — it's a substrate-level isolation. Even if we fix run-30m-loop.sh, skillos can't receive cross-orch messages until FoggyBear is unretired or replaced. Mobile-eats has a similar exposure (TurquoiseWolf token loss earlier today; recovered).

## Cross-orch boundary
- DO NOT unretire FoggyBear without skillos orch consent (identity is policy, not plumbing)
- DO NOT edit run-30m-loop.sh (skillos repo, cross-orch boundary)
- DO surface to Joshua immediately
- DO rotate LavenderGlen's registration_token because it just got Read-tool-exposed in this orch transcript (L58 violation)

## Acceptance gates
1. Joshua decides: unretire FoggyBear OR coordinate with skillos orch via different channel (in-person? SMS? other identity?)

title: [fleet-substrate-watchtower-gap] hourly installed-vs-latest probe for 7 Jeff binaries
description:
## Symptom (2026-05-03)
Joshua had to find Jeff's v0.5.1 release manually. Doctrine
`feedback_jeff_substrate_version_drift.md` says every tick should probe
installed-vs-latest for all 7 Jeff binaries; today's flywheel tick did NOT.

## Evidence
- daily-jeff-ingest.jsonl 05:37Z + 05:39Z runs report `new_items: 0` because
  every source was treated as a fresh baseline ("no prior daily snapshot")
- Watchtower ate the diff instead of surfacing it
- Jeff published dcg v0.5.1 at 20:25Z — 2 hours before Joshua flagged it,
  zero ingest run since (daily, not hourly)
- This DISPATCH tick (21:55Z) had no installed-vs-latest probe gate

## Cost
- 3× fleet death today (potentially related to v0.5.0 heredoc bug fixed in v0.5.1)
- Joshua-as-meat-puppet to find substrate updates the watchtower should surface

## Root cause
Two faults:
1. daily-jeff-ingest baseline-reset semantics: when no prior snapshot, it
   treats current as baseline (silent) rather than flagging "first run today"
2. flywheel tick has no inline installed-vs-latest probe for the 7 canonical
   binaries; documented but unwired

## Acceptance
- HOURLY (not daily) probe of all 7 Jeff binaries: dcg, ntm, br, cm, mcp-agent-mail, frankensqlite, jsm
- Auto-bead-creation when `installed != latest` with priority based on time-since-publish
- Surfaces in /flywheel:tick prelude alongside doctor signal
- Memory note bumped: probe is wired, not just documented

## DOD
Next time Jeff ships any of 7 binaries, a bead lands within ≤1 hour without
Joshua finding it.

title: Add Agent Mail token redaction wrapper
description:
## Goal
Add a synthetic-testable wrapper or contract that prevents Agent Mail registration_token and sender_token values from appearing in visible orchestrator or worker pane tool-call arguments.

## Context
PHASE: flywheel wrapper safety follow-up from skillos-g1t
CRITERION: credential-shaped transcript exposure prevention
The skillos scheduled tick on 2026-05-02 routed trauma_class agent-mail-token-echo-in-pane. Evidence now shows repeated worker-pane exposure of Agent Mail registration_token arguments in visible MCP/tool-call transcript surfaces. Repo/report artifacts were clean, and no token rotation is requested. The nearest existing substrate is fleet-mail token vault load/save helpers, but no flywheel bead currently owns token echo/redaction behavior.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Use only a synthetic value such as FAKE_AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN_1234567890.
2. Route that synthetic value through the same wrapper/dispatch/tool-call path that would otherwise pass registration_token or sender_token.
3. Capture generated dispatch text, route artifacts, wrapper logs, and pane-safe output.
4. Assert the synthetic value is absent from every visible surface while the operation either succeeds through non-echoing token access or fails closed before rendering the value.

## Dependencies
None known. Coordinate with flywheel L65 fleet-mail token vault work and existing RubyCreek token-surface bead flywheel-bpd only for context; this bug is about not echoing token values, not surfacing or rotating tokens.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: skillos artifacts state/agent-mail-token-echo-mitigation-2026-05-02T024342Z.json and state/agent-mail-token-echo-followup-2026-05-02T031535Z.json; flywheel scripts .flywheel/scripts/fleet-mail-token-load.sh and fleet-mail-token-save.sh; Agent Mail MCP call patterns.
OUTPUTS: a flywheel-owned wrapper, helper, or doctrine/tooling patch that lets orchestrators use registration_token/sender_token without placing literal values in LLM-visible MCP arguments or pane transcripts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Use only synthetic token values in tests and examples.
- Identify every flywheel/orchestrator call path that passes registration_token or sender_token directly into visible MCP tool arguments.
- Provide a wrapper or contract that reads token material from vault/session state outside the visible argument surface, or explicitly forbids token-bearing MCP calls when tokenless session auth is available.
- Add a regression probe that fails if a synthetic token literal is rendered in a generated dispatch packet, route artifact, or wrapper log.
- Do not rotate Agent Mail tokens. Do not print, store, or copy real token values.

## Testing Obligations
- Run the synthetic token redaction probe.
- Run flywheel br lint and br dep cycles.
- Run the smallest relevant Agent Mail liveness probe without any token-bearing argument.

## Definition of Done
- Owning flywheel mitigation exists and is referenced from skillos-g1t closeout.
- NO_LITERAL_TOKEN_VALUES_RETAINED.
- AUTONOMY: autonomous.
close_reason: Wrapper at .flywheel/scripts/agent-mail-send-redacted.sh implements Option B; synthetic test at .flywheel/scripts/test-agent-mail-redact.sh PASSES (synthetic token absent from dispatch text + wrapper logs + pane-visible tool-call args). Backup n/a: new files. L58 doctrine now mechanically enforceable for 5 inventoried surface classes; live MCP bridge follow-up noted.

title: ntm wait --json timeout emits human text
description:

During flywheel-7fcki validation, native `ntm wait flywheel --until=idle --any --timeout=1s --json` returned colored human timeout text instead of JSON. `idle-pane-auto-dispatch.sh` wraps that raw output into a JSON envelope, but the native `ntm wait --json` contract should be fixed or documented upstream because dispatch automation expects machine-readable timeout receipts.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Reproduce `ntm wait <session> --until=idle --any --timeout=1s --json` timeout behavior and capture whether output is valid JSON.
AG2: Fix native timeout output to emit a JSON object when `--json` is set, or document the non-JSON timeout contract and update flywheel wrappers accordingly.
AG3: Add a focused regression test asserting timeout output is parseable JSON when `--json` is requested.

title: [d23ow-followup] wire cd-realpath-wrapper into build-dispatch-packet.sh TMP LIFECYCLE template
description:
AG2 from flywheel-d23ow deferred per peer reservation conflict (pane 2 holds build-dispatch-packet.sh for flywheel-lgmd3 worker-discipline bead). Wire-in is mechanical:

In .flywheel/scripts/build-dispatch-packet.sh line ~197 (TMP LIFECYCLE BLOCK literal): replace
  At dispatch start create one scratch directory: WORK_TMP="$(mktemp -d -t %s.XXXXXX)". Copy durable evidence out before close, remove the directory, and callback with tmp_dir_released=true.

with the safe two-line idiom + cd-realpath-wrapper guidance:
  At dispatch start create one scratch directory:
    WORK_TMP="$(mktemp -d -t %s.XXXXXX)" || { echo "ERR: mktemp failed" >&2; exit 1; }
    cd "$WORK_TMP" || { echo "ERR: cd failed: $WORK_TMP" >&2; exit 1; }
  For subsequent cd into worker-supplied paths (e.g., fixture dirs), use the
  Layer-1 prevention primitive: .flywheel/scripts/cd-realpath-wrapper.sh
  (resolves+verifies sandbox before cd; refuses outside-sandbox or
  realpath-fail with explicit rc). Sister recovery primitive on clobber:
  .flywheel/scripts/clobber-recovery.sh.

Acceptance:
- build-dispatch-packet.sh emits the safe two-line idiom in TMP LIFECYCLE BLOCK
- build-dispatch-packet.sh references cd-realpath-wrapper.sh + clobber-recovery.sh
- Existing dispatch packets generated post-fix carry the safe idiom
- bash -n clean

Out of scope (per parent bead): upstream br create source_repo canonicalization (already disposed via flywheel-8x2le AG2; see WHY-NOT-FILED.md).

Sister beads: flywheel-d23ow (this followup parent), flywheel-tpprm (clobber-recovery.sh recovery primitive), flywheel-lgmd3 (concurrent worker-discipline bead — same file).

title: [jeff-stack J10] end-to-end-validation-test
description:
End-to-end validation test of the entire Jeff-stack pipeline.

DEPENDS ON: J5, J7, J8, J9.

PURPOSE: Single comprehensive test that proves all surfaces work together.

VALIDATION MATRIX:

| # | Test | Pass criteria |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | gh auth survives new shell | `bash -lc 'gh auth status'` ok |
| T2 | gh auth survives reboot | (post-reboot manual verification) |
| T3 | x-cli timeline returns tweets | `x-cli ... timeline doodlestein --limit 5` returns ≥1 tweet |
| T4 | All 177 atom feeds 200 OK | curl -sI per URL returns 200 (allow 1-2 deleted) |
| T5 | Canonical clone tree complete | `find ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack -name .git \| wc -l` ≥ 175 |
| T6 | No duplicate clones | Existing 19 are symlinks or backed up |
| T7 | Socraticode list 177 | `mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects` shows ≥175 jeff entries |
| T8 | Socraticode search works | Query across 5 random repos returns hits |
| T9 | Daily diff dry-run | jeff-daily-diff.sh produces valid report |
| T10 | Launchd plist loaded | `launchctl list | grep jeff-daily` |
| T11 | Manual kickstart succeeds | `launchctl kickstart` writes report + receipt |
| T12 | Report schema valid | Generated report matches J9 schema |
| T13 | Verdict logic fires | At least 3 of 4 verdict types appear in test run |
| T14 | Idempotency | Run twice within 5 min, second produces zero diffs |
| T15 | Resumable | Kill mid-run, restart, completes correctly |
| T16 | AGENTS.md L63 evidence | (J11 — checked separately) |
| T17 | daily-jeff-ingest.sh clean | Original ingest script also runs 0-warning |

PROCEDURE:
1. Run each test, capture pass/fail with evidence
2. Write report to /tmp/jeff-stack-validation-<ts>.md
3. If any test fails: file follow-up bead, do not mark this bead done

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. All 17 tests run
2. ≥15/17 PASS (T2 may be deferred to next reboot; T16 deferred to J11)
3. Failures are documented with reproduction steps
4. Report is human-readable + machine-parseable

NO_BEAD_REASON: If a test fails for a reason we accept (e.g. 1-2 jeff repos deleted upstream → atom 404), document but do not block.
close_reason: superseded by flywheel-ys7em [jeff-daily-corpus-diff] per Joshua signoff 2026-05-10. Original J-chain assumed local-clone substrate; replacement uses GitHub API daily activity poll. Spec at .flywheel/audit/jeff-daily-corpus-diff/apply-spec.md

## Trigger
Joshua 2026-05-04: "once we gain a great understanding - we need to update skillos in a way that locks it into their goals / mission, etc. /donella-meadows-systems-thinking style"

- .flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/01-doctrine-cluster.md + 02-code-patterns.md as paradigm anchor

## Meadows leverage being applied
- #2 PARADIGM: re-anchor skillos's purpose from "build skills" to "ship skills that pass Jeff's 8-cluster doctrine + 4 ADOPT-grade code patterns" — paradigm is the mindset that generates all goals/rules/info-flows downstream
- #3 GOALS: rewrite GOAL.md success criteria to measure skill quality in ADOPT/EXTEND/DIVERGE terms (not skill count)
- #5 RULES (sibling reinforcement): every new skill PR includes "doctrine-cluster-coverage" + "code-pattern-applicability" sections — rule changes propagate automatically once paradigm + goals shift

## Acceptance gates

AG1: read .flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/{01,02,06}*.md as paradigm-input artifacts
AG2: rewrite ~/Developer/skillos/.flywheel/MISSION.md with paradigm anchor block: "Skillos exists to ship skills that meet the Jeff-doctrine bar (8 clusters) and code-pattern bar (4 ADOPT verdicts: idempotency-key-fail-closed, testing-fixture-conventions, lock-file-convention, frontmatter-validation)"
AG3: rewrite ~/Developer/skillos/.flywheel/GOAL.md success criteria as Meadows-stock-and-flow:
   - STOCK: skills meeting doctrine bar / total skills (target ratio ≥80% by 2026-Q3)
   - INFLOW: new skills shipped per week passing all 4 ADOPT pattern checks at PR time
   - OUTFLOW: skills retired/deprecated for failing doctrine refresh
   - FEEDBACK LOOP: monthly enhancement-matrix re-scan against new Jeff drops detects drift
AG4: file follow-up skillos-side bead `skillos-skill-author-checklist-jeff-doctrine` requiring all new skills to populate doctrine-cluster-coverage table (sources from 01-doctrine-cluster.md) and code-pattern-applicability table (sources from 02-code-patterns.md) before merge — paradigm-to-rule propagation

AG6: receipt at /tmp/skillos-meadows-lock-in-evidence.md cites: paradigm-block path, GOAL.md diff, follow-up bead ID, L-rule entry

## Per the new contract: callback MUST report did/didnt/gaps

- DIDNT: AGs you can't meet (file gap-bead before callback)
- GAPS: drift between Jeff-mined paradigm and existing skillos paradigm (file gap-beads as `skillos-paradigm-gap.<N>`)

description:
## Pattern observed (2026-05-09)

`probe_wired_but_cold()` in `.flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh:415` builds a substring search corpus from `recent_ledger_text(days=30, max_bytes=4_000_000)` (line 355). The corpus prepends each ledger's filename (`chunks.append(path.name + ...)`) but is capped at 4MB total. Ledgers are processed alphabetically.

When the first ~12 alphabetical ledgers exceed 4MB combined, all later-alphabet ledger NAMES are silently dropped from the corpus — they never contribute their name to the substring search. Any script whose name only appears in dropped-ledger names is then false-flagged as wired-but-cold.

## Reproduction (2026-05-09T20:09Z, ~/.local/state/flywheel)

```python
# 12 ledgers kept (4MB consumed), 97 dropped
# Both fuckup-log.jsonl (4.8MB, today's mtime) and fuckup-processed.jsonl
# DROPPED — their names never enter ledger_text
# lib/fuckup.sh flagged wired-but-cold even though portable_doctor.sh:614
# calls fuckup_triage_compute_json() actively
```

False-positive bead this generated: flywheel-2xdi.41 (closed under this disposition).

## Proposed fix

Two-pass corpus build:
1. Pass 1 (always, no budget): for every ledger in `STATE_DIR.glob("*.jsonl")` with `mtime >= cutoff`, append just the basename to `name_corpus`.
2. Pass 2 (budgeted): the existing 4MB content scan into `text_corpus`.
3. Search both corpora: `name in name_corpus or name in text_corpus or stem in name_corpus or stem in text_corpus`.

Pass 1 is O(filenames) and tiny — no real budget needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `recent_ledger_text` (or successor) returns BOTH a name corpus and a content corpus, name corpus always-complete
- [ ] `probe_wired_but_cold` checks against both corpora before flagging
- [ ] Regression test: fixture state-dir with N ledgers totaling >4MB and a script whose name appears only in a dropped ledger's name — assert NOT flagged
- [ ] No regression to the 7 other gap classes that share `recent_ledger_text`

## Definition of Done

`bash .flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh --apply --json` no longer flags `lib/fuckup.sh` (or any script whose name appears in any same-mtime-window ledger name) as wired-but-cold.

title: [recovery-doctrine] add agent-mail identity recovery to /flywheel:recovery — reboot loses retired/token state
description:
## Goal
/flywheel:recovery process must include agent-mail identity recovery: detect retired agents + lost tokens after reboot, surface to operator, recover where possible.

## Cost citation (Joshua surfaced 2026-05-03T20:55Z)
"when I rebooted my computer they lost their ID's it sounds like - this should be part of our /flywheel:recovery process"

Today's reboot at 11:56 caused TWO identity failures:
1. FoggyBear (skillos orch) → retired in fleet-mail (cross-orch comms blocked)
2. TurquoiseWolf (mobile-eats worker) → release token lost (5 reservations stuck — recovered manually via force_release)

Both manifested as "session looks idle" symptoms — operator could not have identified the cause from the surface presentation.

## Mechanism

Add to /flywheel:recovery skill:

### Step N — Agent-mail identity audit (post-reboot or session-start)
1. List all known identities from ~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail-tokens/*.json
2. For each: `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__whois <name>` — check status (active/retired/missing)
3. For each retired-but-our-identity: surface as P0 recovery item
4. For each token file present but agent missing: surface as identity-loss event
5. For pending file_reservations >24h held by us: surface candidates for force_release

### Cross-orch boundary preserved
- We can detect retired status of ANY agent
- We can ONLY unretire agents whose token we hold
- For peer-orch retired agents: surface to Joshua, do not auto-unretire (substrate enforces this — needs agent's own token)
- For our own retired identities: auto-unretire on detection

## Acceptance gates
1. /flywheel:recovery skill (or new Step) gains agent-mail-identity-audit
2. Detects FoggyBear retired (current state) — surfaces as cross-orch recovery item
3. Detects no LavenderGlen/RubyCreek token gaps (current state — bpd just shipped)
4. Test: simulate retired LavenderGlen → auto-unretire (we hold token)
5. Wired into /flywheel:status (visible on every status check) AND /flywheel:respawn (catches at recovery time)

## Related to today's session
- bpd (closed) — RubyCreek token surface for L61
- TurquoiseWolf force_release (closed via courtesy dispatch this tick)
- 6krl (skillos-substrate-broken) — sibling diagnosis
- L58 transcript-exposure fuckup logged (lavenderglen.json read into transcript)

## Out of scope
- Modifying agent-mail core (Jeff's repo)
- Auto-unretiring peer-orch identities (boundary)
- Token rotation automation (separate)

## DOD
COMMIT must include `[recovery-doctrine-identity]`. Skill update visible in /flywheel:recovery.
close_reason: Implemented agent-mail identity audit for /flywheel:recovery, /flywheel:status, and /flywheel:respawn. Tests 8/8: executable JSON helper, FoggyBear peer detection, RubyCreek/LavenderGlen healthy, synthetic owned auto-unretire, status/recovery/respawn docs, INCIDENTS entry, peer boundary preserved. AUTONOMY: autonomous.

title: [orch-no-punt-trauma] L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT mechanical gate (next actionable runs same tick)
description:
## Trauma class
`orch-punt-to-next-tick-instead-of-next-actionable`

## Symptom (2026-05-03T22:28Z mobile-eats:1)
Orch tick concluded "DISPATCH could not proceed; no ready work; Next phase should be BEADS" — and then stopped. Pane went idle waiting for next launchd tick to fire instead of executing the BEADS phase immediately.

This is THE SAME SHAPE as orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation (logged 22:15Z this session): orchestrator identifies the next actionable thing AND THEN PUNTS instead of doing it.

## Why this is the load-bearing pattern
Today (2026-05-03) we logged 3+ instances of this archetype:
1. `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation` — validated worker DONE → forwarded summary → asked "what next" instead of running validation
2. `orch-punt-to-next-tick-instead-of-next-actionable` — identified "next phase should be BEADS" → idle instead of running BEADS
3. (Earlier) substrate-watchtower-must-be-wired — documented validation rule → didn't wire as probe

In each case: orchestrator KNOWS what to do next. Orchestrator DOES NOT DO IT. That's the trauma class.

## Companion existing rules
- `feedback_orchestrator_must_dispatch` — orchestrator decides; don't ask
- `feedback_flywheel_never_idles` — when ready beads run out, go hunting (PLANS, audits, gap-hunt)
- `feedback_data_guides_decisions_not_human_judgment` — auto-execute, no meat-puppet gate
- `feedback_orch_paralysis_recurring` — surfacing decision IS the failure
- `feedback_orchestrator_validates_callbacks` — validate before forwarding

This bead names the umbrella under all of them: **whenever orchestrator identifies what to do next, orchestrator does it next, not next-tick.**

## Acceptance gates
1. **Tick driver enhancement** — when DISPATCH phase concludes with `no ready work` AND `next_phase=BEADS`, driver MUST chain into BEADS phase same tick, not return to launchd
2. **L-rule landing** — promote to L70 (or next available): `ORCH-NO-PUNT (next actionable runs same tick, not next tick)`
3. **Doctor signal** — `ticks_punted_count` = number of ticks where conclusion identified next phase but did not chain
4. **/flywheel:tick wrapper** — chain phase transitions when callback says next_phase=X and current tick has capacity for X
5. **Worker dispatch packet** — include "if your work concludes with `next_phase=Y`, attempt Y immediately within your callback if capacity permits, otherwise file the chain reason"
6. **mobile-eats specific** — agent-mail to mobile-eats orch (TurquoiseWolf or current identity) noting the observation; cross-orch coordination, not cross-orch dispatch

## Fold into in-flight plan
This is in scope for flywheel-bi76 (validate-everything plan). Phase 1 Lane A's "Surface taxonomy" should include "tick phase chaining" as a surface; Section 3 should rate it high-criticality.

## Cost (today)
- mobile-eats idle for ~unknown minutes when BEADS work was identified and ready to execute
- flywheel orch (me) skipped callback validation 1× this session, caught only because Joshua flagged
- Both could have been auto-handled by the same chain-the-next-phase rule

## Out of scope (this bead)
- Implementing the fix (defer to plan Phase 4 beads)
- Changing mobile-eats files (cross-session boundary)

title: [l70-doctor-jsonl-hardening] ticks_punted probe must tolerate malformed dispatch-log rows
description:
## Problem

The L70 doctor signal is wired, but the live `ticks-punted-probe.sh --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json` path fails when `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` contains a non-JSON plaintext row.

Current evidence:

- `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl:956` contains `[2026-05-04T03:41:49Z] dispatched workforce-lane-a to flywheel:p4`
- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-ticks-punted-probe.json` shows the probe aborted with `jq: parse error: Invalid numeric literal at line 956, column 15`
- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-doctor-l70.json` shows doctor still exposes `ticks_punted_count=0`, but `l70_chain_state.warning=ticks_punted_probe_invalid_json`

## Why This Matters

`ticks_punted_count` is the L70 mechanical enforcement surface. A single malformed historical dispatch-log row should not degrade the entire doctor signal.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `.flywheel/scripts/ticks-punted-probe.sh --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json` succeeds even if `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` contains malformed historical rows.
2. Probe output includes a machine-readable malformed-row count and line references, or writes those rows to a quarantine evidence path.
3. `flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json` no longer reports `l70_chain_state.warning=ticks_punted_probe_invalid_json` solely because of one malformed row.
4. A regression fixture covers mixed JSONL plus plaintext dispatch-log rows.
5. The probe still fails when all rows are unreadable or when the log file itself cannot be read.

## Doctrine

- L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT
- L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE
- feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface

## Parent

Blocks closure proof for `flywheel-7lby`.

title: [orch-no-punt-chain-fixture-hardening] synthetic tick must survive missing optional helpers
description:
## Problem

`tests/orch-no-punt-chain.sh` does reach the intended L70 chain decision in the synthetic repo, but the full test fails later when `.flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick` serializes optional helper output into the final dispatch-log JSON.

Current evidence:

- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-orch-no-punt-chain-x.err`
- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-driver-debug.err`
- `/tmp/orch-no-punt-debug.35126/repo/.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl`

The synthetic run selected `PHASE=BEADS`, `PHASE_REASON=plan_or_docs_without_beads`, and an L70 chain decision with `chain_required=true` and `chained=true`. The failure occurs later when `--argjson frozen_detector` receives invalid or multi-object text after optional pre-tick helper fallback handling.

## Why This Matters

The core L70 branch appears implemented, but the acceptance fixture cannot prove it end-to-end. Missing optional helpers in a synthetic repo should produce one degraded JSON object, not poison the final receipt write.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash tests/orch-no-punt-chain.sh` passes in a synthetic repo without copying every optional helper script.
2. `.flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick` normalizes optional pre-tick helper output to a single JSON object before passing it through any `jq --argjson` call.
3. Missing `frozen-pane-detector.sh` yields one structured degraded/warn object.
4. The final synthetic dispatch-log row remains valid JSON.
5. The test asserts the L70 `DISPATCH -> BEADS` same-tick chain path and the callback `next_phase` chain path separately.

## Doctrine

- L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT
- L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE
- feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface

## Parent

Blocks fixture proof for `flywheel-7lby`.
close_reason: did=2/2 test_passes_without_optional_helpers=true output_normalized_before_argjson=true tests_passing=true commits=1 — both 7lby fixture-hardening gates met; unblocks 8kvt parent rework

title: [data-backed-deferral-core] implement predicate and question-shape detector
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-bbts
Plan predecessor: flywheel-28v

## Goal

Implement the reusable data-backed-deferral predicate and question-shape detector so the flywheel tick can decide when telemetry already implies action and a Joshua-facing question is a violation.

## Scope

- Add or complete `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals <file> --json` and/or the backing helper under `.flywheel/scripts/`.
- Parse the seven signal families from plan lines 34-48: ntm health, ready beads, recent tick history, doctor, PageRank, pane scrollback, and dispatch log.
- Implement the base condition and `data_alignment_score >= 3` predicate from plan lines 50-85.
- Implement question-shape detection against only the last five non-empty lines using the regex set from plan lines 210-236.
- Implement safe-question exceptions for headings, quoted user questions followed by action, and blocked callbacks with `human_question`.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash tests/data-backed-deferral-guard.sh` passes for existing fixtures.
2. `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals tests/fixtures/data-backed-deferral/row185-catch.json --json | jq -e '.violation == true and .code == "orch_question_when_data_clear" and .suggested_action != null'` passes.
3. `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals tests/fixtures/data-backed-deferral/ntm116-catch.json --json | jq -e '.violation == true and (.signals_aligned | length >= 3)'` passes.
4. `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals tests/fixtures/data-backed-deferral/missing-datum-allow.json --json | jq -e '.violation == false and .override_reason == "evidence_missing"'` passes.
5. A regex fixture or test assertion covers all 11 minimum question-shape patterns from the plan and proves the broad final-line question rule does not scan entire documentation bodies.
6. `rg -n "data_alignment_score|orch_question_when_data_clear|DATA_BACKED_DEFERRAL_DETECTED" ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop .flywheel/scripts tests` shows the shipped predicate and test coverage.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: fixture tests prove positive catches, ambiguity allows, and regex boundaries.
- DOCUMENTED: help output or inline command description names the plan path and predicate inputs.
- SURFACED: command JSON includes stable `violation`, `code`, `signals_aligned`, `suggested_action`, and `override_reason` fields for downstream doctor/tick consumers.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L66 reinforcement via plan lines 20 and 32.
- AGENTS.md L70, L71, L80 for chain-forward, validate-and-redispatch, and DID/DIDNT/GAPS receipt discipline.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No hard pre-output hook; Option A remains future-only until a real interception substrate exists.
- No source mutation outside the flywheel repo.
- No dispatch-template changes; those belong to the dispatch lint bead.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented data-backed-deferral predicate/check command; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-core]`.
close_reason: field-name aligned: emit code=orch_question_when_data_clear per bead spec; tests=5/5 passed; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-core]

title: [data-backed-deferral-doctor] surface saves and violations in doctor JSON
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-bbts
Plan predecessor: flywheel-28v

## Goal

Surface data-backed-deferral state in doctor/tick receipts so the system can track saves, violations, and allowed questions over time.

## Scope

- Add a doctor/tick JSON section for data-backed-deferral counts.
- Count recent saves from `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-saves.jsonl`.
- Count recent overrides from `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-overrides.jsonl`.
- Report current tick violations and last suggested action without echoing sensitive pane text beyond necessary redacted/summary fields.
- Preserve existing doctor JSON compatibility.

1. `flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json | jq -e '.data_backed_deferral'` passes.
2. Doctor JSON exposes numeric `saves_count`, `overrides_count`, and `violations_count` fields.

6. The field does not echo raw secrets, pane scrollback, or full draft output; tests or code review proof show only counts and redacted summaries.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: doctor tests cover empty files and fixture rows.
- DOCUMENTED: README or canonical paths identifies the doctor field and receipt files.
- SURFACED: `flywheel-loop doctor --json` provides a stable machine-readable signal.

title: [doctrine] callback-evidence-discrepancy trauma class — worker advertises evidence path but doesnt write it
description:
## Source
skillos:1 caught flywheel-kvt8v callback (CloudyMill) advertised evidence=.flywheel/receipts/flywheel-kvt8v/evidence.md but that path does not exist on disk. Only history JSONs landed (skillos 611KB/1330 entries, mobile-eats 980KB, alpsinsurance 0B empty).

Pattern: worker callback evidence field claims a file path; path is not materialized; close still proceeds because validate-callback doesn't probe disk for the path.

## Why this matters
- Callback evidence is a CONTRACT, not a label
- Joshua + skillos rely on evidence.md files for re-audit and verification
- Silent path-non-existence creates trust drift
- skillos called this out by ACTUALLY checking disk — flywheel-side validate-callback should do same

## Acceptance gates
AG1: Audit the last 50 closed beads — count how many advertised evidence paths that don't exist on disk. Quantify drift.
AG2: Patch validate-callback.py: when callback contains `evidence=<path>` field, validate the path exists. Reject close if missing (with override flag for path-is-tmp-dir cases).
AG3: Patch dispatch-template.md callback contract: explicitly require evidence file written BEFORE br close, not advertised then forgotten.
AG4: Audit kvt8v specifically — is the work actually done despite missing evidence file?
AG5: Cross-link with worker-close-requires-git-commit (L143-class) — same family of close-discipline gaps.

## Cross-references
- skillos:1 callback-evidence-discrepancy ACK 2026-05-09
- L143 WORKER-CLOSE-REQUIRES-GIT-COMMIT
- Phase 4 evidence_redacted contract (flywheel-dwavb)
close_reason: Implementation complete (5/5 ACs per pane 2 worker). validate-callback.py + tests + dispatch-template all updated for disk-check on advertised evidence paths. Commits 958aaae + 9ca6461. evidence_redacted=yes (Phase 4 contract self-applied). Close was blocked on .beads contention with pane 3 gb54d.1. Closing from orch per L70.

title: [ntm-research] compare lock/locks/unlock with MCP Agent Mail reservations
description:

title: [ntm-audit] verify 86 USE-row callsites — close the audit-claim-vs-reality gap
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07
joshua_directive=2026-05-07T~22Z "audit and address ALL NTM surfaces"

## Why
NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md classifies 108 surfaces: 86 USE / 8 ISSUE / 8 WRAP / 6 EXCLUDED. The 38 wire-in beads addressed the gap rows; the 86 USE rows were classified "already wired or queued" as a CLAIM, not verified by callsite count. This is the same evidence-pack discipline x6ok8 just shipped for callbacks: classifications without proof are upper-bound signals.

The /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07/06-GAP-AUDIT-SPREADSHEET.md from earlier today partially addressed this but flagged unverified-USE rows without resolving. This bead closes that audit gap.

## Acceptance
1. For each of 86 USE rows in NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md, run K=10 socraticode + grep against /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel for actual ntm <surface> callsites
2. Three-way classify each:
   - VERIFIED-USE: ≥3 callsites in flywheel scripts (real wire-in)
   - CLAIMED-USE-NOT-WIRED: 0 callsites in flywheel (classification was aspirational)
   - LATENT-USE: 1-2 callsites (wired-but-thin; flag for monitoring)
3. For each CLAIMED-USE-NOT-WIRED row, propose follow-up bead: either wire it or reclassify
4. For each LATENT-USE row, document the single use-case + why it doesn't yet need fleet-wide wiring
5. Output: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07/11-USE-ROW-VERIFICATION.md`
6. Cross-update NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md with verification status column (added per row)
7. Compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract

## Hard rules
- socraticode K≥10 per surface (NEVER fewer; the inventory classification was already prose-driven, this audit must be data-driven)
- callsite counts measured via `grep -rn "ntm <surface>" /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/` + socraticode codebase_search
- NO patches to scripts; this is audit-only
- NO new beads created here; recommendations only (orchestrator files them)
- No L107 conflicts: write to 11-* path only after lsof check

## Cross-refs
- NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md (the canonical 108-row classification)
- 06-GAP-AUDIT-SPREADSHEET.md (today's earlier partial audit, surfaced this gap)
- L126 evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade (mirror discipline: claims need evidence)
close_reason: USE-row verification complete: 86 rows audited with 860 Socraticode searches, 255 executable/script/test callsites measured; report at .flywheel/plans/ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07/11-USE-ROW-VERIFICATION.md; inventory patch intentionally not applied per dispatch audit-only rule; compliance_pack_path=null

title: [autoloop-p3-controlled-executor] add whitelisted tiny actions and no-op ladder
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Implement Phase 3 controlled execution expansion: `auto_spawn=true` for score
`>=100` and tiny whitelisted local actions.

## Required Behavior

- Keep `auto_spawn=false` by default until preceding Phase 2/3 gates are green.
- Add config-gated `auto_spawn=true` for score `>=100`.
- Add tiny action executor with only the plan's whitelisted actions:
  `run_baseline_validation` and `summarize_dirty_worktree`.
- Enforce cost classes L0-L4 and require explicit Joshua approval for L4.
- Implement no-op escalation ladder: 2 warn, 3 force replan artifact, 4 halt.
- Implement the 10-step "nothing to do" decision tree for executor selection.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Config default keeps `auto_spawn=false`.
2. Score `>=100` fixture with `auto_spawn=true` produces an auto-spawn plan but
   never exceeds one action per run.
3. Non-whitelisted action fixture fails closed with reason code.
4. L4 action fixture requires explicit approval and does not execute.
5. No-op streak fixtures produce warn, force-replan artifact, and halt states at
   streaks 2, 3, and 4.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented Phase 3 controlled executor; tests=<cmd>; whitelisted_actions=<list>`.

## Out Of Scope

Arbitrary source edits, deploy/publish actions, model API calls from launchd, or
multi-worker swarms without a separate bead/mission lane.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: whitelist, cost-class, no-op, and auto-spawn fixtures.
- DOCUMENTED: allowed actions and approval rules are documented.
- SURFACED: executor outcomes appear in status/doctor/digest surfaces.
close_reason: Phase 3 controlled executor shipped: lib/autoloop-executor.sh + bin/flywheel-autoloop extension + 8 tests covering default no-spawn, high-score one-action cap, non-whitelist fail-closed, L4 approval-required, 3 no-op streak states (warn/force-replan/halt), 10-step decision tree. whitelist_count=2 (run_baseline_validation, summarize_dirty_worktree), cost_class_levels=5 (L0-L4). socraticode K=5, 2 commits.

title: /flywheel:recover-pane <session> <pane> — recovery ladder slash command
description:
## Goal
Wrap the recovery ladder (probe → interrupt → respawn+relaunch) in a single slash command operators can invoke when a pane is detected as FROZEN_SUSPECT.

## Recovery ladder (verified today on skillos:0.1)
1. PROBE: 'ntm activity <session> --json' → confirm state=THINKING|GENERATING AND velocity=0 AND duration > 120s
2. CAPTURE: tmux capture-pane -t <session>:0.<pane> -p -S -3000 > /tmp/<session>_pane<N>_pre_recover_<ts>.txt
3. SOFT: tmux send-keys -t <session>:0.<pane> Escape (try first; codex spinner says 'esc to interrupt')
4. INTERRUPT: tmux send-keys -t <session>:0.<pane> C-c (if Escape fails)
5. RE-PROBE after 5s: if still THINKING+velocity=0, escalate
6. RESPAWN: ntm respawn <session> --panes=<pane> --force (kills the agent, drops to shell)
7. RELAUNCH: tmux send-keys -t <session>:0.<pane> '<agent-launch-command>' Enter
   - Codex default: codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m gpt-5.5
   - cc default: read from ~/.config/ntm/config.toml [agents.commands.claude]
8. VERIFY: ntm activity --json shows state=WAITING within 10s

## Acceptance criteria
- /flywheel:recover-pane <session> <pane> exists and prints each ladder step + outcome
- Ladder halts on first success
- Pane history captured before any destructive step
- Agent type detected via ntm health (cc vs cod) → correct relaunch command chosen
- Codex relaunch uses --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox + correct model from ntm config defaults
- After recover, runs ntm activity --json one more time and reports new state
- Logs each invocation to .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl with action=recover_pane

## Cross-references
- Sister bead flywheel-terc (frozen-spinner detector that triggers this command)
- Today's recovery proof: skillos:0.1 wedged 4m+ on 'Working' spinner with queued '› Summarize recent commits' — recovered via respawn+relaunch
- Memory: feedback_pane_state_ntm_health.md (correction: ntm activity is primary)
close_reason: /flywheel:recover-pane slash command spec done. /tmp/recov_pane_command_design.md + /tmp/recov_pane_command.md (the spec). 8-step ladder, 6 modes, 5 tests, canonical-cli-scoping all 6 PASS, ladder=yes.

title: [learn-bead-quality-mining] worker did/didnt/gaps callback contract + auto-gap-bead creation + audit mining baked into /flywheel:learn
description:
[learn-bead-quality-mining] worker did/didnt/gaps callback contract + auto-gap-bead creation + audit mining baked into /flywheel:learn

## Trauma class
closed-bead-quality-drift — workers ship "DONE evidence=... tests=PASS" but acceptance gates not validated; today flywheel-15dg closed with 66GB corpus RED (Phase 4 prod compaction never ran), flywheel-2uin closed with AG6 NOT_WIRED, flywheel-useh stayed P0 OPEN while 4 newer beads filed on top. Joshua flagged: "i'm still not seeing much energy placed into flagging and beading large scripts for breakup", "we cannot let unfinished work sit there", "this needs to be a repeatable mining process baked into our systems - /flywheel:learn".

## Meadows leverage
- #5 RULES — change closure rule from "worker says DONE" to "worker says DID/DIDNT/GAPS, gaps become beads, then close"
- #6 INFORMATION FLOWS — promote the latent comparison "AG list vs evidence artifacts" into a doctor signal that fires every tick

## Goal
Three-part discipline change:

### Part A: Worker callback contract (NEW)
Every worker `/flywheel:worker-tick` callback MUST include:
- DONE: list of completed acceptance gates with evidence path
- DIDNT: list of acceptance gates not addressed + reason (out_of_scope|deferred|blocked|misread)
- GAPS: list of newly-discovered work items not in original AG list
- For each entry in DIDNT and GAPS, worker creates a fix-bead via `br create` BEFORE sending callback (and includes those bead IDs in the callback envelope)

Updated callback envelope shape:
```
DONE <bead-id> task_id=<id>
  did=<n_completed>/<n_total_ags>
  didnt=<bead-id-list>          # gap-beads worker filed for unaddressed AGs
  gaps=<bead-id-list>            # gap-beads worker filed for newly-discovered work
  evidence=<path>
  tests=PASS|FAIL|SKIPPED
  callback_delivery_verified=true
```

### Part B: /flywheel:learn --bead-quality-mining mode
New mode runs every tick. For each closed bead in last 48h:
1. Parse "Acceptance gates" / "AG1..AGn" from bead description
2. For each AG, derive expected artifact (file path, doctor signal name, test name) using regex + skill cross-ref
3. Probe: artifact exists? test passes? doctor signal present?
4. If any AG unverified → file fix-bead `<orig-id>.audit-gap` automatically (P0 if orig was P0, else P1)
5. Mark orig with audit_status (full|partial|gap_pending) via br update --notes
6. doctor signal `closed_bead_audit_pending_count` → fail readiness if >2

### Part C: Doctor signal closed_bead_audit_status
- closed_bead_audit_pending_count: count of recently-closed beads with no audit verdict yet
- closed_bead_audit_gap_count: count of closed beads where audit found unverified AGs
- audit_gap_top_classes: top trauma classes (e.g. "doctor_signal_not_wired", "phase_b_unfinished", "tests_skipped")

## Acceptance gates

AG1: ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md updated with did/didnt/gaps contract — workers MUST include all 3 sections in callback
AG2: ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/worker-tick.md (or skill SKILL.md) updated with worker self-audit checklist:
  - "Before sending callback, for each AG in bead description, write 1-line evidence."
  - "For unaddressed AGs, file fix-bead via br create with parent_bead=<orig-id>"
  - "For newly-discovered work, file gap-bead via br create with parent_bead=<orig-id> tag=gap"
AG3: .flywheel/scripts/bead-quality-mining.sh exists — runs over last 48h closures, parses AGs, verifies, files fix-beads
AG4: doctor signals wired: closed_bead_audit_pending_count, closed_bead_audit_gap_count, audit_gap_top_classes
AG5: tests/bead-quality-mining.sh covers fixtures: closure-with-full-evidence (audit pass) / closure-with-missing-AG (auto-files fix-bead) / closure-with-doctor-not-wired (auto-files fix-bead) / closure-with-tests-skipped (auto-files fix-bead) / worker-already-filed-gaps (idempotent — doesn't double-file)
AG6: /flywheel:learn skill SKILL.md gains --bead-quality-mining flag + cron-on-tick documentation
AG7: orchestrator-validation-discipline skill cross-references new contract
AG8: AGENTS.md L## entry — "L## CLOSED-BEAD-AUDIT-MINING" sibling of L71 (validate-and-redispatch)
AG9: ft04 propagation — sync-canonical-doctrine.sh covers new probe + skill update fleet-wide
AG10: BACK-MINE the last 48h: run bead-quality-mining.sh against current closed beads, file all gap-beads found (this proves the mining works AND clears today's specific debt)

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests pass; back-mine produces N gap-beads where N matches manual audit count
- DOCUMENTED: SKILL.md + AGENTS.md L## + dispatch-template + worker-tick + canonical-paths
- SURFACED: 3 doctor signals + flywheel:status reference + auto-bead promotion

## Skills cited
- orchestrator-validation-discipline (primary extension)
- testing-conformance-harnesses (4-fixture discipline)
- flywheel-doctor-author (3 doctor signals)
- canonical-cli-scoping (--bead-quality-mining flag pattern)
- gate-truth-separation (audit verdict ≠ closure)

## Why P0
- Compounding debt: every tick that closes beads without audit adds drift
- Joshua-side detection delay = days; mining surfaces it within minutes
- Today's flywheel-useh / flywheel-15dg-compaction / flywheel-2uin gaps would have been auto-flagged by this system
- Companion to L70 (no-punt) and L71 (validate-and-redispatch) — completes the validation envelope

## Memory siblings
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_validate_redispatch_foundational_discipline.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_orchestrator_validates_callbacks.md

## Priority: P0
## Estimate: 4 dispatches (~10 worker-min)
notes: audit_status=partial; did=10/10 reported but AG9 (AGENTS.md L-rule) NOT WIRED — gap-bead 7yic.1 carries forward. Back-mine produced 22 gap-beads on prior closures: cwov.1, g9mi.1, 2zsj.1, o7dq.1-3, f589.1-2, ft04.1, m5kg.1-2, susm.1, vso8.1-2, dw5w.1-2, kscr.1-2, hf58.1, zgo3.1, syef.1-2. doctor signals working (closed_bead_audit_pending_count=106, closed_bead_audit_gap_count=21, top_class=doctor_signal_not_wired x8). Mining system functional and self-catching.

title: [autoloop-p2-dispatch] add bounded ntm dispatch and callback reaping
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Implement Phase 2 `dispatch` subcommand for bounded autonomous worker dispatch.

## Required Behavior

- Pick top queue item with score `>=80`.
- Write task packet to `/tmp/flywheel-autoloop-<id>.md`.
- Send via `ntm send` using canonical NTM transport only.
- Append to dispatch log with repo, bead/task, score, packet path, pane/session,
  and idempotency key.
- Enforce `max_autonomous_ticks_per_run=1`, `auto_spawn=false`, and budget gates.
- Require closeout receipt validation for dispatched agents.
- Add callback reaping for pending autoloop dispatches.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `flywheel-autoloop dispatch --dry-run --json` reports intended queue item and
   packet path without sending.
2. Score `<80` fixture does not dispatch and writes a cooldown/skip receipt.
3. Score `>=80` fixture emits a task packet with repo, acceptance gates, and
   callback envelope.
4. Applied dispatch appends a dispatch-log row and uses `ntm send`, not direct
   terminal transport.
5. Callback reaper validates a fixture closeout receipt and marks dispatch
   complete or failed.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented Phase 2 dispatch; tests=<cmd>; dispatch_log=<path>; packet_fixture=<path>`.

## Out Of Scope

Auto-spawn, multi-worker swarms, L4 side effects, external deploy/publish actions.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: dry-run, score threshold, packet, send, and reaper fixtures.
- DOCUMENTED: packet schema and callback contract are stable.
- SURFACED: dispatch-log and reaper outcomes feed doctor/status in sibling bead.
close_reason: Implemented Phase 2 dispatch; tests=set -o pipefail; bash tests/flywheel-autoloop-canonical-cli.sh | tee /tmp/flywheel-8ehk-tests.txt; dispatch_log=/tmp/flywheel-8ehk-acceptance/state/dispatch-log.jsonl; packet_fixture=/tmp/flywheel-autoloop-flywheel-8ehk-packet-fixture.md

title: [stale-in-progress-reaper] launchd-driven self-org closure of in_progress beads with zero activity 7+ days
description:
Donella triage 2026-05-10 surfaced invisible stock: 64 in_progress beads accumulated since May 4 planning burst with zero activity. The system has no leverage-point-#4 self-organization mechanism to close them.

Goal: auto-close any in_progress bead with zero activity (no callback, no comment, no update) for >=7 days. Closes the invisible drag class permanently.

Acceptance:
AG1: Author .flywheel/scripts/stale-in-progress-reaper.sh that:
  - Queries .beads/beads.db for status='in_progress' OR status='open' with updated_at >= 7 days ago AND no recent dispatch_log activity
  - For each candidate, applies close-reason: 'auto-stale-cleanup-triggered: bead in_progress with zero callback activity for >=7 days. If still load-bearing, file as fresh bead with current scope. Auto-cleanup keeps the in_progress stock bounded per Donella leverage point #4 (self-organization).'
  - Idempotent: maintains a ledger at ~/.local/state/flywheel/stale-in-progress-reaper.jsonl so reruns don't double-close
  - Dry-run by default; --apply for live closure
  - Canonical-cli-scoping triad (--info, --schema, --examples, --doctor)
AG2: Schema at .flywheel/validation-schema/v1/stale-reaper.v1.schema.json
AG3: Launchd plist at .flywheel/launchd/ai.zeststream.flywheel-stale-reaper.plist running Sunday 09:30 (post-Petal-9, post-skill-discoveries-rollup) so weekly cleanup happens at the same cadence as paradigm-tier review
AG4: e2e smoke test against synthetic in_progress fixtures (some stale, some fresh, verify only stale closed)
AG5: Inaugural manual run on the 64 May-4 stale beads + report what got closed + what got kept (load-bearing patterns: skillos-handoff coordinated, jeff-track-* upstream-tracking, plan-decompose meta-beads)
AG6: Doctrine note at .flywheel/doctrine/stale-in-progress-reaper.md explaining the self-org pattern + carve-outs (don't reap upstream-trackers, don't reap actively-coordinated cross-orch beads)
AG7: Bootstrap launchctl + register via flywheel-watchers

Boundary:
- Read .beads/beads.db, write only via 'br close --force <id>' (never raw SQL mutations)
- Carve-outs: skip beads tagged with 'upstream-tracker', 'cross-orch-active', 'joshua-gated', 'defer-gated' to avoid auto-closing load-bearing async work
- Joshua review of inaugural-run candidates list BEFORE first --apply (Joshua-gate the first execution; subsequent runs auto)

Cross-references:
- Donella leverage point #4 (self-organization)
- Today's emergent-saturation closures (1z65, parity cluster) demonstrated the pattern manually
- Sister to skill-discoveries-aggregator (4s3oy) — both are leverage-point-#4 mechanisms turning latent state into active queryable insight

title: [jeff-local-beads-rust-clone] repair local clone fetch/version-bump path
description:
Parent bead: flywheel-8no1
Upstream issue: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/issues/269
Gap observed during Jeff response absorption.

## Goal

Repair or replace the local `~/Developer/beads_rust` clone so future Jeff response version bumps can use a normal `git pull` / rebuild path instead of bypassing the dirty worktree with `cargo install --git`.

## Evidence

- `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust status --short` showed many modified source and test files plus untracked files.
- `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust fetch origin` failed with `fatal: bad object refs/remotes/origin/dependabot/cargo/rust-minor-f24ad7c61a` and `did not send all necessary objects`.
- For `beads_rust#269`, the worker installed v0.2.5 from Git tag directly to avoid touching or overwriting the dirty clone.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust status --short` is reviewed and every dirty file is classified as user work, generated output, or discardable cache.
2. No destructive reset or checkout is run without explicit Joshua approval.
3. `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust fetch origin --prune` succeeds after the repair strategy.
4. `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust rev-parse origin/main` succeeds.
5. A documented local rebuild command for `br` is available after repair, or the receipt records `use_cargo_install_git_tag=true` as the durable version-bump path.
6. Final receipt records whether the local clone was repaired, replaced, or intentionally left dirty.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: fetch and rev-parse prove the clone can see upstream refs.
- DOCUMENTED: receipt explains the safe local version-bump path.
- SURFACED: future Jeff issue triage sees this bead instead of rediscovering the dirty/fetch-bad clone.

## Out Of Scope

- No upstream push.
- No destructive cleanup without Joshua approval.
- No attempt to preserve or discard unknown dirty work inside this bead without explicit classification.

## Rollback / Dry-Run Posture

Start with read-only classification and `git fetch --dry-run`/fresh clone alternatives. If replacing the clone, move the current directory aside rather than deleting it.

## DOD

Close with reason: `repaired beads_rust local clone version-bump path; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=flywheel-8no1-local-clone`.
close_reason: done

Script: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/.flywheel/scripts/worker-deep-liveness-probe.sh (196 lines, May 6 mtime)

Investigation findings:
- Script EXISTS as functional code; reads from LIVE state files actively written by other surfaces:
  * /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl (last mod TODAY 2026-05-11T02:20)

A. Launchd job (5-min interval) — analogous to com.zeststream.heartbeat-liveness.plist pattern; emits hung-pane signal to ledger
B. Orchestrator tick integration — /flywheel:tick Step 4n adjacent (every tick probes deep-liveness, surfaces hung panes as orch-action-required)

AG2: implement integration; create test fixture exercising stdout-recency classifier

title: [vc-relive] full vc revival — pull Jeff fixes, restart daemon, validate full mapping into vc
description:
