title: [dispatch-v2-wrapper] implement schema v2 canonical dispatch wrapper
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/dispatch-enforcement-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-pnr9
Plan predecessor: flywheel-8i5

## Goal

Upgrade `/flywheel:dispatch` to emit schema v2 dispatch receipts and prove every dispatch was produced by the canonical wrapper.

## Scope

- Patch `/Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/dispatch.md`.
- Add `dispatch_skill_version=flywheel-dispatch/v2`.
- Require topology resolution before choosing worker and callback panes.
- Require `edits_expected=true|false|unknown` and `socraticode_required=true|false` classification.
- Export `FLYWHEEL_DISPATCH_WRAPPER=1` only around the canonical `ntm send`.
- Write schema v2 `dispatch_sent` rows to `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` before send and update with send result.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `rg -n "dispatch_skill_version=flywheel-dispatch/v2|schema_version.*2|FLYWHEEL_DISPATCH_WRAPPER|edits_expected|socraticode_required" /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/dispatch.md` proves the wrapper contract landed.
2. A dry-run or fixture dispatch writes a schema v2 row and `tail -1 .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl | jq -e '.schema_version == 2 and .event == "dispatch_sent" and .dispatch_skill_version == "flywheel-dispatch/v2"'` passes.
3. `tail -1 .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl | jq -e '.topology_resolved_pane and .topology_role and .callback_pane and .task_sha256 and .channel == "ntm"'` passes for the fixture row.
4. The wrapper records `agent_type`, `worker_substrate`, `pane_state_source`, `socraticode_required`, and `file_reservation_required` as non-null schema v2 fields.
5. Existing legacy dispatch-log rows remain readable; no migration deletes or rewrites old rows during normal dispatch.
6. The command path uses absolute `/Users/josh/.local/bin/ntm` for sends and does not introduce a new transport.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: fixture dispatch produces machine-readable schema v2 row.
- DOCUMENTED: `/flywheel:dispatch` docs name schema v2 and classification fields.
- SURFACED: dispatch-log row exposes wrapper version, topology, and task hash for doctor consumers.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L29, L50, L51, L52, L53, L70, L71, L80.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No PreToolUse hook blocking in this bead.
- No callback contract validation beyond writing dispatch rows.
- No retroactive backfill of old dispatch-log rows.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented schema v2 canonical dispatch wrapper; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[dispatch-v2-wrapper]`.

title: [codex-PATH-missing] worker panes lack ~/.local/bin; jeff substrate invisible
description:
## Symptom (2026-05-03 22:08Z)
Live codex pane (flywheel:3) probe returned:
`DCG_PROBE_RESULT: PATH_MISSING; /Users/josh/.local/bin/dcg v0.5.1 (which dcg failed)`

Binary exists on disk + works via absolute path. `which dcg` fails in codex
pane shell = ~/.local/bin not in codex's PATH.

## Implications
Every Jeff substrate binary the agent invokes by bare name fails silently in
codex panes: dcg, br, ntm, cm, jsm. This means:
- Codex agents writing "dcg explain ..." in dispatch templates → command not found
- Codex agents calling `br create` → command not found
- Tool calls that shell out to these tools fail in ways the agent may not surface

## Connection to flywheel-delp (fleet-death-rca)
Strong candidate. Pattern of dead panes = clean exit after tool call success.
Hypothesis: agent shells out → tool not found → MCP/codex layer treats some
class of exec failures as fatal → process exits clean.

## Truth checks needed before claiming RCA
1. Capture exit reason for one death with stderr redirected (already in flywheel-delp scope)
2. Verify orchestrator panes (flywheel:1, flywheel:2) DO have ~/.local/bin in
   PATH while worker panes do NOT — that explains differential survival
3. Find where codex panes' PATH gets stripped — likely in launchd plist
   for ai.zeststream.flywheel-flywheel-loop or in the codex launch wrapper

## Acceptance
- ~/.local/bin in PATH for ALL codex panes, orchestrator AND workers
- Verified by ntm send probe responding `which dcg` → /Users/josh/.local/bin/dcg
- launchd plists audited; whichever wrapper strips PATH is fixed

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The launchd or plist artifact named in `flywheel-orx1` validates with `plutil -lint` or an equivalent dry-run.
AG2: A restart or health probe proves the daemon behavior described in this bead.
AG3: The close receipt names the plist path, validation command, and rollback posture.
notes:
# ORX1 H1 vs H3 Diagnosis

## Verdict

`h=H1`

The earlier `PATH_MISSING` probe came from a different shell context inside the same Codex runtime: Codex tool execution uses non-interactive login zsh (`/bin/zsh -lc`), and `/Users/josh/.zprofile` reset PATH in that context. That made `which dcg` fail even though NTM-launched Codex panes could have the Jeff substrate visible in their pane shell.

`H2` is also true after intervention: PATH was fixed between probes by the `.zprofile`, Codex wrapper, NTM template, and launch PATH-order changes. It is not the primary explanation for why the original observation contradicted pane parity.

`H3` is not supported by the current evidence.

## Evidence

- Pre-fix Codex tool probe in flywheel:3 returned `which dcg` failure while `/Users/josh/.local/bin/dcg --version` returned `0.5.1`.
- `/Users/josh/.zprofile` reset PATH to `/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin`, ran `path_helper`, and did not restore `/Users/josh/.local/bin` until the ORX1 fix added:
  `export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"`.
- The mismatched context was reproducible before the fix: a plain zsh command with PATH injected could resolve `dcg`, while a login zsh command (`/bin/zsh -lc`) lost it through `.zprofile`.
- NTM-launched Codex process evidence showed Codex node processes launched from `/Users/josh/.cargo/bin/codex` with user-bin PATH present in the parent environment. That points away from a universal NTM/Codex pane spawn strip.
- Current parity validation from both pane 1 and pane 3 resolves all five binaries:
  `dcg`, `br`, `ntm`, `cm`, `jsm`.
- `e493cca` frozen-pane-detector v2 added `.flywheel/scripts/frozen-pane-detector.sh`; its recovery command is:
  `CODEX_CMD="${FROZEN_PANE_CODEX_CMD:-codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox}"`.
  It does not modify PATH or introduce a separate stripped environment. It relies on whichever pane shell PATH resolves bare `codex`.

## Recommendation

Downgrade `flywheel-orx1` from P0 to P2 and reclassify it as an `orchestrator-probe-discipline` / login-shell bootstrap hygiene issue.

Keep `flywheel-delp` open as fleet-death RCA. ORX1 restored Codex tool-shell substrate visibility and fixed PATH order, but the evidence no longer supports treating ORX1 as the P0 root cause for fleet death.

Do not close `flywheel-orx1` yet; leave it open for orchestrator decision after this refinement.
close_reason: done

description:

The bead-isolation plan exists at .flywheel/PLANS/bead-isolation-fix-2026-04-30.md as 4 phases × 8 fixes (FM-1 through FM-8) with 20-26h estimate. ZERO beads exist for it. Doctor still reports leakage_count=23. Plan-to-bead arc broke.

## Why this matters
- Doctor leakage symptom is downstream of this missing plan-to-bead conversion
- 23-leakage gets worse with every cross-project bead operation
- Fix lives in plan-space (converged 2026-04-30) but never reached bead-space
- Self-organization gap: doctor signals fail, no auto-promotion to bead

## Steps
1. Read .flywheel/PLANS/bead-isolation-fix-2026-04-30.md fully
2. Create one bead per phase (Phase 1 stop-the-bleed, Phase 2 schema, Phase 3 source_repo SQL, Phase 4 testing/regression)
3. Add fix-level subtasks where needed (FM-1, FM-5, FM-6 in Phase 1; FM-8 in Phase 2; etc)
4. Wire dependencies (Phase N depends on Phase N-1)
5. Tag every bead [bead-isolation-P<N>] for filtering
6. Validate: br dep cycles --json shows 0
7. Reference plan in each bead description

## Acceptance gates
1. >=4 beads created (one per phase, more if subtask-level)
2. Dependency DAG validated (0 cycles)
3. Doctor leakage_count expected to drop to <5 once Phase 1 ships (track baseline now)
4. Each bead body cites the plan synthesis decision (DP1-DP10) it implements

## Out of scope
- Implementing the fixes (separate dispatches per bead)
- Touching beads_rust source (that's Phase 3 work, separate bead)

## Plan reference
.flywheel/PLANS/bead-isolation-fix-2026-04-30.md (converged 2026-04-30 from Opus 4.6 + Sonnet 4.6 + Haiku 4.5 triangulation)

## DOD
COMMIT must include `[bead-isolation-decompose]`. Beads filed; not dispatched yet.

title: [doctrine-prop] flywheel:doctrine-sync — version-stamped pull from canonical AGENTS template
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07
joshua_directive=2026-05-07T~21Z "stamp all of this into the rest of the fleet"

## Why
Today shipped: L125 (env-file-sealed-substrate), L126 (evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade), close-handler/dispatch-template/worker-tick/worker-orchestration/dispatch-tool-contracts/orchestrator-validation-discipline rewrites, fuckup-heuristics promotion, jeff-issue-chain v1.1, RESHAPED-OUR-SCOPE 5th shape. All landed in `flywheel/` + canonical surfaces. **Rest of fleet (mobile-eats, alpsinsurance, zesttube, skillos, picoz, cfs) does NOT have these yet.**

The canonical-meta-rules-sync three-surface watchdog already exists for *flywheel's own* root/canonical/template parity. What's missing: the cross-repo pull mechanism so peer fleet repos pick up template updates.

## Acceptance
1. `~/.local/bin/flywheel-doctrine-sync` (or `.flywheel/scripts/doctrine-sync.sh` + symlink) — diffs target repo's AGENTS.md against `templates/flywheel-install/AGENTS.md` from flywheel HEAD; surfaces missing L-rules; --apply mode appends them with provenance citation
2. Doctrine version stamp in every fleet repo's `.flywheel/STATE.json` — `doctrine_version: "2026-05-07.L126"` (semver-like: date + highest-L-rule)
3. Doctor probe `doctrine_version_current` lands in `flywheel doctor` — fails if behind template
4. flywheel:onboard checklist gains `doctrine-sync` step (so new onboards never start stale)
5. Per-repo tick reads doctrine-version + auto-pulls when behind (Donella leverage point #5: continuous-sync invariant)
6. Agent Mail broadcast template at `templates/peer-orch-broadcasts/doctrine-update-template.md` for one-shot announcements
7. Run against the 6 fleet sample repos as a verify pass; emit compliance pack per repo

## Hard rules
- dry-run is the default; --apply requires --idempotency-key (mirrors storage-prune.sh, session-residue-prune.sh contract)
- refuses to write to a repo without `.flywheel/` (not flywheel-onboarded)
- never overwrites local L-rules; only appends missing ones with `# Pulled from flywheel/templates/flywheel-install/AGENTS.md@<sha>` provenance
- per-repo CASCADE not BULK — script runs against ONE repo at a time, fails-closed if anything mismatches schema
- compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract (no four-lens emission)

## Cross-refs
- canonical-meta-rules-sync skill (existing flywheel-internal three-surface watchdog)
- /flywheel:onboard (per-repo install)
- /flywheel:tick step 1a awareness check (per-repo doctrine reading)
- L126 evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade (the load-bearing rule we're propagating)
- session-residue-prune.sh (safety contract template)
close_reason: done

title: [jeff-track-ntm-114-followup] robot activity still diverges from work/health gate
description:
## Goal
Track the remaining ntm robot-surface convergence failure after #114: `--robot-activity` reports live Codex panes as `THINKING` while `--robot-is-working` and `--robot-agent-health` report the same panes safe/healthy.

## Context
Jeff fixed the original #114 mis-typing bug in `f4506605` by passing tmux-tracked agent type into `agent.Parser.ParseWithHint`. Local `ntm` includes that commit, and dogfood confirms the type now converges to Codex-ish (`cod`), but the scheduling gate still disagrees on work state.

Live receipts from `flywheel-4d4a`:
- `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-activity-r2.json`: panes 2/3/4 have `agent_type=codex`, `state=THINKING`, patterns include `codex_esc_interrupt` plus `codex_working` or `codex_waiting_background`.
- `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-is-working-r2.json`: same panes have `agent_type=cod`, `is_working=false`, `is_idle=true`, `recommendation=SAFE_TO_RESTART`.
- `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-agent-health-r2.json`: same panes have `local_state.is_working=false`, `local_state.is_idle=true`, `recommendation=HEALTHY`.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: ntm#114 thread, local ntm source at `/Users/josh/Developer/ntm`, receipts under `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-*.json`.
OUTPUTS: upstream follow-up/reopen or local doctrine identifying which robot surface is authoritative for scheduling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Re-run `ntm --robot-activity=flywheel --panes=2,3,4`, `ntm --robot-is-working=flywheel --panes=2,3,4`, and `ntm --robot-agent-health=flywheel --panes=2,3,4 --no-caut` in a controlled active Codex pane case.
- If activity reports `THINKING` or `GENERATING`, then either is-working reports `is_working=true`/`DO_NOT_INTERRUPT`, or the CLI documentation explicitly marks `--robot-activity` as the scheduling authority.
- If agent-health uses `GetIsWorking()`, it must not report `HEALTHY` / ready-for-work for a pane where the authoritative activity surface has current positive busy evidence.
- File or update the upstream issue with a source trace if the mismatch reproduces on current main.
- Preserve machine-readable receipts for every probe.

## Testing Obligations
- JSON receipts from all three robot surfaces.
- Source trace covering `internal/robot/is_working.go`, `internal/agent/parser.go`, `internal/robot/activity.go`, and `internal/robot/agent_health.go`.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: none unless a local integration/doc patch is required. Close reason must include VALIDATED/DOCUMENTED/SURFACED and the upstream issue/comment URL or explicit no-upstream reason.
close_reason: VALIDATED/DOCUMENTED/SURFACED: r3 robot-surface probes reproduced mismatch on panes 2 and 3: robot-activity=THINKING with codex_working/codex_esc_interrupt while robot-is-working=SAFE_TO_RESTART and agent-health=HEALTHY. Filed upstream ntm#133. Receipt .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-p4nu-receipt.md. DIDNT: no local doctrine patch because mismatch persists upstream. GAPS: none. evidence=/tmp/flywheel-p4nu-robot-activity-r3.json,/tmp/flywheel-p4nu-robot-is-working-r3.json,/tmp/flywheel-p4nu-robot-agent-health-r3.json,.flywheel/receipts/flywheel-p4nu-receipt.md upstream_issue=https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/issues/133 tests=PASS commits=1 beads_updated=flywheel-p4nu:closed no_bead_reason=no_new_gap fuckups_logged=none

title: ntm-stale-error-text-classifier-issue
description:
[ntm-stale-error-text-classifier-issue] file ntm issue: classifier reads stale ERROR/api_error text from scrollback, produces persistent ERROR state until pane is poked

## Trauma class
ntm-classifier-stale-error-poisoning — recurring 2026-05-04 (3rd observation today): skillos:p2 (failed_text), flywheel:p4 (api_error), prior: flywheel sessions earlier today.

## Symptom
- Pane content settles at codex_chevron_prompt (truly idle, awaiting input)
- BUT capture window still contains old "failed" / "api_error" / similar text from prior dispatch run output
- Classifier reads BOTH patterns: `[failed_text|api_error, codex_chevron_prompt]`
- Decision: state=ERROR (because error pattern wins over chevron)
- Reality: pane is alive and idle — sending a fresh prompt makes the agent respond instantly
- Ping clears scrollback, classifier reads only chevron, state→WAITING

## Why this matters
- Watcher v4 correctly filters on state==WAITING and skips ERROR (fail-closed, good)
- Result: pane appears persistently idle to humans ("pane 4 idle - why no dispatch?")
- Workaround required: manual ping per pane every time error text scrolls in
- Already happened 3x in <1h on this Mac — high frequency

## Proposed fix (for upstream ntm issue body)
Classifier should weight patterns by RECENCY relative to `state_since`:
- If `failed_text` / `api_error` was last NEW in capture >5min ago AND `codex_chevron_prompt` is NEWER → state=WAITING (idle at prompt after stale error scrolled past)
- If `failed_text` / `api_error` is fresh (within last minute or new since last probe) → state=ERROR (genuine recent failure)
- Use line-position / scroll-distance heuristic: error pattern at top of capture window = old, chevron at bottom = current truth

Alternative: add `--debounce-error-by-prompt-recency` flag opt-in to test this without breaking existing behavior.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: file ntm issue at Dicklesworthstone/ntm with title "classifier persistent-ERROR false-positive when stale failed_text or api_error in capture scrollback above current chevron prompt"
AG2: cite 3 observed cases (skillos:p2 03:18Z, flywheel:p4 03:26Z, earlier flywheel today) with capture-pane evidence

title: [parity-probe-binary] implement flywheel-toolset-parity CLI
description:
Implement the toolset-parity-probe binary at ~/.local/bin/flywheel-toolset-parity.

Surface (canonical-cli-scoping compliant):
  flywheel-toolset-parity check --runtime={claude,codex,both} --json
  flywheel-toolset-parity doctor
  flywheel-toolset-parity --help / --info / --examples / --schema

Probe per cell (tool × runtime):
- found: which <tool> exits 0
- abs_path: resolved binary path
- version: <tool> --version output (parsed)
- hook_wired: Claude → grep ~/.claude/settings.json hooks; Codex → grep ~/.codex/config.toml + hook scripts in PATH
- smoke_ok: tool-specific smoke (e.g. dcg test, br --version, ntm --version, cm --version)

Output: ~/.local/state/flywheel/toolset-parity-<ts>.json with full matrix
Exit codes: 0 uniform, 1 drift_runtime, 2 drift_version, 3 unreachable_runtime

Codex-pane probing uses ntm send + ntm tail with 90s timeout; if unresponsive, that runtime cell is `runtime_unresponsive` not blank.

Parent: flywheel-2p25
notes: 2026-05-04T08:08:06Z: four-lens BLOCK_CLOSE. 3 open children (flywheel-75py, flywheel-m1j4, flywheel-2p25) + public_lens=no_bar_self_grade. brand+sniff+jeff PASS. did=7/7 tests=PASS. Note: flywheel-2p25 now blocking 3+ parents today — recurring hub blocker.

comments.author: ChartreuseCave
comments.text:
Constraint from `flywheel-cnep` / L69 ORCH-PROBE-AGENT-CONTEXT:

The Codex column in `flywheel-toolset-parity` MUST probe through Codex agent execution context, not an orchestrator shell or pane PATH snapshot. Required schema shape for each Codex cell:

- `agent_context={found,abs_path,realpath,version,smoke_ok,probe_transport,callback_received}`
- `orchestrator_shell_context={found,abs_path,realpath,version}` only as a companion diagnostic
- `context_drift=true` when the two contexts disagree
- `runtime_unresponsive` when the Codex agent does not callback within timeout; raw-shell fallback is forbidden

Implementation remains in q03g scope; this comment only adds the doctrinal constraint.

title: [probe-quality] flywheel-loop doctor status=fail with empty errors/warnings — diagnostically opaque
description:
## Gap surfaced by flywheel-i2k6v (2026-05-09)

`flywheel-loop doctor` returned `status=fail` while emitting EMPTY `errors` AND `warnings` arrays in 4 fuckup-log rows on 2026-05-02 (mobile-eats:0.1 RESEARCH tick prelude). When the doctor reports failure, the safe-refusal pattern correctly aborts the tick — but with no diagnostic information, the RESEARCH worker cannot route the failure to its owner. The probe is doing the right thing operationally (refuse) but failing diagnostically (no signal).

Trauma class: research-health-prelude-fail (4 events, all 2026-05-02 22:07-23:07 UTC, all severity=medium, all mobile-eats:0.1).

## Why this is a probe-quality issue, not a fleet trauma

The Forever-Rule for the parent class (`integrate-prelude-blocked`, INCIDENTS.md line 6735) says: 'record the blocking doctor classes and worker activity proof, route each blocker to its owner.' That requires the doctor to NAME the blocking class. When errors/warnings are both empty, there's nothing to route.

The likely root cause is one of:
1. Doctor checks computing status=fail but failing to populate the errors array (probe-emit bug).
2. Doctor checks failing in a code path that bypasses the errors array (e.g., early exception caught and reduced to status=fail without diagnostic).
3. Status-aggregation bug where the doctor sums sub-checks but loses the per-check details.

## Definition of Done

- Locate the flywheel-loop doctor code path that emits status=fail.
- Add an assertion or invariant: when status=fail, errors[] OR warnings[] must be non-empty (or the doctor must emit a fallback diagnostic like `reason=unknown_probe_failure`).
- Regression test: a doctor invocation that returns status=fail emits at least one diagnostic field.

## Live evidence (re-runnable)

```bash
# 4 events
grep -c research-health-prelude-fail ~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl
# expected: 4

# Confirm empty errors/warnings phrasing
grep research-health-prelude-fail ~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl | head -1 | jq -r .what_happened
# expected: 'RESEARCH tick aborted: flywheel-loop doctor returned status=fail with empty errors/warnings...'

# Find doctor-emit code paths
grep -rn 'status=fail\|"status":"fail"\|status:.fail' ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/lib/portable/core.d/portable_doctor.d/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

## Boundary

Read-only research bead. Does NOT mutate the doctor — that's the closing worker's scope. Filed by flywheel-i2k6v close (MistyCliff, 2026-05-09) so the substrate bug doesn't get re-discovered next time the trauma class fires.
close_reason: 3/3 DoD: locate-verdict (sentinel exists at doctor.d/part-01:400-408), invariant pre-existed externally, 6/6 regression test landed in tests/doctor-status-fail-non-empty-errors.sh; commit 5df0bfe

title: [autoloop-p1-config-tests] add config hermetic tests and 10min cadence
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Add Phase 1 config, tests, and 10-minute launchd cadence after scan/queue proves
safe.

## Required Behavior

- Create `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/config/autoloop.json` or canonical
  equivalent with scoring, cooldown, budgets, and repo allowlist settings.
- Add hermetic tests for STOP behavior, no-ready-repo, ready-repo queue, dry-run,
  and lock behavior.
- Update the LaunchAgent interval from 1800 to 600 only after tests pass.
- Preserve Phase 0 no-agent-wakeup invariant.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `jq '.schema_version and .cooldowns and .budgets' <config>` succeeds.
2. `bash <autoloop-test-script>` passes STOP, no-ready-repo, ready-repo queue,
   dry-run, and lock cases.
3. `plutil -lint ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.flywheel-autoloop.plist`
   passes after interval change.
4. `grep -A1 StartInterval ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.flywheel-autoloop.plist`
   proves 600-second cadence.
5. Launchd kickstart still produces structured `last_run.json`.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Added Phase 1 config/tests/cadence; tests=<cmd>; config=<path>; plist=<path>`.

## Out Of Scope

Dispatching agents, queue consumption, auto-spawn.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: hermetic tests plus launchd lint/kickstart.
- DOCUMENTED: config schema and cadence change are explicit.
- SURFACED: later doctor/status bead reads these config and cadence values.

## Out of scope for THIS bead

- Layer 2 (post-merge auto-doc) → separate bead
- Layer 3 (daily-report rollup) → separate bead
- Layer 4 (session synthesis) → separate bead
- Onboarding wiring → separate bead

Layer 1 is the foundation; Layers 2-4 depend on the schema being ratified first.

## Definition of Done

- Schema file landed under `.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/journey-entry.v1.schema.json`
- Dispatch-template callback contract names `journey_entry_path`
- Validator refuses callbacks without `journey_entry_path`

## Boundary

This bead is OUR-substrate work (flywheel skill bin/lib/scripts + dispatch templates). No upstream push, no Jeffrey-restraint violation. Per memory rule `feedback_use_codex_workers`: dispatch this to a codex worker via ntm.

title: [doctrine] apply agent-ergonomics skill to our own .flywheel/scripts substrate
description:
## Source

Jeffrey Emanuel released /agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools — a 189-file, 1.5MB skill for converting CLIs from human-first to agent-first via 11-dim scoring + recommendation loop. Already in our skill registry (Joshua paid $20/mo). Joshua shared his announcement piece + GPT-5.5 review 2026-05-08T~21:50Z.

## Why apply to our substrate

Our flywheel runs on CLIs that workers + orchestrators + watchdogs invoke constantly:
- `flywheel-loop`, `dispatch-and-verify.sh`, `build-dispatch-packet.sh`
- `validate-callback.py`, `tmp-aggressive-prune.sh`, `flywheel-doctor`
- `frozen-pane-detector-fleet.sh`, `peer-orch-respawn-permit.sh`
- 50+ scripts across `.flywheel/scripts/`

If we apply the skill to even the top-10 most-invoked, we'd:
- Surface agent-friction patterns we've been hand-treating
- Generate `agent_surfaces.jsonl` baseline for time-series tracking
- Get specific 11-dim scores + ranked recommendations per script
- Catch wrong-but-legible invocation failure modes (exact LODP false-negatives we've been seeing)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Inventory top-10 most-invoked CLIs in our substrate. Rank by dispatch-log frequency × failure-rate × blast-radius (skill's own ranking heuristic).

AG2: Run skill against the top-3 (likely: `flywheel-loop`, `dispatch-and-verify.sh`, `build-dispatch-packet.sh`). Generate `agent_surfaces.jsonl` + `recommendations.jsonl` per tool.

AG3: Apply changes auto-mode for low-blast-radius recommendations; surface high-blast-radius for Joshua review.

AG4: Re-score post-changes. Confirm fresh-agent simulation (subagent without prior context) successfully invokes each tool first-try.

AG5: Doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/agent-ergonomics-application-baseline-2026-05-08.md` capturing 11-dim scores + applied changes + re-score deltas.

AG6: Establish quarterly re-audit cadence — agent-ergonomics is a time-series, not one-off.

## Strategic angle

This skill IS the meta-skill for our entire flywheel substrate. Every dispatch packet, every wrapper script, every doctor tool either does or doesn't pass agent-first design. Applying systematically gives us:
- Compounding leverage: one rubric → 50+ improved tools → fleet-wide friction reduction
- Cross-orch propagation: doctrine-sync 6h cadence pushes improved scripts to alps/mobile-eats/skillos
- New-orch onboarding: future workers face cleaner tooling, climb learning curve faster

## Cross-references

- Jeffrey's piece (shared by Joshua 2026-05-08T21:50Z)
- GPT-5.5 review of skill in same context
- Memory: `feedback_canonical_cli_at_dispatch` (canonical-cli-scoping enforcement at dispatch)
- Skill: `canonical-cli-scoping` (our existing CLI discipline skill — complementary)
- Skill: `world-class-doctor-mode-for-cli-tools` (Jeffrey's prior CLI doctor skill, already applied)
- Memory: `feedback_jeffrey_emanuel_name_preference` (Jeffrey not Jeff in human-facing surfaces)
close_reason: Applied agent-ergonomics baseline to flywheel CLI substrate: top-10 inventory, top-3 artifacts, low-blast dispatch-and-verify introspection patch, doctrine cadence, and follow-up for high-blast surfaces.

[doctrine-promotion-N3] joshua-domain-cluster-maintainer-pattern — canonical-pattern-doctrine + auto-detection in gap-hunt-probe
description:
## N=3 trigger fired
- N=1: **flywheel-03yaj** — research-triad cluster (jsm-unmanaged Joshua-domain), 31/31 SKILL.md coverage in 1 tick, 4 sub-beads bulk-closed
- N=2: **flywheel-xhevf** — agent-ergonomics-cli-max (jsm-managed Skillos-substrate), patch-only artifact for skillos handoff
- N=3: **flywheel-plue9** — skill-builder (jsm-unmanaged Joshua-domain), 4/10 → 10/10 SKILL.md coverage in 1 tick, 2 sub-beads retrospectively closed

## Pattern (canonical recipe)
Given: worker discovers cluster of N≥2 wired-but-cold beads in same skill substrate, same shape
Recipe:
1. File `[cluster-doc-completeness]` bead with:
   - Substrate classification (jsm-unmanaged Joshua / jsm-managed Skillos / Jeff Premium AUDIT-ONLY)
   - Joshua-authorized cross-repo block IF jsm-unmanaged
   - Cite N≥3 precedent (03yaj/xhevf/plue9)
2. Dispatch to worker with cluster context warm
3. Worker writes paired patch artifact at `.flywheel/audit/<bead>/patches/`
4. Auto-close subordinate auto-beads with `resolved-upstream-via-<cluster-bead>` disposition

## Why doctrine + auto-detection
- N=3 satisfies 3-strike skill-promotion rule
- gap-hunt-probe currently files individual beads; should DETECT clusters and propose cluster-maintainer bead first
- Same shape as forward-link-doctrine-doc-recipe (pmg3c shipped as auto-injector at N=4)

## Acceptance options
A. Doctrine doc only: write `.flywheel/doctrine/cluster-maintainer-pattern.md` citing 3 instances + recipe
B. Doctrine + cluster-detection in gap-hunt-probe: when probe finds N≥2 wired-but-cold in same `~/.claude/skills/<x>/`, emit single cluster bead instead of N individual ones
C. Doctrine + cluster auto-suggestion in build-dispatch-packet: when bead title prefix matches `[gap-wired-but-cold]` AND ≥2 sibling beads exist in same substrate, inject "consider cluster bead" hint

Worker decides per cost-benefit (kwjja Option D precedent — cheapest mechanization that still moves substrate forward).

## Sister to pmg3c
pmg3c shipped forward-link-doctrine-doc-recipe at N=4. Cluster-maintainer at N=3 follows same promotion shape: doctrine doc + optional auto-detection.
close_reason: done

title: [data-backed-deferral-receipts] implement save and override JSONL receipts
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-bbts
Plan predecessor: flywheel-28v

## Goal

Implement append-only save and override receipts for data-backed deferral so prevented Joshua questions become durable learning rows instead of scrollback-only wins.

## Scope

- Write prevented-deferral rows to `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-saves.jsonl` using the plan shape from lines 238-265.
- Write allow/override rows to `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-overrides.jsonl` using the plan shape from lines 193-208.
- Suppress duplicate save rows with the same `draft_question` and `suggested_action` inside a 10-minute window.
- Preserve append-only behavior; a later dispatch failure must not delete the save row.

## Acceptance Gates

1. A test fixture run creates or validates a save row with `event=="data_backed_deferral_prevented"` in `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-saves.jsonl` or a temp-path equivalent.
2. `jq -e 'select(.event=="data_backed_deferral_prevented") | .ts and .session and .draft_question and .suggested_action and (.signals_aligned | length >= 3)' <receipt-file>` passes against the save receipt.
3. An allow fixture creates or validates an override row with `event=="data_backed_deferral_override"` and `reason` in `evidence_missing,cost,source_scope,reject_revert,require_confirm,live_prod,tie_between`.
4. Duplicate-suppression test runs the same prevented-deferral case twice and proves only one matching save row appears inside the 10-minute window.
5. `rg -n "data-backed-deferral-saves|data-backed-deferral-overrides|data_backed_deferral_prevented|data_backed_deferral_override" ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop .flywheel/scripts tests` finds implementation and tests.
6. Receipt writing supports temp-path injection for tests so test runs do not depend on mutating the live JSONL files.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: tests prove save, override, duplicate suppression, and append-only posture.
- DOCUMENTED: receipt field schema is described in help or adjacent docs.
- SURFACED: downstream tick/doctor surfaces can count saves and violations without parsing prose.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L56 for promotion ladder evidence linkage.
- AGENTS.md L71 and L80 for validation receipts and DID/DIDNT/GAPS evidence.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No doctor JSON field wiring; that belongs to the doctor surface bead.
- No dispatch-template lint changes.
- No deletion or compaction of existing receipt logs.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented data-backed-deferral save/override receipts; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-receipts]`.
close_reason: implemented data-backed-deferral save/override receipts; tests=bash tests/data-backed-deferral-guard.sh,bash -n ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop,bash -n tests/data-backed-deferral-guard.sh,rg acceptance grep; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-receipts]

title: [doctrine-prop] skillos cohort plan: 75 L-rules in 4-batch waves with cross-orch handoff
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07
parent=flywheel-owirq

## Why
4 fleet repos synced cleanly today (alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, zesttube, polymarket-pico-z all at doctrine_version=2026-05-07.L126). skillos is the outlier — 75 L-rules missing (L29, L35, L48-L73, L75-L107, L110-L111, L115-L126). Bulk-applying 75 L-rules to a peer orchestrator's substrate without phased review is risky; some rules may conflict with skillos's local doctrine, and the orchestrator session is in active use.

## Acceptance
1. /flywheel:plan a 4-wave cohort:
   - Wave 1 (foundation): L29 + L35 + L48-L57 (~12 rules)
   - Wave 2 (substrate): L58-L73 (~15 rules)  
   - Wave 3 (recovery+dispatch): L75-L99 (~25 rules)
   - Wave 4 (recent): L100-L126 (~23 rules including today's L125+L126)
2. Per-wave: cross-orch handoff to skillos:1 via Agent Mail asking for L-rule conflict review BEFORE doctrine-sync --apply
3. Per-wave: skillos:1 reviews + acks + flywheel:1 runs --apply with separate idempotency key
4. Per-wave: skillos:1 verifies post-apply on their next tick + confirms no doctrine conflicts
5. Final: skillos doctrine_version stamps at 2026-05-07.L126
6. compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract

## Hard rules
- NO bulk apply (75 rules at once) — would overwhelm skillos:1's review capacity + risk silent doctrine conflicts
- Cross-orch handoff REQUIRED at every wave boundary
- L107 + canonical-meta-rules-sync three-surface watchdog must pass after each wave
- skillos:1 has veto on any individual L-rule that conflicts with their local doctrine — flywheel:1 documents the deferral, never overrides
- Compliance pack per wave (4 packs total, not 1 bulk)

## Cross-refs
- flywheel-owirq (parent: doctrine-sync infrastructure)
- /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07/01-VERIFY-PASS.json (skillos missing list)
- L107 shared-surface reservation
- canonical-meta-rules-sync three-surface watchdog
- Cross-orch protocol per Agent Mail

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[doctrine-prop] skillos cohort plan: 75 L-rules in 4-batch waves with cross-orch handoff` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-rdqc7` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.
close_reason: Completed dispatch flywheel-rdqc7-fda50c. Authored skillos 4-wave doctrine cohort plan and plan-level compliance pack. Evidence: .flywheel/PLANS/doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07/03-SKILLOS-COHORT-PLAN.md, .flywheel/PLANS/doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07/03-SKILLOS-COHORT-PLAN.json, compliance/flywheel-rdqc7/. Validation PASS: JSON cohort validator and compliance score. Gap filed: flywheel-rdqc7.1 for doctrine-sync per-rule allowlist required before wave apply.

title: [full-corpus] clone + index ALL 177 Dicklesworthstone repos
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03T17:33Z: 'lets get ALL of it indexed - entire fucking stack' + 'dicklesworthstone has like 177 repos'

## Status

- Already indexed: ntm, beads_rust, mcp_agent_mail, mcp_agent_mail_rust, .claude/skills (Jeff doctrine docs covered)
- Indexing in flight (17 repos): destructive_command_guard, beads_viewer, agentic_coding_flywheel_setup, pi_agent_rust, claude_code_agent_farm, coding_agent_session_search, frankensqlite, frankensearch, frankentui, your-source-to-prompt.html, swiss_army_llama, llm_aided_ocr, bulk_transcribe_youtube_videos_from_playlist, acip, dcg, cass, Dicklesworthstone (umbrella README only — no subdirs)
- Total local Jeff repos: ~21 of 177
- Gap: ~156 repos not cloned locally

GitHub API confirms public_repos=177 on Dicklesworthstone profile. The local 'Dicklesworthstone' directory is just his profile README repo, NOT a clone-all umbrella.

Codex watchtower confirmed live 17:38Z (78 new issues, 18 relevant) — surveillance pattern works at scale. info-source-watchtower meta-skill in flight on flywheel:2 — this corpus becomes its first deep-dive case study after complete.

## Acceptance gates

A. Inventory the gap via `gh api /users/Dicklesworthstone/repos --paginate` and diff against existing 21 local repos. Output to `/tmp/jeff-all-repos.txt`.

B. Clone all missing ~156 via `gh repo clone Dicklesworthstone/<name>` into `~/Developer/<name>`.
   - Skip archived repos unless evidence-cited valuable
   - SHALLOW clones (--depth=50) for the long-tail; full history for ADOPT-tier
   - Disk budget: aim under 50GB; abort if approaching 100GB
   - Throttle: respect GitHub API rate limit
   - Track progress in `/tmp/jeff-corpus-clone-progress.jsonl`

C. After clone, fire `mcp__socraticode__codebase_index` for each new repo. Socraticode handles concurrent indexes; fire in parallel batches of ~30.

D. Update dicklesworthstone-stack INVENTORY.md with full 177-row table (currently 25 curated). Each row gets: stars, lang, last commit, archived, our_local, indexed, verdict (ADOPT/EVALUATE/SKIP/ARCHIVE).

E. Filter pass: identify long-tail repos and mark SKIP with rationale.

F. After indexing complete, file followup bead `jeff-corpus-deep-dive-synthesis` for cross-repo pattern extraction.

## Skills cite (source-(a))

- dicklesworthstone-stack (PATTERN SOURCE — extend INVENTORY.md, don't duplicate)
- gh-cli (canonical clone path)

title: [file-length-split-reshape] hzsro Phase 6.2-6.6 — Sections A-G are ONE jq aggregator, not 5 shells
description:
Reshape: Phase 6 sub-beads 6.2-6.6 (Sections A/B/C/D+E/F+G) were filed assuming each Section is an extractable ~80-150 line shell file. Inspection of the subject file shows they are jq-pipe fragments (Section C: 12 lines, F+G: 16 lines, A: 17 lines, D+E: 20 lines, B: 12 lines) inside ONE jq aggregator invocation at lines 1697-1791 (~95 lines total including helper defs and packet wrapping).

These cannot be extracted as separate shell files because:
1. Each Section is a jq pipe-fragment (not a function — no shell entry point)
2. They share the def lrule and def primitive helpers (lines 1697-1712) that are jq-scope-internal
3. They must be concatenated in a single jq invocation against the same packet variable; splitting requires reassembling the jq script via shell string concatenation, which adds complexity for no readability win
4. The aggregator is one logical unit: augment the doctor packet with the deferred-fields surfaces

CORRECT EXTRACTION (replaces 6.2-6.6):
- ONE new helper portable_doctor.d/02-doctor-field-aggregator.sh (~100 lines)
- Contains a function _portable_doctor_apply_field_aggregator that runs the entire jq invocation and returns the augmented packet on stdout
- Caller replaces inline 95-line jq invocation with single function call
- Pure stdin/stdout transformation (Jeff functional-shell pattern), no dynamic-scoping needed

CLOSE AS DUPLICATES of new bead:
- flywheel-0h6ko (6.2, Section C)
- flywheel-tdeft (6.3, Sections F+G)
- flywheel-jzndo (6.4, Section A)
- flywheel-4ivbe (6.5, Sections D+E)

Per feedback_calibrate_test_to_actual_contract_before_filing_upstream and feedback_jeff_response_shape_5_reshaped: when a plan diverges from upstream reality, reshape the plan, do not force-execute against wrong premises.

title: [skill-bug] agent-ergonomics-... skill name 76 chars exceeds codex 64-char limit
description:
## Source

Joshua's purchased skill from jeffreys-skills.md fails to load on every codex 0.125 / 0.129 startup with:

```
⚠ /Users/josh/.codex/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools/SKILL.md:
  invalid name: exceeds maximum length of 64 characters
⚠ /Users/josh/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools/SKILL.md:
  invalid name: exceeds maximum length of 64 characters
```

Skill name from SKILL.md frontmatter:
`agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools` = 76 chars

## Workaround

Symlink with shorter name:
```bash
ln -s agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools \
  ~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics
```

This may or may not work depending on whether codex resolves symlinks before validating name. If frontmatter `name:` is what's checked, need to also patch SKILL.md frontmatter to a shorter alias.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Verify whether codex's 64-char limit applies to:
- Directory name
- SKILL.md frontmatter `name:` field
- Both
Determines workaround shape.

AG2: Apply workaround locally — shorten name to fit. Suggested: `agent-ergonomics-cli` (already exists at ~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-cli/ — different/older skill).
Or: `agent-ergonomics-max` (20 chars).

AG3: File issue at jeffreys-skills.md / Dicklesworthstone/jeffreys-skills (or wherever the skill ships from) noting the name length collides with codex 64-char limit.

AG4: Document the codex 64-char limit as a SKILL-NAMING-CONSTRAINT canonical L-rule for our own skills (avoid future authoring trip).

## Cross-references

- Jeffrey announcement piece (Joshua shared 2026-05-08T~21:50Z)
- Memory: `feedback_jeffrey_emanuel_name_preference` (Jeffrey not Jeff)
- canonical-cli-scoping skill (audit subject of meta-application — being audited via this skill)
close_reason: Fixed: verified Codex limit applies to SKILL.md frontmatter name, confirmed local short-name workaround, filed upstream issue https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/jeffreysprompts.com/issues/5, documented L150 SKILL-NAMING-CONSTRAINT on canonical doctrine surfaces and README; evidence .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-rzgqc/skill-name-findings.md

title: [skillos-discovery-coordinator] implement fleet skill discovery promotion ladder
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/fleet-skill-reporting-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-xbuh

## Goal

Implement the skillos coordinator reader that groups `skill-discoveries.jsonl` rows and applies the 1/2/3/5-sighting promotion ladder.

## Scope

- Add skillos-owned coordinator tick logic or a reusable flywheel script that skillos invokes.
- Read the last 24h of `~/.local/state/flywheel/skill-discoveries.jsonl`.
- Group by `candidate_skill_name`, with exact match first and fuzzy-match only when explicitly safe.
- Ladder:
  - 1 sighting: log only.
  - 2 sightings: notify/create Agent Mail thread candidate.
  - 3 sightings: create skill-builder bead if one does not exist.
  - 5 sightings: dispatch skillos worker when bead is open and capacity exists.
- Emit a `fleet-skill-pulse` row to `~/.local/state/flywheel/team-pulse.jsonl`.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Coordinator command supports `--dry-run --json` and reports grouped candidates without mutating Beads or Agent Mail.
2. Fixture with one sighting returns action `log_only`.
3. Fixture with two sightings returns action `notify_skillos` or `agent_mail_thread_needed`.
4. Fixture with three sightings runs `br create --dry-run` in dry-run mode and reports a planned skill-builder bead with consolidated evidence.
5. Apply mode at three sightings requires an idempotency key and creates at most one skill-builder bead for the same candidate on repeated runs.
6. Fixture with five sightings reports `dispatch_worker_needed` and includes the target skill-builder bead id if one exists.
7. Coordinator writes a valid `fleet-skill-pulse` row with candidate counts and pending action count.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: dry-run/apply/idempotency fixtures cover all ladder thresholds.
- DOCUMENTED: coordinator help describes thresholds and ownership by skillos.
- SURFACED: pulse rows and planned bead ids make skill promotion visible outside one pane.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L52, L55, L56, L61, L70, L71.
- Plan lines 75-101 for ladder and skillos HQ role.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No Agent Mail thread implementation beyond emitting planned action.
- No live NTM dispatch send; dispatch is a separate notification/dispatch bead.
- No supervised quality scoring for discoveries.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented skillos skill-discovery coordinator ladder; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[skillos-discovery-coordinator]`.
close_reason: implemented skillos skill-discovery coordinator ladder; tests=tests/test_skillos_discovery_coordinator.sh; commit_tag=[skillos-discovery-coordinator]

title: [dispatch-template-validation] append validation block to every worker dispatch packet
description:
### Title

`[dispatch-template-validation] append validation block to every worker dispatch packet`

### Goal

Append a canonical validation block to dispatch packets so every worker callback carries enough evidence for the orchestrator to validate it. This bead makes the validation contract visible at dispatch time, not after callback receipt.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`, `dispatch-acceptance-gate-incomplete-corpus`, and `orchestrator-observability-contract-bypass`. Lane A found dispatch templates partially documented but not mechanically enforcing validation, and 00-INTENT names dispatch-template injection as the first mechanical gate.

### Acceptance gates

1. Dispatch template renderer appends a `VALIDATION BLOCK` without restructuring existing packet schema. Proof receipt: `B02_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. The block requires worker callback fields: `evidence=`, `artifact_checks=`, `validation_notes=`, `files_released=`, `beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=`, and `fuckups_logged=` when trauma occurred. Proof receipt: `B02_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. The block includes `chain_if_capacity`: worker must run named `next_phase` in the same turn if capacity exists, or return `chain_blocked_reason=`. Proof receipt: `B02_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Template audit fixture fails a dispatch packet that lacks callback instructions or validation fields. Proof receipt: `B02_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Template audit fixture passes a valid Claude worker packet and a valid Codex worker packet. Proof receipt: `B02_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Generated dispatch text includes Agent Mail reservation/release and L52/L53 callback receipt requirements. Proof receipt: `B02_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. The block names `validate-callback` receipt as the orchestrator-side next step before summary or integration. Proof receipt: `B02_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `template_path=<path> audit_cmd=<cmd>:PASS sample_dispatch=<path> validation_block_hash=<sha256> commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[dispatch-template-validation]`.

### Doctrine references

L50, L51, L52, L53, L60, L70; `flywheel-1z65`; `flywheel-7lby`; `feedback_orchestrator_must_dispatch.md`.

### Out of scope

No implementation of `validate-callback`, no doctor signal, no automatic bead mutation.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B01.  
Blocks: B03, B05, B08, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings if dispatch block wording depends on Joshua-disposes decisions around blocking vs background validation.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B02_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B02_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B02_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B02_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B02_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B02_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B02_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [monitor-noise-investigation] task-notification replays stale events on every turn — context pollution class
description:
Joshua surfaced 2026-05-09 (~end of session): the persistent Monitor (task bf05p716p, tail -F dispatch-log.jsonl filtering callbacks) emits task-notifications containing the SAME stale May 7-8 events on every conversation turn, regardless of whether new lines have appeared. Net effect: orchestrator context fills with hundreds of duplicate task-notification blocks per session, polluting reasoning surface and risking compaction.

Symptom evidence: every recent turn includes 1-3 task-notifications each containing the exact same 12-event head from 2026-05-07T21:35Z through 2026-05-08T03:42Z. None correspond to current-tick callbacks (those arrive separately as user messages from worker dispatch in the format DONE flywheel-xyz...).

Likely root causes (to verify):
1. Monitor runtime replays initial buffer on every consumer reconnect (event-loop pattern bug)
2. tail -F seek behavior — may rewind to file head when restart-on-truncate fires
3. grep --line-buffered output buffering interacting with task-notification consumer
4. Monitor was armed at session start but the task-notification delivery contract treats every turn-boundary as a 'new subscriber' replaying snapshot

Acceptance:
AG1: Reproduce stale-replay in a clean Monitor invocation against a static log file (no new appends) — verify replay fires every turn with same content
AG2: Identify which layer is replaying (Monitor framework vs tail -F vs task-notification delivery)
AG3: Propose fix: either (a) make Monitor's persistent state track last-emitted line offset, OR (b) document that tail -F-based filters need explicit dedup, OR (c) change /loop dynamic-mode wake contract to NOT use persistent Monitor for dispatch-log (too noisy) and fall back to ScheduleWakeup with shorter interval
AG4: If fix (c) preferred: update /loop skill memory feedback_orch_wake_event_driven_not_time_based to caveat with 'persistent Monitor on append-only log files replays stale events; use polling Monitor or ScheduleWakeup with inotify-style semantics instead'

Cross-references:
- Memory: feedback_orch_wake_event_driven_not_time_based (says use Monitor; this bug breaks that contract in practice)
- Today's session demonstrated the bug across ~30+ turns of stale replays
- Sister class to flywheel-iyaym (promotion-candidate-fire-investigation) — both are 'event repeated despite being old' classes
close_reason: shipped: tail -n 0 -F fix at /loop skill + test fixture + memory file; root cause confirmed via two-Monitor contrast; 4-char insert (commit c7b74bc)

title: [value-gap-probe] dedup filings against still-open same-dimension beads
description:
## Pattern observed (2026-05-09)

value-gap-probe.sh dimension rotation has now produced TWO duplicate-bead pairs against still-open same-dimension beads:

- mobile-eats-end-user-health: flywheel-1rmp.7 (primary, closed 2026-05-09) ↔ flywheel-1rmp.17 (duplicate, closed 2026-05-09 same day with no functional change)
- cross-time-synthesis: flywheel-1rmp.9 (primary, closed 2026-05-09) ↔ flywheel-1rmp.19 (duplicate, closed 2026-05-09 same day with no functional change)

Both duplicate-pairs were filed by the value-gap-probe rotation when the dimension's prior bead was still open. The probe currently caps "1 bead per tick" but does not check whether an open bead already exists for the dimension across ticks.

## Proposed enhancement

Before filing a new value-gap bead, value-gap-probe.sh should:
1. Query br for open beads matching the dimension keyword in the title
2. If an open same-dimension bead exists: emit `bead_action: "skipped_duplicate", existing_bead_id: <id>`
3. Otherwise: file as today

Smallest change: add a pre-filing predicate function `dimension_has_open_bead(<dimension>)` and gate the existing filing call on it. Tests: fixture-based (dim_open ⇒ skip; dim_closed ⇒ file; dim_never_filed ⇒ file).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pre-filing dedupe predicate added to value-gap-probe.sh
- [ ] When predicate fires, emit structured `bead_action: "skipped_duplicate"` event with `existing_bead_id`
- [ ] Regression test exercises all three states (open/closed/never)
- [ ] No regression to existing 10-dimension rotation; cap-1-per-tick guardrail preserved

## Definition of Done

Probe rotates without filing a duplicate bead when prior dimension bead is still open; ledger and dispatch-log show `bead_action: "skipped_duplicate"` rows for those rotations.

title: [jeff-stack J9] daily-report-schema-and-verdict-template
description:
Daily report schema + L64-layer-2 "what can we learn" verdict template.

DEPENDS ON: J7 (script generates reports).

PURPOSE: Define the canonical structure of /tmp/jeff-report-<date>.md so the report is consistent, machine-parseable, and human-scannable. Bake L64 layer-2 verdict logic per artifact.

SCHEMA (markdown structure):
```
# Jeff Daily Report — <date>

## Run metadata
- run_ts: <ISO>
- duration_sec: <N>
- repos_checked: <N>
- repos_with_changes: <N>
- new_commits_total: <N>
- new_tweets: <N>
- new_blog_posts: <N>
- re_indexed_repos: <N>

## New Commits (by repo)
### <repo-name>
- Commits: <N>
- Files changed: <N>
- Net lines: +<N>/-<N>
- Highlights: <subject lines>
- Verdict: YES_ADOPT | YES_ADAPT | NO_NOT_OUR_DOMAIN | NEED_RESEARCH
- Reason: <one-liner>
- Suggested action: <bead title or "monitor">

## New Releases
...

## New Tweets (doodlestein)
...

## New Blog Posts
...

## Re-indexed (socraticode)
- <repo>: <new chunks indexed>

## Aggregate "What can we learn" digest
- Top 3 patterns from today: ...
- Cross-repo themes: ...
- Candidate adopt-jeff-pattern beads: ...
```

VERDICT LOGIC (per artifact):
- YES_ADOPT — uses primitive we already have a different version of, his approach is better
- YES_ADAPT — pattern applicable to our domain with modifications
- NO_NOT_OUR_DOMAIN — interesting but not relevant (e.g., specific to his ML projects)
- NEED_RESEARCH — looks important but we don't yet know how it fits

The verdict is HEURISTIC v1: regex/keyword on commit messages + file paths. Not LLM-graded. v2 (separate bead) can add LLM verdict.

DELIVERABLES:
1. /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-report-template.sh — generates the report from raw diff data
2. /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-verdict-heuristic.sh — emits one of the 4 verdicts per repo
3. Documentation in dicklesworthstone-stack skill SKILL.md describing the schema
4. Sample report at /tmp/jeff-report-EXAMPLE.md

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. Schema validates: each section heading present in test run
2. Verdict logic emits valid enum on every artifact
3. Report is grep-able: `grep "YES_ADOPT" report` returns hits when patterns apply
4. SKILL.md updated with schema reference
5. Backward-compatible: J7's report generation continues to work after schema migration

CROSS-REF: This bead implements L64 Layer 2 doctrine per AGENTS.md.

title: Wire ntm activity (not health) into pane-state checks; add frozen-spinner detector
description:
## Problem
Today skillos:0.1 was wedged in codex 'Working (4m 04s • esc to interrupt)' state with a queued '› Summarize recent commits' prompt unsubmitted. The orchestrator did not catch this because flywheel scripts call 'ntm health' for pane state, which classifies the pane as idle (matches '›' as idle_prompt without checking spinner above).

ntm activity --json correctly reports state=THINKING with velocity=0 and duration=0s. We have detection — we just consume the wrong probe.

## Trauma class
codex-queued-not-submitted-undetected. Earlier session instance: 02:14Z meat-puppet cluster.

## Fix
1. Update _shared/pane-state.sh (and any callers in /flywheel:status, /flywheel:tick Step 3) to consume 'ntm activity --json' as primary truth source. 'ntm health' becomes secondary cross-check only.
2. Add freeze rule: state in [THINKING, GENERATING] AND velocity=0 AND duration > 120s => classify FROZEN_SUSPECT. Surface in /flywheel:status output.
3. Update memory feedback_pane_state_ntm_health.md: correct the rule to 'ntm activity is primary, ntm health is secondary'.

## Acceptance criteria
- _shared/pane-state.sh outputs include state field from activity probe
- /flywheel:status shows FROZEN_SUSPECT row when applicable
- /flywheel:tick Step 3 refuses to dispatch to a FROZEN_SUSPECT pane
- Memory file updated
- Test: synthetic frozen pane (codex Working+queued) classified as FROZEN_SUSPECT

## Cross-references
- Today's recovery (skillos:0.1) used: ntm respawn --panes=1 --force then tmux send-keys 'codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m gpt-5.5' Enter
- Sister bead: flywheel:recover-pane slash command
- Upstream issue: ntm health vs ntm activity disagreement (file at Dicklesworthstone/ntm)

title: [clobber-recovery] canonical primitive shipped 2026-05-09
description:
Shipped .flywheel/scripts/clobber-recovery.sh + tests/clobber-recovery-smoke.sh as canonical primitive for the clobbered_doctrine_docs class. Class signature: worker fails change-dir into temp fixture path (special-char), subsequent printf/echo redirect lands in repo root truncating MISSION/STATE/GOAL/AGENTS/INCIDENTS to single-line schema_version. Today incident: flywheel-m49r2 worker clobbered all 3 doctrine docs (17345/241/138 to 1 line each); restored from HEAD commit 9c1f61f.

Primitive contract: takes --paths list (explicit) OR canonical default set; heuristic gate in default mode only restores files where cur_bytes<100 AND head_bytes>1000 (truncation signature); explicit --paths bypasses heuristic; per-file NOOP if blob hash matches HEAD; REFUSED if HEAD blob tiny; NOT_CLOBBER if heuristic fails; otherwise overwrites with HEAD blob content. Receipts append to .flywheel/clobber-recovery-log.jsonl plus fuckup-log.jsonl class=clobber-recovery. Exit codes: 0 restore/dry-run, 3 all-refused, 4 all-noop.

Smoke 5/5 PASS: T1 noop, T2 single-line restore, T3 runtime drift NOT_CLOBBER, T4 tiny HEAD refused, T5 receipt log.

AG1: bash tests/clobber-recovery-smoke.sh exits 0 with 5/5 PASS
AG2: --dry-run on canonical set reports NOOP+NOT_CLOBBER (no false-restore on legitimate runtime drift)
AG3: orch dispatch policy: when worker callback contains clobbered_doctrine_docs OR damage=mission.md_*->1 marker, orch invokes clobber-recovery non-interactively (no Joshua-ask) then proceeds with re-dispatch
AG4: doctrine: add to .flywheel/rules/ index as canonical primitive for this class; point dispatch templates at it

title: [agentmail-fd-pressure] daemon FD exhaustion under reservation traffic — reproducer + plist ulimit bump
description:
UPDATED 2026-05-09T~19:50Z per skillos:1 FENNEL ACK to my 185500Z plan_response.

**Live FD probe data from skillos:1:**
- Plist: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.mcp-agent-mail-local
- PID at probe time: 33104
- Current FD count: 15
- Soft limit: 4096
- Pressure type: burst-only, NOT steady-state
- Plist creator: NO canonical script in skillos or flywheel — fleet-shared, manually-created by Joshua or via deleted setup

**Refined acceptance** (B1 reproducer must actually trigger the burst):
AG1: Build .flywheel/scripts/agentmail-fd-pressure-probe.sh that spawns concurrent reservation traffic against the daemon and samples lsof FD count. Minimum target: >70% of soft_limit (>2867 FDs) sustained for 5+ seconds. If we can't reach 70% threshold under heaviest realistic load, the FD class is benign and B2 ulimit bump is unnecessary.
AG2: Sample baselines: idle (15 expected), single-worker, 4-worker burst, 8-worker burst, sustained reservation churn. Capture peak FD count and headroom_pct.
AG3: Decision tree based on AG2 data:
  - If peak >70% soft: ulimit bump justified -> route to skillos:1 (B2: 4096->16384)
  - If peak 40-70%: marginal pressure, monitor only -> file doctor invariant only
  - If peak <40%: no action needed, close as benign
AG4: Doctor invariant agentmail_fd_count_under_pressure (flywheel:1 owned per my plan B3) — sample lsof FD count for daemon PID, FAIL if >85% soft limit.

**SLA refinement APPROVED**: compaction-aware tick semantics (per skillos handoff 194900Z). Take to next Petal-9 ratification.

Cross-reference: companion handoffs at .flywheel/handoffs/2026-05-09T185500Z-from-flywheel-1-to-skillos-1-plan-response-storage-fd-blocker.md and .flywheel/handoffs/2026-05-09T194900Z-from-skillos-1-to-flywheel-1-plan-response-ack-storage-fd-blocker.md
close_reason: done: AG3 verdict=benign_no_action across 5 baselines + 16-worker heavy stress; daemon FD held at 14/4096 (0.34%) under all load shapes (kqueue+connection-pooled async server doesn't allocate per-request FDs). B2 ulimit bump UNNECESSARY; B3 doctor invariant agentmail_fd_count_under_pressure wired and passes. Commit 8707722. did=4/4

title: [ntm-upstream] review-queue needs L85 idle-state schema before idle-state-probe replacement
description:
Mission-anchor: continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
Source bead: flywheel-txeui / ntm-wire-in-W2-txeui-2026-05-07

Research verdict: ISSUE for replacing .flywheel/scripts/idle-state-probe.sh with native ntm review-queue.

Observed:
- idle-state-probe.sh is the L85 canonical classifier for dispatching/cooldown/light_queue/saturated/disabled_class/not_waiting.
- ntm review-queue --json emits human output, not JSON.
- ntm review-queue flywheel --format json emits INFO log lines before the JSON object, so stdout is not strict robot JSON.
- Native review-queue JSON contains generated_at, session, idle_agents[], suggestions[]. It does not expose L85 fields: idle_state_class, idle_state_summary, idle_dispatching_over_threshold_count, idle_state_config_path, idle_state_config_loaded, thresholds, cooldown remaining seconds, ready P0/P1 counts, disabled_class, not_waiting, capture_provenance.

Needed for USE/WRAP:
- A strict --json or --format json robot envelope with no log contamination on stdout.
- L85-compatible idle classifier schema or a separate native subcommand that exposes structured idle-state rows, config, thresholds, cooldown, and doctor fields.
- Stable tests proving dispatching/cooldown/light_queue/saturated/disabled_class/not_waiting parity with tests/idle-state-probe.sh.

Evidence: /tmp/ntm-wire-in-W2-txeui-2026-05-07-evidence.md

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[ntm-upstream] review-queue needs L85 idle-state schema before idle-state-probe replacement` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-txeui.1` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.

## Gap

The root worker-close regression expects the `beads-compliance-and-completion-verification` skill to contain the deterministic dirty-scope marker (`declared_scope_dirty_at_close` / close requires committed declared scope). That skill is JSM-managed, so this worker did not patch it directly.

- `bash tests/test_worker_close_requires_git_commit.sh` passes.

title: [dispatch-enforcement-rollout] add migration rollback and promotion surfaces
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/dispatch-enforcement-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-pnr9
Plan predecessor: flywheel-8i5

## Goal

Ship migration, rollback, and promotion surfaces for dispatch enforcement so schema v2 can harden safely without breaking legacy rows.

## Scope

- Add one-time backfill/dry-run command or script for old dispatch-log rows.
- Infer `session`, `pane`, and legacy version fields where possible; leave `dispatch_skill_version="legacy"` when unknown.
- Keep strict enforcement versioned: legacy rows warn, schema v2 rows can fail.
- Preserve rollback controls: global disable, per-gate env disable, sentinel directory, repo-local sentinel, and `JOSHUA_OVERRIDE`.
- Wire doctor-signal promotion for repeated dispatch enforcement violations.

## Acceptance Gates

1. A backfill dry-run command prints planned row annotations without modifying `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl`.
2. Backfill apply requires explicit idempotency key or confirmation and writes an audit receipt.
3. `flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json | jq -e '.dispatch_contract_violations or .dispatch_enforcement'` passes.
4. Doctor fixture treats old rows missing v2 fields as warn-only and schema v2 rows missing required fields as fail.
5. `rg -n "dispatch_gate_false_positive|FLYWHEEL_DISPATCH_ENFORCE|FLYWHEEL_DISPATCH_GATE_DISABLE|dispatch-gates-disabled|no-enforce-dispatch|dispatch_contract_violations" /Users/josh/.claude/hooks/flywheel-loop-dispatch-transport-gate.sh ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop .flywheel/scripts README.md .flywheel/canonical-paths.txt tests` proves rollback and surfacing are wired.
6. `doctor-signal-bead-promotion.sh` or equivalent promotion path creates or updates a bead when repeated dispatch enforcement violations exceed the configured threshold.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: backfill dry-run/apply and doctor fixture tests pass.
- DOCUMENTED: README/canonical paths describe migration, rollback knobs, and doctor fields.
- SURFACED: doctor and bead-promotion paths make enforcement drift visible.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L29, L50, L51, L52, L53, L56, L71.
- Plan lines 233-285 for compatibility audit and rollback.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No implementation of individual hook gates; this bead integrates migration/surfacing once gates exist.
- No destructive rewrite of dispatch-log history.
- No commits.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented dispatch enforcement migration/rollback/doctor surfaces; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[dispatch-enforcement-rollout]`.
close_reason: DID: shipped dispatch-log v2 backfill dry-run/apply surface, rollback sentinels, doctor contract fail/warn policy, and auto-promotion for repeated dispatch_contract_violations. DIDNT: did not mutate pane3/4 owned files; README had pre-existing unrelated dirt and only dispatch-enforcement hunk was committed. GAPS: live hook/lib files are outside repo git but patched in place. evidence=/tmp/flywheel-u1x3-compliance-pack.md commit=74bcb63 tests=PASS beads_updated=flywheel-u1x3:closed no_bead_reason=no_new_gap fuckups_logged=none

title: [codex-parity-validation] enforce L69 agent-context validation for Codex and Claude callback probes
description:
### Title

`[codex-parity-validation] enforce L69 agent-context validation for Codex and Claude callback probes`

### Goal

Make callback validation and parity checks work across Claude Code and Codex without collapsing raw shell truth into agent runtime truth. This bead binds the validation primitive to `flywheel-2p25` and `flywheel-q03g` so Codex-specific probes run through Codex agent callbacks.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `fleet-death-rca`, `codex-pane-crashed-mid-dispatch`, `frozen-codex-spinner-misclassified-as-thinking`, and `orchestrator-probe-discipline`. ORX1 proved that raw shell and Codex execution context can diverge; L69 was written to stop that exact misclassification.

### Acceptance gates

1. Validation receipt supports `agent_context` and `orchestrator_shell_context` as separate fields. Proof receipt: `B11_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Codex probe path sends probe through `ntm send <session> --pane=<n>` and validates the callback content. Proof receipt: `B11_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Claude probe path uses Claude agent Bash context where applicable. Proof receipt: `B11_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Raw shell pass plus agent failure returns `context_drift`, not pass. Proof receipt: `B11_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Agent timeout returns `runtime_unresponsive`, not pass and not raw-shell fallback. Proof receipt: `B11_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. CLI identity proof records `command -v`, `realpath`, version/help/smoke for collision-prone commands per L65. Proof receipt: `B11_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Integration point with `flywheel-q03g` parity probe is documented; if q03g is absent, this bead runs fixture-only or blocks. Proof receipt: `B11_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
8. Tests cover Codex agent pass, Codex agent fail, raw-shell/agent drift, and runtime timeout. Proof receipt: `B11_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `codex_probe_fixture=<cmd>:PASS claude_probe_fixture=<cmd>:PASS q03g_integration=<yes|blocked:reason> context_drift_test=<cmd>:PASS commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[codex-parity-validation]`.

### Doctrine references

L65, L67, L69, L70; `flywheel-2p25`; `flywheel-q03g`; `flywheel-orx1`; `/tmp/orx1-h1-vs-h3-diagnosis.md`.

### Out of scope

No implementation of the full parity probe binary if `flywheel-q03g` owns it; no Codex hook installer symmetry work owned by `flywheel-sx1e`.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B03, B04.  
Blocks: B12.  
Related existing epic: `flywheel-2p25`.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: `flywheel-q03g` if final implementation requires the parity probe binary before Codex/Claude matrix checks can run. Also blocked by Lane B-prime if Jeff substrate provides a better callback/probe primitive.

### Phase 3 audit amendments before creation

Audit sources: `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:37`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:49`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:78`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:80`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:117`.

- Raw shell pass plus agent-context fail must increment `agent_context_probe_drift_count` and block active-runtime parity compliance.
- Fixture-only work may prove schemas and packet rendering only. Any claim that an active runtime is parity-compliant requires `flywheel-q03g` or equivalent live in-agent probe.
- B11 depends on B13 where prompt capture parity is part of the proof.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B11_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B11_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B11_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B11_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B11_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B11_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B11_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG8` closes with `B11_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.
