description:
REPLACES the 5 mis-decomposed Section sub-beads (6.2-6.6: 0h6ko, tdeft, jzndo, 4ivbe, wekpa) per flywheel-rusvs reshape rationale.

Extract the doctor_field_aggregator jq invocation (lines ~1697-1791 in part-02-portable_doctor.sh, slightly shifted post-luzk7) into new portable_doctor.d/02-doctor-field-aggregator.sh (~100 lines).

Scope: the entire jq aggregator block — def lrule, def primitive, helper defs, then Sections A-G as jq pipe-fragments (15 lrule calls + 11 primitive calls + 35 deferred-fields across Sections C/D/E/F/G).

Pattern: pure functional shell (NO dynamic-scoping caller-locals like 6.1; this one is stdin/stdout transformation):

In helper:
- function _portable_doctor_apply_field_aggregator takes packet as arg, runs jq aggregator, returns augmented packet on stdout

In caller (entry portable_doctor):
- packet = result of calling _portable_doctor_apply_field_aggregator with current packet

Acceptance:
- New file portable_doctor.d/02-doctor-field-aggregator.sh exists (~100 lines)
- Entry sources it before the jq invocation site
- Inline jq aggregator block replaced with single function call
- tests/part-02-portable_doctor_parity_fixture.sh PASSES 8/8 (already calibrated to multi-file search by 6.1)
- bash -n clean on both files
- Entry line count drops ~95
- All 30+ Section A-G fields still appear in the output JSON (behavioral parity verifiable via synthesized invocation against synthetic packet)

Pattern proven: 6.1 (luzk7) used dynamic-scoping for caller-local population; this one uses pure functional shell. Sub-beads 6.7 (blmd8) and 6.8 (08jug) will use dynamic-scoping again for the case-statement scoped-probe blocks.

Parity oracle: tests/part-02-portable_doctor_parity_fixture.sh. Parent: flywheel-rusvs. Plan ref: .flywheel/audit/flywheel-hzsro/split-plan.md File 3 (with reshape applied per parent bead).

title: [jeff-substrate] track FrankenTerm release + index with Jeff corpus
description:
## Source

Joshua signal 2026-05-08T11:56Z+ (Jeff Emanuel tweets):
- Jeff: "Man, I can't finish FrankenTerm fast enough... WezTerm is killing my machine every time I go away for a while."
- Jeff: "(FrankenTerm is my enhanced fork of WezTerm that integrates lots of improvements for handling massive agent swarms without leaking crazy memory or glitching)."
- Jeff: "I'm using it actively now alongside WezTerm so it's getting close."
- Joshua: "thoughts on this? we should have frankenterm stuff indexed with jeff corpus"

## Why this matters

Frankenstack lineage already proven: NTM, beads_rust, frankensqlite, frankentui, agent-mail are all Jeff's tools we depend on. FrankenTerm is the next substrate Joshua's fleet will likely adopt.

WezTerm pain Jeff describes mirrors what we'd see at scale (memory leaks during agent-swarm work, glitching after long sessions). Joshua's fleet runs many codex panes 24/7 — exactly the failure mode FrankenTerm targets.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Add FrankenTerm to `~/.claude/skills/dicklesworthstone-stack/references/INVENTORY.md` watchlist. Repo name TBD when public; likely `Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm` based on Jeff's naming pattern (frankensqlite, frankentui).

AG2: Extend `.flywheel/scripts/jeff-binary-version-watchtower.sh` to monitor for FrankenTerm release. Add to checklist of repos checked daily.

AG3: Extend the daily Jeff intel scheduled runner (`ai.zeststream.flywheel-daily-jeff-ingest.plist`) to include FrankenTerm GitHub once released. Auto-clone + index via socraticode the moment it's public.

AG4: Author migration plan when FrankenTerm hits v0.1+: WezTerm → FrankenTerm transition path for the 4 fleet sessions (flywheel, alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, skillos) running multi-pane codex orchestration.

AG5: File followup bead for adoption when public — block AG4 on FrankenTerm release announcement.

## Watch surface

- Jeff's GitHub: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone (regen-sources-from-gh.sh already polls this)
- Jeff's X feed (@doodlestein) — daily-jeff-ingest already monitors
- Likely repo names: frankenterm, franken-term, terminal — add all three to watchtower

## Cross-references

- Memory: `reference_jeff_substrate_inventory.md`
- Memory: `feedback_jeff_substrate_version_drift.md`
- Skill: `dicklesworthstone-stack`
- Skill: `wezterm` (current terminal skill — will need migration counterpart)
- Skill: `info-source-watchtower`
- Daily ingest: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.flywheel-daily-jeff-ingest.plist`
- Watchtower: `.flywheel/scripts/jeff-binary-version-watchtower.sh`
close_reason: Score 9/10. AUTONOMY: autonomous. All 5 AGs met: AG1 inventory+sources contain frankenterm; AG2 jeff-binary-version-watchtower emits frankenterm_release watchlist; AG3 Jeff corpus path indexed at /Users/josh/Developer/jeff-corpus/frankenterm and daily ingest/source regen cover it; AG4 v0.1+ migration plan authored in .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-ubrb5-frankenterm-watchtower-evidence.md; AG5 follow-up adoption bead flywheel-g6xaw created and depends on flywheel-ubrb5. Tests: bash tests/jeff-binary-version-watchtower.sh PASS 11/11; br dep cycles clean.

title: [gap-hunt-probe-self-ref-clearance] wired-but-cold class corpus 1 reads gap-hunt.jsonl which contains the probe's own auto-filed gap_ids — script names self-clear after first run
description:
Discovered during flywheel-2xdi.104 worker-tick. gap-hunt-probe corpus 1 (recent_ledger_text from ~/.local/state/flywheel/*.jsonl mtime <30d) INCLUDES gap-hunt.jsonl — the probe's OWN findings ledger. After first auto-bead-filing run, the flagged script's name appears in gap-hunt.jsonl's gap_ids entry. Subsequent probe runs match name-in-corpus-1 and clear the wired-but-cold flag, even though the script is canonically orphan in doctrine/SKILL.md/scaffold/launchd corpora.

Sister-but-distinct from xbsd8 (which captures memory-without-cross-link class name-grep-only blind spot). This is a NEW class: wired-but-cold class self-ref clearance via own-findings ledger contamination.

Empirical evidence (flywheel-2xdi.104 probe):
- build-spend-ledger-rust.sh is canonically cold (no SKILL.md/scaffold/launchd citation)
- But probe currently does NOT flag it because gap-hunt.jsonl contains its own past flag entries
- 11th distinct posterior shape this session: probe-self-clears-via-own-findings-ledger

Cross-source-silos class already allowlists gap-hunt.jsonl + gap-hunt-false-positives.jsonl in cross_source_silos_allowlist (.flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh:1582). The wired-but-cold class needs sister allowlist:
- Exclude gap-hunt.jsonl from recent_ledger_text() OR
- Strip gap-hunt.jsonl's own gap_ids entries from the corpus before name-match OR
- Skip-clear if the only matching ledger is gap-hunt.jsonl itself (matched-only-by-self check)

Parent: flywheel-2xdi (gap-hunt-probe substrate). Per faqj2 substrate-self-improving loop, this finding-type candidate (`wired_but_cold_corpus1_self_ref`) could be added to gap-hunt-probe-self-calibration.sh Phase 2 finding-type taxonomy.

L52 receipt for parent bead flywheel-2xdi.104.
close_reason: done

title: [D] version ntm spawn templates
description:
## Goal
Track ntm spawn-template SHAs against ntm version and regenerate or warn on version drift.

## Context
ntm upgrades may break worker spawn templates baked at install time.

## Dependencies

## Inputs / Outputs
Files in scope: ~/.config/ntm/spawn-templates; flywheel doctor invariant; template registry
Output artifact: committed implementation and/or evidence artifact named in acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria
- doctor invariant ntm:spawn-templates-versioned returns green or actionable warn with template SHA/version matrix

## Testing Obligations
- Run template hash collection and compare against ntm version; test with a temp modified template fixture.

## Origin
00-MASTER-PLAN.md §IV.6 lines 359-364.

## Out of Scope
Do not overwrite user templates without explicit regeneration command.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: include [D2] in the commit message or close reason.
- Acceptance evidence is captured in the bead closeout.
- Dependency graph remains acyclic after close.

title: phase-C-byte-identical-synthesis-supersede-predicate-mirror-plus-audit-machinery-hygiene-v0.1.8-sd-enrollment
description:
**Phase C of skillos pack-feedback cadence-loop false-up coordination (cross-orch v1.0.0).**

Source: skillos:1 handoff 2026-05-11T04:38Z (commits 974fb36 + 62823a4 + 7f938ba). Phase A + B shipped same-arc; first measured finding_to_pack_update_cadence_p50 = 49.76h baseline (GOAL rev-5 transition: undefined → measured ✓). My ratification at 2026-05-11T04:35Z committed Phase C within 6h default-accept window (deadline 2026-05-11T10:35Z).

**Surface audit (completed 2026-05-11T~04:42Z):**

Flywheel has NO pack_synthesis_receipt schema, NO pack_feedback/cadence_metric code, NO synthesis-receipts.jsonl. The skillos infrastructure is unique to skillos's mission-fidelity-substrate pack. Phase C is therefore primarily:

1. **Doctrine fold-in** — enroll the canonical predicate "synthesis-supersede surfaces require citation verification, not timestamp comparison" as fleet-wide rule.
2. **SD enrollment** — `sd-synthesis-supersede-timestamp-only-false-up` in audit-machinery-hygiene-discipline v0.1.8 (Shape A at synthesis layer).
3. **Future-readiness** — if flywheel ever ingests synthesis receipts (e.g., from skillos packs or its own pack-feedback substrate), the predicate is canonically defined.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [ ] AG1: audit-machinery-hygiene-discipline.md authored to v0.1.8 byte-identical with skillos's v0.1.8 (once skillos publishes mirror). Enrolls `sd-synthesis-supersede-timestamp-only-false-up` as Shape A skill discovery.
- [ ] AG2: Operator-responsibility statement added to doctrine: "synthesis-supersede surfaces require citation verification, not timestamp comparison" (applies fleet-wide: mission_claim_unwired AND blocker resolution AND doctor subsystem transitions AND bead state updates).
- [ ] AG3: Cite skillos commits 974fb36 + 7f938ba + 62823a4 as canonical reference implementations.
- [ ] AG4: Sister-check passes: `shasum -a 256 .flywheel/doctrine/audit-machinery-hygiene-discipline.md` matches skillos's v0.1.8 sha256 byte-identical.
- [ ] AG5: Closeout handoff to skillos:1 confirming v0.1.8 mirror cycle complete; symmetric ratification request.

**Anti-pattern guard:**

Do NOT author skillos-specific schema/code into flywheel (no `.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/skillos.pack_synthesis_receipt.v1.schema.json`, no `mission_claim_parser.py`). Flywheel doesn't have the consuming surfaces yet; mirroring those would be premature substrate. The byte-identical adoption applies only when/if flywheel grows the consuming surfaces. The doctrine + SD enrollment is the load-bearing canonical fold-in.

**Mission anchor:** `80a15c4368187483a6ba91a904248e10266233fb4d14f301b277f9a6c1a12d0a` (matched bilaterally).

**Default-accept window:** 2026-05-11T10:35Z (5h57m from filing).

**Reference handoffs:**

- skillos packet IN: `skillos:/Users/josh/Developer/skillos/.flywheel/handoffs/20260511T0425Z-from-skillos-1-to-flywheel-1-pack-feedback-cadence-loop-false-up-and-fleet-wide-mission-claim-gate-wiring.md`
- flywheel ratification OUT: `flywheel:/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/handoffs/2026-05-11T043500Z-from-flywheel-1-to-skillos-1-pack-feedback-cadence-loop-false-up-RATIFIED-plus-skill-discovery-enrollment.md`
- skillos Phase A+B shipped: `skillos:/Users/josh/Developer/skillos/.flywheel/handoffs/20260511T0438Z-from-skillos-1-to-flywheel-1-phase-A-B-shipped-first-cadence-baseline-49h-plus-phase-C-handoff.md`

**Estimate:** ~30-45min (doctrine authoring + sister-byte-check + closeout handoff). Lower-bound because no code change.

title: [file-length-discipline] extend canonical-cli-scoping with file-length rule + auto-route to language best-practices
description:
[file-length-discipline] extend canonical-cli-scoping with file-length rule + auto-route to language best-practices

## Trauma class
`monolithic-file-debt-accumulating` — Joshua flagged 2026-05-04T00:02Z: "our flywheel loop is getting to be a really long file - we should probably start breaking down long files and build that into our /canonical-cli-scoping process - any file over x lines needs to be broken down using /python-best-practices or /rust-best-practices depending on language"

## Ground truth probe
- `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop`: **6,353 lines** (the worst offender)
- `.flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh`: 1,036 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/frozen-pane-detector.sh`: 1,001 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/daily-jeff-ingest.sh`: 766 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/flywheel-onboard.sh`: 678 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/vc-observability-probe.sh`: 667 lines
- `AGENTS.md`: 1,222 lines (doctrine, may be acceptable)
- 5 scripts >500 lines, 8 scripts >300 lines

Existing canonical-cli-scoping / python-best-practices / rust-best-practices skills have NO line-length rule.

## Goal
Two-phase work:

**Phase A — extend canonical-cli-scoping with file-length rule.**
- Add explicit threshold: files >X lines must be broken down (proposed: bash >500, python >400, rust >500, doctrine markdown >1500)
- Add auto-routing: bash files → recommend extracting library/library + main; python → /python-best-practices; rust → /rust-best-practices
- Add doctor signal `oversized_files_count` to flywheel-loop doctor JSON (lists files exceeding their language threshold)
- Auto-promote to bead when count > 3

**Phase B — refactor flywheel-loop binary (the worst case).**
- 6,353-line bash binary almost certainly has natural seams (subcommands listed in --help: init, doctor, tick, finalize-state-lock, validate-receipt, fleet, roster, check, register-session, verdict, blocks, fuckup log/list/triage/harvest, data-backed-deferral-check)
- Extract per-subcommand into `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop-<subcommand>` or library functions sourced from a central file
- Preserve `flywheel-loop` as thin dispatcher
- All existing tests must pass (no behavior change)

## Acceptance gates
1. canonical-cli-scoping SKILL.md gains "File-length thresholds" section with: bash 500, python 400, rust 500, markdown 1500 (justifications) + override pattern (`# canonical-cli-scoping-allow-large: <reason>` comment for legitimate exceptions like generated code or doctrine)
2. python-best-practices and rust-best-practices SKILL.md cross-reference canonical-cli-scoping file-length rule
3. New `.flywheel/scripts/file-length-probe.sh` returns JSON with files exceeding threshold
4. flywheel-loop doctor JSON gains `oversized_files_count` and `oversized_files` array
5. doctor-signal-bead-promotion auto-files `monolithic-file-debt` bead when count > 3
6. canonical-paths.txt entry for new probe + rule
7. Tests in tests/file-length-probe.sh: fixtures for bash-over, python-over, rust-over, allowed-override, doctrine-markdown-allowed
8. Phase B: flywheel-loop binary split into sub-files, all existing tests still pass, no regression in any tick
9. AGENTS.md L? mentions file-length discipline (proposed L72)

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: probe runs; all sub-extracted files retain test coverage; flywheel-loop tick still works
- DOCUMENTED: skills updated; AGENTS.md L72; canonical-paths
- SURFACED: doctor signal; auto-bead promotion

## Why P0
- 6,353-line binary is brittle: every bead in this session that touched flywheel-loop (B04 doctor signals, B05 VALIDATE phase, B08 ticks_punted_count) had to navigate the monolith
- File-length debt compounds — every new feature gets harder to land
- Companion to L71 validate-and-redispatch: if files are too big, validation/refactor velocity drops

## Doctrine refs
- skill canonical-cli-scoping (extend, primary)
- skill python-best-practices
- skill rust-best-practices
- skill simplify (existing — may have prior art)
- skill simplify-and-refactor-code-isomorphically
- skill code-simplifier
- ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop (Phase B target)
- AGENTS.md L48 (no commits — refactor stays uncommitted, Joshua reviews)
- AGENTS.md L70 (no-punt — refactor IS the work)
- AGENTS.md L71 (validate-and-redispatch — extracted modules need tests)

## Skills cited
- canonical-cli-scoping (primary extension target)
- python-best-practices
- rust-best-practices
- simplify
- code-simplifier
- testing-conformance-harnesses

title: [file-length-discipline] split flywheel-loop into thin dispatcher and sourced modules
description:
## Gap from flywheel-useh acceptance gate 8 (UPDATED 2026-05-08)

Phase A shipped the canonical file-length rule. Phase B (this bead) is split monolith into thin dispatcher + sourced modules.

## REGRESSION SCOPE (2026-05-06 to 2026-05-08)

The lib/ split was authored 2026-05-06 22:54-22:56 (14 files in ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/lib/), but bin/flywheel-loop was NEVER replaced with the thin dispatcher. Workers added new logic to bin/ (today: 1l1z dispatch-contract additions, prior: doctor/tick/portable subsystem). Current state:

- bin/flywheel-loop: 12,511 lines / 565 KB (grew from ~7.8k at original split)
- lib/*.sh: 14 modules totaling ~199 KB, 171 functions
- 30+ functions in bin/ NOT in lib/ — the entire "portable_*" subsystem (portable_doctor, portable_fleet, portable_tick, portable_audit, portable_validate_callback, portable_dispatch_contract, portable_fuckup_*, portable_register_session, portable_repair, portable_state_mine, portable_health, portable_init, portable_quickstart, etc.)
- 9 functions in lib/ NOT in bin/ — fw_classify_source, fw_event, fw_log, fw_normalize_url, fw_now_ms, fw_quote, fw_quote_or_null, fw_require_db, fw_sql (orphaned helpers)
- 22 .bak files at bin/flywheel-loop.bak.* growing 35k -> 416k

## Acceptance gates (UPDATED)

1. Map flywheel-loop functions/subcommands into modules with a before/after inventory. Reconcile bin/ "portable_*" subsystem with lib/ — decide canonical location for each (probably new lib/portable.sh).
2. Preserve ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop as the stable thin dispatcher entrypoint (target: <500 lines).
3. Extract doctor/tick/identity/fleet/fuckup/validation/portable clusters into sourced libraries with no behavior drift. lib/doctor.sh (currently 36k) and lib/misc.sh (currently 29k) ALSO need further splitting per gate 7.
4. Run bash -n on dispatcher and every extracted shell module.
5. Run tests/file-length-probe.sh, tests/doctor-validation-signals.sh, tests/flywheel-loop-core.sh, tests/validate-tick-phase.sh, tests/validation-e2e.sh — record any pre-existing failing blockers verbatim, do not fake-pass.
6. flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json still exposes oversized_files_count, file_length, dispatch_contract_violations, and ALL pre-existing doctor fields.
7. After split, no extracted shell module exceeds 500 lines unless it carries canonical-cli-scoping-allow-large with a reviewed reason.
8. (NEW) Archive 22 bin/flywheel-loop.bak.* files into a single tarball at bin/archive/flywheel-loop-bak-YYYY-MM-DD.tar.gz then delete originals. Bundle in same commit as the final dispatcher swap.
9. (NEW) Add a doctor invariant `monolith_size_regression`: fail if bin/flywheel-loop > 500 lines after this bead closes. This is the structure-level leverage point that prevents recurrence (Donella).

## DOD
Close with DID/DIDNT/GAPS receipt, test evidence paths, and no behavior regression in tick/doctor/callback/portable. Commit message tag when Joshua commits: flywheel-useh.1.

Mission: a flywheel rule that exists but isn't enforced is structure debt. L128 says "plans must prove convergence with data" — file-length L83 says modules must stay small. The doctor invariant in gate 9 is what makes the rule enforceable. Without it, the next worker adds 200 lines to bin/ tomorrow.

## Dependencies
Depends on flywheel-useh Phase A file-length substrate.
close_reason: DID: split flywheel-loop into a 236-line dispatcher sourced from lib modules; added MIGRATION-MAP.md for 197 functions; wired dispatch_contract_violations and monolith_size_regression doctor fields; archived 25 backup files into bin/archive/flywheel-loop-bak-2026-05-08.tar.gz; added regression test. DIDNT: fully decompose legacy large lib modules beyond reviewed canonical-cli-scoping-allow-large exceptions. GAPS: follow-up module-level decomposition remains for portable/core, identity, misc, repo, doctor, wire, loop, deferral, fuckup once parity fixtures are broader. Evidence: /tmp/flywheel-useh1-compliance-pack.md

title: [L-rule] PUBLIC-READY-DEFAULT — all repos audited/scored as if public, regardless of private status
description:
## Joshua directive 2026-05-08T21:26Z

> "I want all repos to be set up as if they were public — even if they are private — if I decide to flip the switch one day, it needs to be ready."

## Exemption taxonomy (CLARIFIED)

Three exemption classes — only ONE is now deprecated:

| Class | Status under PUBLIC-READY-DEFAULT |
|---|---|
| **private_internal** (Joshua-owned, currently private) | **DEPRECATED** — re-audit per flywheel-sib04. Joshua wants public-ready posture regardless of current visibility. |
| **EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED** (client deliverable, client owns public posture) | **KEEP EXEMPT** — ZestStream voice gate doesn't apply because the surface isn't ZestStream-owned. Client decides their own public posture. Examples: alpsinsurance, mobile-eats client deliverables. |
| **EXEMPT_PUBLIC_FACING** (already public, no scoring needed because already passed gate) | **KEEP** — no-op for already-public surfaces. |

## Precision rule

The directive applies to surfaces where ZestStream brand-voice IS the canonical voice — i.e. flywheel, skillos, zeststream-infra, picoz, polymarket-pico-z, vrtx (Joshua-owned/internal). Client-owned surfaces (alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, etc) maintain their EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED status.

## Acceptance gates (UNCHANGED, scope refined)

AG1: Author canonical L-rule PUBLIC-READY-DEFAULT in .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md.
AG2: Update publishability-bar.sh + L89 voice-gate scripts: remove `private_internal` short-circuit, KEEP `EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED` and `EXEMPT_PUBLIC_FACING` short-circuits.
AG3: Re-audit per flywheel-sib04 — only the `private_internal` closes (lzc6, f3s7, u1zd, 1t8t, lzw7), NOT the `EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED` closes (wrjv etc).
AG4: Propagate via doctrine-sync 6h cadence — all 57 fleet repos pick up the L-rule + script changes.
AG5: Update L89 trigger language to reflect three-way exemption taxonomy.

## Why this matters

- Optionality: Joshua can flip private→public on Joshua-owned repos without scrambling
- Brand discipline: ZestStream voice + grounding applies to Joshua-owned, not client-owned
- Quality consistency: same bar for same class
- Client respect: client-owned voice posture stays client-owned
close_reason: Implemented PUBLIC-READY-DEFAULT: L148 three-surface doctrine, L89 taxonomy update, private repo scanner enforcement, explicit exemption preservation, doctrine sync allowlist, tests, and compliance pack.

description:

Subject: 1 monolithic `portable_doctor()` function, 58 `local` declarations, 17+ inline sub-probes, 4 explicit exit calls, 65 JSON_OUT references.

Canonical motion (split-plan File 3, lines 119-130):
- entry `part-02-portable_doctor.sh` (~150 lines): parse args + dispatch + aggregate
- new dir `portable_doctor.d/` with 8 sub-files:
  1. `01-arg-parse.sh` (~50 lines): strict, fix, scope, storage thresholds
  2. `02-scoped-probes-pre.sh` (~250 lines): 11 scoped_doctor calls (auto-l112, quality-bar-close-gate, watcher-isomorphic, stale-in-progress, jsm-sandbox-auth, substrate-loop-contract, storage-headroom, peer-orch-respawn, peer-orch-freeze, codex-template-stuck, callback-envelope)
  3. `03-scoped-probes-mid.sh` (~200 lines): 6 more scoped_doctor calls (tick-hook-firing, flywheel-tick-driver, flywheel-loop-driver-writeback, l70-ticks-punted, agents-md-fleet-propagator, beads-db-recover)
  4. `04-section-a-l-rule-fields.sh` (~150 lines): Section A
  5. `05-section-b-substrate-primitives.sh` (~120 lines): Section B
  6. `06-section-c-quality-bar.sh` (~80 lines): Section C
  7. `07-section-de-propagation-plan-skill.sh` (~150 lines): Sections D + E
  8. `08-section-fg-comms-session.sh` (~80 lines): Sections F + G

Each sub-file = own bead (8 beads total). Risk: shell function-body extraction with `local`-variable contract preservation; if `local` is scoped to the outer function, extracted sub-files must either (a) accept those locals as args, (b) use `declare -g` in caller and unset after, or (c) source-with-shared-scope. Plan caveat (line 135): behavior fixtures essential to catch state-leak regressions.

title: [operator-library-auto-route] inject cognitive operators (ORIENT/MOTIVATE/MENTAL-MODEL/EXEMPLIFY/WARN/CROSS-LINK) for doc-authoring beads, sister to pmg3c forward-link
description:
## Source: documentation-website-for-software-project skill / OPERATOR-LIBRARY.md
Extends the pmg3c forward-link-recipe auto-injection pattern to apply cognitive operators per the docs-website skill operator library.

## Operators (from OPERATOR-LIBRARY.md)
- **★ ORIENT** — first 3 paragraphs: what/who/where
- **✦ MOTIVATE** — why does this exist? what problem solved?
- **◐ MENTAL-MODEL** — diagram/sketch showing relationships
- **⬡ EXEMPLIFY** — copy-pasteable runnable example
- **⚠ WARN** — anti-patterns / pitfalls / gotchas
- **✧ TIP** — non-obvious insight
- **⇄ CROSS-LINK** — links to related surfaces
- **⤵ DECOMPOSE** — break complex idea into parts
- **⊕ SYNTHESIZE** — merge fragments into coherent whole
- **⊙ DE-SLOP** — remove fluff, tighten prose
- **⌘ REDUCE** — cut by 30%+

## Auto-route triggers
When bead title prefix matches:
- `[doctrine]` or `[gap-memory-without-cross-link]` → inject pipeline: ★ ORIENT → ✦ MOTIVATE → ◐ MENTAL-MODEL → ⬡ EXEMPLIFY → ⚠ WARN → ⇄ CROSS-LINK
- `[skill-md]` or `[skill-promotion]` → inject pipeline: ★ ORIENT → ✦ MOTIVATE → ⬡ EXEMPLIFY → ⚠ WARN → ⇄ CROSS-LINK → ⌘ REDUCE
- `[client-doc-*]` → inject pipeline: ★ ORIENT → ⬡ EXEMPLIFY → ⚠ WARN → ⇄ CROSS-LINK
- `[readme]` → inject pipeline: ★ ORIENT → ✦ MOTIVATE → ⬡ EXEMPLIFY → ⌘ REDUCE

## Build
- `.flywheel/scripts/inject-operator-library-recipe.sh`
  - INPUT: task body + bead title
  - OUTPUT: task body with `## OPERATOR LIBRARY RECIPE BLOCK` section injected, citing relevant operators with one-line description each
- Wire into `.flywheel/scripts/build-dispatch-packet.sh` similar to inject-forward-link-recipe.sh

## Source attribution
Citing `~/.claude/skills/documentation-website-for-software-project/references/OPERATOR-LIBRARY.md` as canonical. Doctrine doc at `.flywheel/doctrine/operator-library-recipe.md` (sister to forward-link-doctrine-doc-recipe.md).

## Acceptance
- [ ] Injector script written + tests pass
- [ ] build-dispatch-packet.sh wires injector for `[doctrine]`/`[skill-md]`/`[client-doc-*]`/`[readme]` title classes
- [ ] Doctrine doc at .flywheel/doctrine/operator-library-recipe.md (sister to forward-link recipe)
- [ ] Live test: dispatch a `[doctrine]`-class bead, verify packet contains OPERATOR LIBRARY RECIPE BLOCK
- [ ] Per-class operator pipeline documented in doctrine doc

## Sister to pmg3c
pmg3c shipped forward-link-doctrine-doc-recipe auto-injector. This adds cognitive-operator auto-injection. Together: dispatch packet pre-loaded with both "what fix shape" (forward-link) and "how to write" (operators).
close_reason: done

title: [orch-blocker-coordination] orchestrators MUST coordinate with flywheel:1 on blockers — never sit idle
description:
[orch-blocker-coordination] orchestrators MUST coordinate with flywheel:1 on blockers — never sit idle

## Trauma class
`peer-orch-idle-on-blocker` — Joshua flagged 2026-05-04T00:31Z: "we need orchestrator rules that say - when you've gotten a flywheel blocker - work with pane 1 of flywheel to address all of it - they can't sit idle due to a blocker"

## Evidence (this session, 3 documented occurrences)
1. **mobile-eats:1** (2026-05-03T23:58Z) — INTEGRATE blocked by `canonical_doctrine_drift_local`. Orch logged blocker receipt + idled awaiting pane 2 callback. Did NOT proactively coordinate with flywheel:1. Flywheel:1 had to PROBE directly + run sync-canonical-doctrine.sh to unblock. Result: 30+ min idle time.
2. **skillos:1** (2026-05-04T00:08Z) — same `canonical_doctrine_drift_local` blocker. Sent xpane packet to flywheel:1 (good!) BUT bounced auth-marker contract back to Joshua initially. Joshua redirected: "work with pane 1 of flywheel not me." Required Meadows analysis + ratification by flywheel:1 (#5 RULES leverage).
3. **mobile-eats:1** (2026-05-03T22:28Z) — L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT pattern: orch named next phase + idled awaiting next launchd tick instead of executing. Required cross-orch directive + mechanical gate (flywheel-zdva ticks_punted_count).

## Root cause
No canonical rule says: "flywheel-class blockers → coordinate with flywheel:1 immediately." Each peer-orch invents its own protocol. Some bounce to Joshua (skillos initial), some idle (mobile-eats), some send xpane packets (skillos eventual). Reminder substitution per ANTI-PATTERNS.md #3.

## Meadows leverage point
**#5 RULES** — formalize the coordination contract. Sibling to L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT, L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE, auth-marker/v1 (yesterday).

## What "flywheel-class blocker" means
A blocker that:
- Originates from doctrine/substrate flywheel:1 owns (canonical_doctrine_drift, missing L-rule, missing doctor signal, missing canonical contract, missing skill, etc.)
- OR can be unblocked by file edits across repos / system-wide rule ratification (NOT worker dispatch into peer's repo)
- OR matches existing trauma class with known fix in flywheel-orch's scope

NOT flywheel-class: peer-orch's own worker dispatch, peer-orch's own mission scope, peer-orch's own taste calls within their repo (mission/goal/state docs).

## Acceptance gates
1. AGENTS.md gets L75 ORCH-BLOCKER-COORDINATION rule with explicit "if blocker is flywheel-class → ntm send flywheel --pane=<orch-pane> WITHIN 5min, never idle waiting" enforcement
2. ft04 sync mechanism propagates L75 to all 17 flywheel-installed repos
3. flywheel-loop doctor JSON gains `peer_orch_blocker_age_seconds` field per recent blocker observation
4. tick driver detects: if peer-orch session has reported blocker via cross-orch-coordination.jsonl AND >5min elapsed AND no flywheel:1 ack, flag as `peer_orch_idle_on_blocker` and auto-promote to bead
5. Cross-orch-coordination.jsonl schema gets `blocker_type` field with values: `flywheel_class | peer_class | external | unknown`
6. New script `.flywheel/scripts/peer-orch-blocker-watch.sh` scans coordination ledger, surfaces stale blockers
7. xpane packet template (skill `agent-mail` or new): canonical shape with `blocker_class`, `requested_owner`, `proposed_action`, `flywheel_orch_action_required`
8. Worker dispatch packet template extended (canonical-cli-scoping) with `if_blocker_class=flywheel_class then xpane_to_flywheel_pane_1` boilerplate
9. Tests in tests/ for: idle-on-blocker detection, xpane-packet schema validation, age-threshold tripping
10. canonical-paths.txt entry

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests pass; doctor signal active; auto-promotion fires on simulated stale blocker
- DOCUMENTED: AGENTS.md L75; xpane packet template; coordination ledger schema versioned
- SURFACED: doctor signal + daily-report (flywheel-o7dq companion) + notify (Pushover) on stale-blocker

## Doctrine refs
- AGENTS.md L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT (sibling — no idle waiting for next tick)
- AGENTS.md L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE
- Memory: feedback_orchestrator_must_dispatch.md (don't punt to Joshua)
- Memory: feedback_orch_paralysis_recurring.md (orch paralysis = failure mode)
- Memory: feedback_orchestrator_scope_boundary.md (workers-only boundary; files/coordination IN scope)
- Memory: feedback_meadows_rules_unblock_paradigm_intact.md (Meadows #5 leverage pattern)
- bead flywheel-7lby (parent — orch-no-punt mechanical gate)
- bead flywheel-zdva CLOSED (ticks_punted_count signal)
- bead flywheel-ft04 CLOSED (propagation mechanism)
- ~/.local/state/flywheel/cross-orch-coordination.jsonl (ledger)

## L-rule candidate
**L75 ORCH-BLOCKER-COORDINATION** — when peer-orch encounters flywheel-class blocker, MUST send xpane to flywheel:1 within 5min. Idle-while-blocked is failure mode.

## Companion fleet rules (already shipped or in-flight)
- L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT (closed via flywheel-zdva)
- L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE (closed via flywheel-dw5w)
- L72 candidate FILE-LENGTH-DISCIPLINE (in flight via flywheel-useh)
- L73 candidate FIRST-AUTH-MARKER-RECEIPT (ratified via auth-marker/v1)
- **L75 ORCH-BLOCKER-COORDINATION (this bead)**

## Skills cited
- canonical-cli-scoping (xpane packet shape)
- agent-mail (cross-orch routing)
- agent-fleet-management
- testing-conformance-harnesses (idle-blocker detection tests)
- system-health (doctor signal pattern)

## Why P0
- 3 documented occurrences this session = recurring trauma
- Each peer-orch idle minute is dispatch capacity wasted fleet-wide
- Joshua explicitly flagged: "they can't sit idle due to a blocker"
notes: 2026-05-04T06:26:28Z: four-lens BLOCK_CLOSE. did=10/10 tests=PASS but public_lens=no_bar_self_grade — evidence enumerates gates but doesn't self-grade against Three Judges/publishability/brand-voice/Jeff/Donella. NOT closing. brand+sniff+jeff PASS. Light rework needed.

title: [fleet-onboarding] canonical-cli-scoping process for new-repo onboarding/sync/stamp
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~08:05Z: 'we also need a full mapped /canonical-cli-scoping process for onboarding new repos into the flywheel. I have a lot of sessions. We need full scoped onboarding, sync, stamp'

GAP: Current state has no canonical onboarding contract. Each repo (flywheel, skillos, mobile-eats, alps, terra-title, blackfoot, zesttube, zeststream-infra, etc.) was bootstrapped ad-hoc. Result: skillos LIMPING for weeks before tonight's diagnostic; mobile-eats receipt-mirror gap discovered manually 2026-05-03; INCIDENTS distribution gap (flywheel-rx1t) discovered manually. Fleet member contract is implicit, so repos drift into LIMPING by default.

MEADOWS #5 (RULES): Define canonical-cli-scoping contract that EVERY fleet-managed repo MUST satisfy for HEALTHY signals from day 1. Make the implicit explicit, mechanically enforced.

PROPOSED canonical onboarding surface (scoped per canonical-cli-scoping skill):

  /flywheel:onboard <repo-path> [options]
    --dry-run           # full scope check, no mutations
    --doctor            # health probe of existing onboarding
    --info              # what onboarding does + checklist
    --schema            # JSON schema of onboarding contract
    --examples          # sample onboarded-repo inventory
    --json              # machine-readable result
    --stamp             # write canonical stamp to repo
    --sync              # re-sync existing repo to latest fleet-member contract
    --upgrade           # apply latest fleet-member doctrine bumps
    --explain           # show what each step would do + why
    --idempotency-key   # safe re-run

ONBOARDING CONTRACT (required for HEALTHY):
  Tier 1 — Files (substrate):
    [ ] .flywheel/MISSION.md (with mission anchor)
    [ ] .flywheel/GOAL.md (current goal locked)
    [ ] .flywheel/STATE.md (active state)
    [ ] AGENTS.md (canonical L-rules synced from fleet)
    [ ] INCIDENTS.md (selected canonical entries — solves flywheel-rx1t)
    [ ] .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl (worker callback log)
    [ ] .flywheel/ticks/ (receipt directory)

  Tier 2 — Loop driver (mobile-eats-pattern, addresses Joshua escalation tonight):
    [ ] launchd plist registered for canonical loop fire
    [ ] product-specific work payload script (NOT just /flywheel:tick)
    [ ] ledger write path (canonical mirror via flywheel-1naj.1 pattern)
    [ ] receipt write path → ~/.local/state/flywheel-loop/last_tick_<project>.json
    [ ] callback contract → flywheel orchestrator pane via L61

  Tier 3 — Session topology:
    [ ] ntm session created with declared roles
    [ ] session-topology.jsonl entry (orchestrator_pane, callback_pane, worker_panes, human_pane)
    [ ] FoggyBear-equivalent vault token if cross-session L61 needed
    [ ] Agent Mail identity registered per pane

  Tier 4 — Health probes (the doctor surface):
    [ ] flywheel-loop doctor PASS
    [ ] gap-hunter HEALTHY (5/5 loop-integrity signals — flywheel-aucl)
    [ ] mission-lock fresh
    [ ] pane states all reportable via --robot-activity

  Tier 5 — Fleet membership stamp:
    [ ] .flywheel/fleet-member.json with: onboarded_at, contract_version, doctrine_hash, last_sync_at
    [ ] Registered in ~/.local/state/flywheel/fleet-roster.json
    [ ] Daily-sync subscription (gets doctrine drift, INCIDENTS additions, AGENTS.md L-rule updates)

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
  [ ] /flywheel:onboard skill authored at ~/.claude/skills/flywheel/onboard/ (or as /flywheel:onboard slash command)
  [ ] passes canonical-cli-scoping/scripts/check-cli-scoping.sh
  [ ] dry-run mode validated against existing flywheel + mobile-eats (should report 'already-onboarded')
  [ ] dry-run validated against skillos (should report tier-2 gaps matching tonight's diagnostic)
  [ ] dry-run validated against a fresh disposable test repo (should report all 5 tiers missing)
  [ ] stamp mode applied to skillos brings it to HEALTHY within 24h
  [ ] sync mode propagates AGENTS.md + canonical INCIDENTS to existing fleet members
  [ ] daily-sync wires into skillos's new mobile-eats-pattern loop (compose with flywheel-b6js)

DEPENDENCIES:

[heuristic-fix] gap-hunt-probe wired-but-cold: 4MB budget cap creates false positives
description:
## Gap surfaced by flywheel-2xdi.42 (wired-but-cold false positive, 2026-05-09)

The `probe_wired_but_cold()` heuristic in `.flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh:415-433` reads recent jsonl ledgers via `recent_ledger_text(days=30, max_bytes=4_000_000)` (line 355). It iterates `sorted(STATE_DIR.glob('*.jsonl'))` ALPHABETICALLY and accumulates text until 4MB cap.

## False-positive class

When high-volume ledgers consume the 4MB budget early in alphabetical order, later ledgers are unread and their script references are missed.

Concrete evidence (flywheel-2xdi.42, 2026-05-09):
- `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/lib/mission.sh` flagged as wired-but-cold.
- Reality: sourced unconditionally by `bin/flywheel-loop:33-34`, function `mission_lock_age_json` called from `lib/portable/core.d/part-02-portable_doctor.sh:609`.
- 43 dispatch-log.jsonl hits for `mission_lock_age` in the repo (most recent 2026-05-09T17:23:09Z).
- `~/.local/state/flywheel/doctrine-sync-ledger.jsonl` has 1777 `mission` hits but sorts to position 23+ alphabetically; preceded by 1.8MB `agents-md-fleet-propagation.jsonl` + ~1MB `br-db-corruption-monitor-ledger.jsonl` + others. Budget exhausted before reaching the high-signal file.

## Proposed fix (one of)

1. Sort by mtime descending (most recent first), not alphabetical.
2. Use per-file budget (e.g., 50KB head) instead of global cap, so all files get sampled.
3. Add a function-name aware match (parse script for top-level function definitions, search ledgers for those tokens, not just `script.name` / `script.stem`).
4. Cross-check repo-local `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` (currently only `STATE_DIR` ledgers are sampled).

## Definition of Done

- gap-hunt-probe wired-but-cold heuristic stops flagging `lib/mission.sh` as cold.
- Regression test asserts the false-positive case (sourced+called script with high-volume ledger budget pressure) doesn't fire.

## Live evidence (re-runnable)

```bash
# Confirm script is HOT
grep -n 'mission' ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop | head -3
# expected: line 33-34 sources $LIB/mission.sh

grep -c mission_lock_age /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl
# expected: 43+ (most recent 2026-05-09)

# Confirm probe budget is exhausted before doctrine-sync-ledger
ls -laS ~/.local/state/flywheel/*.jsonl | head -5
# expected: large files dominate alphabetical-early positions

# Confirm probe returns false-positive currently
.flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh --dry-run --json | jq -r '.gaps[] | select(.gap_id | test("mission.sh"))'
# current: mission.sh flagged
```

## Boundary

Read-only research bead. Does NOT mutate the probe — that's the closing worker's scope. Filed by flywheel-2xdi.42 close (MistyCliff, 2026-05-09) so the heuristic gap doesn't get re-invented next time the probe fires.
close_reason: done

title: [storage-override-mechanization] mechanize Joshua-disposed storage override receipts — fleet-wide
description:
[storage-override-mechanization] mechanize Joshua-disposed storage override receipts so doctor reads them automatically — fleet-wide

## Trauma class
manual-override-receipt-honor

## Background
2026-05-04T02:10Z fleet storage trauma: peer orchs (skillos, mobile-eats) blocked at 9.91% vs 10% threshold while flywheel:p4 finished jeff-corpus bulk index. flywheel:1 issued Joshua-disposed override receipt at /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/storage-overrides/joshua-disposed-2026-05-04T0210Z.json (min_free_pct_override=8, expires_at=2026-05-04T03:18:56Z, applies_to=[skillos,mobile-eats,alpsinsurance,picoz,flywheel]) and broadcast via xpane. Peer orchs honored it MANUALLY by reading the receipt from disk. This bead mechanizes the read so next time it's automatic.

## Sibling pattern
Same #5 RULES family as auth-marker/v1 — Joshua-disposed bounded override receipt, gate honors it, gate auto-restores on revert. auth-marker schema lives at .flywheel/validation-schema/v1/auth-marker.schema.json.

## Goal
Doctor reads storage-override/v1 receipts automatically. Override path canonized. CLI flag added. Doctor signal exposes when override is in play.

## Acceptance gates

1. AG1: schema canonized at .flywheel/validation-schema/v1/storage-override.schema.json (subset of auth-marker family — required fields: schema_version, issued_at, expires_at, issuer, scope, min_free_pct_override, applies_to, rotation_reason, auto_clear_signal, rollback_guard)
2. AG2: flywheel-loop CLI flag --storage-min-free-pct=N respected when present (also reads FLYWHEEL_STORAGE_MIN_FREE_PCT env var)
3. AG3: flywheel-loop doctor reads /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/storage-overrides/*.json and applies the active receipt automatically (active = not expired AND session in applies_to AND signature_valid_or_optional)
4. AG4: doctor signal storage_override_active_count exposed in JSON (count of active receipts honoring this session)
5. AG5: doctor signal storage_override_expiring_in_min exposed (minutes until earliest expiry)
6. AG6: doctor still fails CLOSED when no receipt is active and disk_free_pct < threshold — overrides are PERMISSIVE not REPLACING
7. AG7: receipt expiry handling — auto-stop applying receipt when expires_at passes; also honor STORAGE-CLEARED event log entry as auto-revert signal
8. AG8: tests/storage-override.sh covers: valid receipt + low disk → pass; expired receipt + low disk → fail; missing applies_to → fail; receipt present but disk above standard threshold → standard path
9. AG9: storage-override receipt schema documented in .flywheel/AGENTS.md as L## (next available L-rule slot, sibling of L74 AGENT-SECURITY-DENY-RULES-CANONICAL)
10. AG10: ft04 propagation extended — sync-canonical-doctrine.sh covers storage-override.schema.json so all 17 flywheel-installed repos honor the same schema

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests/storage-override.sh passes 4 fixture cases; live-fire dry-run honors today's receipt
- DOCUMENTED: schema file + AGENTS.md L-rule + skill update
- SURFACED: doctor signal storage_override_active_count + storage_override_expiring_in_min in JSON; flywheel:status references when overrides are active

## Skills to consult
- canonical-cli-scoping (CLI flag pattern + --info/--examples/help-topic)
- safe-migrations (schema versioning)
- testing-conformance-harnesses (fixture discipline)
- flywheel-doctor-author (doctor signal addition pattern)
- gate-truth-separation (override-vs-bypass distinction — overrides have receipt evidence; bypasses do not)

## Dependencies
- depends-on flywheel-ft04 (CLOSED — propagation pipe)
- sibling of flywheel-2zsj (storage-discipline-system-wide, CLOSED)
- sibling of flywheel-15dg (jeff-corpus-accretive-ingestion, OPEN — same accretive ingestion pattern context)

title: Rewrite Flywheel SMB site story system
description:
## Goal
Define and implement a complete SMB-facing Flywheel public-site story system, grounded in Jeff Emanuel visual patterns and ZestStream brand voice.

## Context
Joshua rejected the current Flywheel page as unusable end to end. The blocker is not copy polish; the site needs a next-generation visual/narrative wireframe and a reusable foundation for ZestStream Next.js projects.

## Dependencies
None. Related public-share readiness beads remain open for release and CI surfaces.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: site/*, docs/runbooks/public-site-smb-journey-wireframe.md, docs/runbooks/public-user-journey-pack.md, tests/website-static.sh, Jeff public reference sites.
OUTPUTS: revised wireframe/doctrine, rebuilt private review site, updated static/journey checks, screenshot/probe evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Public wireframe defines the SMB owner journey before implementation details.
- Site no longer resembles the rejected placeholder page.
- Visual system includes reusable tokens/primitives suitable for broader ZestStream Next.js work.
- Claims stay grounded and private state stays out.
- Static, accessibility, journey-pack, readiness, and screenshot checks pass.

## Testing Obligations
- bash tests/website-static.sh
- bash tests/website-accessibility.sh
- python3 scripts/validate_user_journey_pack.py --json
- python3 scripts/publication_readiness.py --release --json
- Screenshot review for desktop and mobile
- SKILLS: zeststream-brand-voice, de-slopify, deployment-strategy

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: redesign(site): rebuild SMB story system [flywheel-site-story]
- Tests green
- Private review deploy refreshed
- SkillOS notified
- AUTONOMY: autonomous
close_reason: Score 8/10. AUTONOMY: autonomous. Replaced rejected placeholder with SMB operating-room wireframe and static private review build; tests website-static/public-docs/accessibility/user-journey/public-surface/readiness probes pass for site surfaces; private Vercel deploy refreshed; public release blockers intentionally remain.

title: [caam-auto-rotate-test-calibration] test 02 'schema/help surface' asserts wrapper-result schema against canonical-CLI introspection output
description:
PRE-EXISTING failure surfaced during flywheel-023hs (rename .sh→.py); not introduced by the rename. Verified against HEAD blob c457583... — same failure exists pre-rename.

META-RULE 2026-05-09 (calibrate-test-to-actual-contract-before-filing-upstream) class — same shape as flywheel-bgtv8.

Root cause:
`.flywheel/tests/test_caam_auto_rotate_on_usage_limit.sh:69` test 02 asserts wrapper-result-schema field shape on the output of `$SCRIPT --schema --json`:

```
jq_ok '.schema=="caam-auto-rotate-on-usage-limit.result.v1"
       and .native_surface=="ntm rotate"
       and .caam_vault_only==true
       and .ttl_native=="3600s"
       and .ttl_wrapper=="24h_historical_receipt"
       and (.native_wrapper_delta|test("native_ntm_rotate_owns_account_swap"))
       and (.authorized_operations|index("ntm_rotate_preserve_context"))'
```

But the script's --schema surface was repurposed by canonical-CLI scaffolder (flywheel-0pkcf) to emit canonical-CLI INTROSPECTION schema, not wrapper-result schema:

```
{"command":"schema","schema_version":"caam-auto-rotate-on-usage-limit/v1",
 "stable_exit_codes":{...},"surface":"--json","surfaces":[...]}
```

The two schemas have NO overlapping fields. Test 02 calls the wrong surface for what it's asserting.

Fix options:
A) Drop test 02 (canonical-CLI scaffolder test `tests/caam-auto-rotate-on-usage-limit.py-canonical-cli-py.sh` covers --schema canonically; 14/14 PASS)
B) Replace test 02 with a wrapper-result-schema assertion sourced from an actual rotate invocation (run_case + assert on result envelope, similar to test 03/04 pattern)
C) Calibrate assertion to canonical-CLI introspection shape (.schema_version == "caam-auto-rotate-on-usage-limit/v1", .surface, .surfaces)

Recommendation: B (replace with wrapper-result assertion from actual rotate run). Preserves test intent while sourcing from correct surface. Aligns with bgtv8 calibration pattern.

AG1: choose fix option (A/B/C) with rationale
AG2: implement chosen fix (test calibration only; do NOT modify script under test)
AG3: rerun .flywheel/tests/test_caam_auto_rotate_on_usage_limit.sh; assert pass=18 fail=0
AG4: receipt at .flywheel/audit/<this-bead>/evidence.md citing pre-existing nature + bgtv8 sister pattern

Sister pattern: flywheel-bgtv8 closed 2026-05-11 with same META-RULE 2026-05-09 calibration class.
close_reason: test 02 calibrated to wrapper-result envelope via run_case d02 (Option B); --schema --json was repurposed by canonical-CLI scaffolder; META-rule 2026-05-09 (calibrate-test-to-actual-contract); 18/18 PASS (was 17/18); script under test untouched; commit 35ff68e

title: [jeff-corpus-deep-pattern-mining] systematic measure of Jeff's work/quality/methods + accretive bake-in
description:
[jeff-corpus-deep-pattern-mining] systematic measure of Jeff's work/quality/methods + accretive bake-in to flywheel

## Trauma class
underutilized-corpus — 177 Jeff repos / 791K embedding points indexed but not yet mined for patterns. Joshua flagged 2026-05-04: "we need to fucking deep — measure all of his work / quality / methods, etc. and bake them into our flywheel in the most accretive way possible."

## Goal
Systematic cross-corpus mining of Jeff's 177 Dicklesworthstone repos to extract patterns, conventions, quality signals, and architecture across his work — then bake them into flywheel as ADOPT/EXTEND/AVOID rules. Output: `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/` with structured findings + auto-promoted skills/L-rules where applicable.

## Approach (5 phases — accretive, each phase ships before next starts)

### Phase 1: AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / README.md harvest
- For each of 177 repos, extract: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, .gitignore, package.json/Cargo.toml/pyproject.toml
- Aggregate into `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/doctrine-corpus/<repo>.json` per-repo + `index.md`
- Cluster by topic: testing conventions, doctor/health patterns, error handling, idempotency, ipc/transport
- Output: `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/01-doctrine-cluster.md` with N clusters, cited by repo+line

### Phase 2: Code pattern mining via socraticode
- Run socraticode searches across ALL 177 collections (not just one) for high-signal queries:
  - "doctor health repair triad implementation"
  - "idempotency-key handling"
  - "fail-closed error handling"
  - "testing fixture naming conventions"
  - "schema versioning v1 v2 migration"
  - "callback envelope shape"
  - "auto-recovery retry pattern"
  - "audit log append-only"
  - "frontmatter validation"
  - "lock file convention"
- Per-query: top 20 hits, extract pattern, dedupe across repos, count frequency
- Output: `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/02-code-patterns.md` per pattern with adopt/extend/avoid verdict

### Phase 3: Quality-signal scoring
- For each repo, derive quality signals:
  - Test coverage proxy (tests/ dir presence, test file count vs code file count)
  - Doc completeness (README + AGENTS + per-script comments)
  - Schema discipline (validation-schema/ or equivalent)
  - Doctor presence (`*-doctor*` scripts or doctor command)
  - Idempotency markers (idempotency-key, dry-run, --apply gate)
  - Test pass rate if Makefile/CI present
- Rank top 20 + bottom 20 by composite score
- Output: `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/03-quality-ranking.md`

### Phase 4: ADOPT/EXTEND/AVOID synthesis
- Cross-reference Phase 1+2+3 findings with our existing flywheel patterns
- Per pattern: ADOPT (Jeff has it, we don't, should copy), EXTEND (we have, Jeff has improvements), AVOID (Jeff did it differently for known-bad reason), DIVERGE (we deliberately differ, document rationale)
- For each ADOPT: file follow-up bead with implementation scope
- For each EXTEND: file follow-up bead with our-side amendment
- Output: `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/04-adopt-extend-avoid.md` + N follow-up beads

### Phase 5: Skill/L-rule promotion
- Patterns found in ≥10 Jeff repos AND not in flywheel skills become candidate skills
- Auto-promote to `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` draft (worker creates draft, Joshua approves before mv)
- L-rule candidates added to `.flywheel/AGENTS.md` as proposed (under "Candidate L-rules" section, not numbered until ratified)

title: [jeff-corpus-rubric-augment] cross-scan Jeff drafts against full corpus for similarity/prior-art/precedent
description:
[jeff-corpus-rubric-augment] cross-scan Jeff drafts against full corpus for similarity/prior-art/precedent

## Trigger
Joshua flag 2026-05-04T00:34Z: "once we get jeff's entire library indexed, we can add some commentary about scanning his entire repo looking for similarities, etc. to provide even more accretive value to our issue process"

## Goal
Extend `flywheel-3p1j` jeff-issue-rubric (CLOSED) with a 9th axis (or composite signal): cross-corpus similarity/prior-art scan. Before filing any Jeff issue, the rubric queries the full Jeff socraticode corpus (177 repos when complete) for:
- Similar issue patterns (existing issues we could comment on instead of filing new)
- Prior art (Jeff already solved this in another repo)
- Shape preference (Jeff's actual API conventions for this primitive class)
- Anti-patterns Jeff has explicitly rejected

## Why this is accretive
Without cross-corpus check:
- We file issues Jeff already solved → noise, derails his work
- We propose APIs that don't match Jeff's idioms → low signal, high friction
- We miss precedent that would strengthen our rationale

With cross-corpus check:
- Issues cite "Jeff also did X in repo Y line Z, this is consistent with that pattern"
- Anti-patterns in Jeff's own code become implicit warnings
- Higher signal-to-noise per Jeff's mailbox

## Dependencies
- `flywheel-9nhx` library-ingestion: must reach 177/177 indexed first (currently 85/177; 92 skipped_budget)
- `flywheel-3p1j` jeff-issue-rubric: CLOSED — this extends, doesn't replace
- `flywheel-9nhx` Phase B accretive learning extraction: provides query patterns this rubric can reuse

## Acceptance gates
1. Rubric script (`.flywheel/scripts/jeff-issue-rubric.{py,sh}` from B12) gains `--corpus-scan` flag that:
   - Extracts key terms from draft (primitive name, feature, behavior keywords)
   - Runs `mcp__socraticode__codebase_search` across all indexed Jeff repos
   - Surfaces top 10 hits with: repo, file, line, snippet, similarity score
   - Categorizes each hit: same-issue-already-filed / prior-art / shape-precedent / anti-pattern
2. Output includes per-category citation block injected into the issue body
3. New rubric axis: `corpus_aware` (passes if --corpus-scan ran AND draft cites at least one corpus reference in body)
4. Threshold: corpus-scan exit non-zero if >0 hits in same-issue-already-filed category (block draft until human decides to amend existing instead of file new)
5. Doctor signal `jeff_drafts_unscanned_count` (drafts without corpus-scan evidence)
6. Tests: fixture corpus with seeded prior-art match, anti-pattern match, no-match, similar-issue-exists case
7. canonical-paths.txt entry for corpus-scan tool
8. Companion bead spec for skill `cross-collection-fanout` integration (already exists as skill — leverage, don't reinvent)

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests pass; live scan against current 85-repo index produces meaningful citations on the in-flight Jeff draft
- DOCUMENTED: rubric README updated; AGENTS.md cross-ref added; jeff-issue-chain skill referenced
- SURFACED: doctor signal; daily-report (flywheel-o7dq) Jeff section consumes corpus-scan stats

## Why P1 (not P0)
P0 blocked-by: requires flywheel-9nhx Phase B (accretive learning extraction script + 177/177 indexed). Until those land, this would scan against partial corpus → false confidence. So P1 with explicit dependency: BLOCKED on flywheel-9nhx.

## Doctrine refs
- skill jeff-issue-chain (existing — primary)
- skill cross-collection-fanout (existing — leverage for query)
- skill mcp-secret-scanner (existing — sibling pattern: scan corpus for class)
- bead flywheel-3p1j CLOSED (jeff-issue-rubric — extends this)
- bead flywheel-9nhx OPEN (library-ingestion — gating dep)
- bead flywheel-1lpv OPEN (jeff-intel-network — companion)
- bead flywheel-k5yp OPEN (jeff-philosophy-study — companion)
- /tmp/jeff-corpus-learnings-sample.md (proof-of-concept output shape)

## Skills cited
- jeff-issue-chain
- cross-collection-fanout (existing skill — DON'T reinvent the cross-corpus query)
- socraticode (MCP integration)
- mcp-secret-scanner (sibling shape)
- canonical-cli-scoping (--corpus-scan flag design)
close_reason: Gating dep flywheel-9nhx claim was stale: live probe (mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects) confirms 177/177 jeff-corpus repos fully indexed. Bead deliverable (corpus_aware 8th axis + same-issue rc=4 blocker + companion bead spec for cross-collection-fanout producer) is independent of flywheel-9nhx Phase B (accretive learning extraction). See .flywheel/audit/flywheel-wbnb/evidence.md.

title: publishability-bar-three-judges-doctrine
description:
[publishability-bar-three-judges] every flywheel-touched repo passes the "Jeff/Donella/Josh would fork-and-star on first look" bar — codified goalpost for ALL repos

## Trigger
Joshua 2026-05-04: "if we were to publicize it to github - would our target audience, jeff, donella meadows, and me - Josh - look at and say - wow this is well documented, good codebase, easy to read, and really valuable - I have to download and star this - good work"

## Meadows leverage
- #3 GOALS: this is a goal-rewrite for the entire repo portfolio. Existing implicit goal = "code works + tests pass." New goal = "code works + tests pass + three judges (Jeff, Donella, Josh) would fork-and-star on first look."
- #2 PARADIGM (downstream): once the goal shifts, the paradigm of "what is a finished repo" shifts — README quality, doc clarity, code aesthetic, demo-ability all become first-class success criteria, not afterthoughts.
- #5 RULES (downstream): every PR-merge gate, every "is this done?" question, every doctor signal can refer back to this as the canonical bar.

## The three judges (target audience)
1. **Jeff Emanuel (Dicklesworthstone)** — substrate engineer; values: tight CLI surfaces, doctor/health/repair triads, executable verification, idempotency, lock-files, frontmatter validation, schema versioning, append-only audit. Lens: "would this fit cleanly into the dicklesworthstone-stack? Would I download it?"
2. **Donella Meadows (in spirit)** — systems thinker; values: clear stocks/flows, identifiable feedback loops, named leverage points, paradigm-anchored goals, visible information flows, no parameter-thrashing. Lens: "is the system legible? Could I reason about it as a model?"
3. **Joshua Nowak (Josh)** — founder/operator; values: ZestStream brand voice, agentic coding flywheel doctrine, plan-space>code-space economics, taste, AaaS-grade polish, accretive leverage. Lens: "would I show this to a client? Would I be proud to publicize on github.com/zeststream-ai or my personal account?"

## Three-Judge Rubric (canonical)

A repo passes the bar IFF all 7 facets earn YES from all 3 judges:

1. **README front-door** — within 30 seconds of opening the repo, you understand: what it does, why it exists, who it's for, how to install, how to verify it works, and where to go next. (Jeff: scannable; Donella: legible; Josh: branded)
2. **Doctrine clarity** — the repo names its mission/goal/leverage explicitly (MISSION.md or equivalent at root), with paradigm + stocks/flows + feedback loops surfaced. (Donella YES bar)
3. **Doctor/health/repair triad** — `<repo>-doctor` or equivalent CLI exists, machine-readable JSON output, ties to readable failure modes, has an auto-repair path. (Jeff YES bar)
4. **Tests are executable verification, not theater** — `make test` or equivalent runs in <60s, covers golden path + 2-3 edge cases per public surface, fails loudly. (Jeff YES bar)
5. **Idempotent install + clean uninstall** — `make install` and `make uninstall` both work twice in a row without error; no orphaned state. (Jeff ADOPT pattern)
6. **Code aesthetic** — file structure mirrors mental model; names are precise; no commented-out blocks; no dead code; no slop. (Josh YES bar)
7. **Demo-ability** — there's a copy-paste 3-line demo in the README that produces a visible "wow" within 30 seconds. (All three judges YES)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: doctrine file at .flywheel/PUBLISHABILITY-BAR.md (rubric, three-judges definition, 7 facets, examples of YES/NO per facet)

description:

title: [jeff-corpus-socraticode-index] index 177 cloned Jeff repos for cross-corpus semantic search
description:
## Goal
Run socraticode index on all 177 repos in ~/Developer/jeff-corpus/ to enable cross-corpus semantic search, pattern extraction, and watchtower consumption.

## Cost citation
2026-05-03T20:55Z Joshua flagged: clone done 5h+ ago, deliverables 5/5, but ZERO socraticode indices on the actual corpus. Original mission was "clone+index for watchtower" — got data-on-disk but not consumability. Acceptance-gate framing failure logged as fuckup class=dispatch-acceptance-gate-incomplete-corpus.

## Steps
1. Verify socraticode is healthy: `mcp__socraticode__codebase_about` (or equivalent CLI)
2. For each of 177 repos in ~/Developer/jeff-corpus/<name>/:
   - `mcp__socraticode__codebase_index projectPath=~/Developer/jeff-corpus/<name>`
   - Wait for status complete via `mcp__socraticode__codebase_status`
3. Parallelize (3-5 concurrent indexes if substrate allows, otherwise sequential)
4. Track progress: state/socraticode-index-progress.jsonl with row per repo (start_ts, end_ts, chunk_count, status)
5. After all done: verify `mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects` shows 177+ entries with jeff-corpus prefix

## Acceptance gates
1. >= 170/177 repos indexed (allow 7 tolerance for genuine errors — log them)
2. Per-repo progress JSONL exists at ~/Developer/jeff-corpus/.socraticode-progress.jsonl
3. Test cross-corpus query: `mcp__socraticode__codebase_search query="rust async error handling" includeLinked=true` returns results from multiple jeff-corpus repos
4. Disk usage logged (qdrant collection size delta)
5. Wired into info-source-watchtower or jeff-issue-chain skill (one-line note: "Cross-corpus knowledge available via socraticode_search includeLinked across jeff-corpus/*")
6. INCIDENTS entry: corpus-dispatches-must-include-consumability-gate

## Cross-orch boundary
Read-only on jeff-corpus repos (we cloned them, never pushing). Socraticode indexing writes to qdrant + local socraticode metadata only.

## Skills cite
- socraticode (the indexer itself)
- info-source-watchtower (downstream consumer)
- jeff-issue-chain v1.0.0 (will use this for pattern lookup during Jeff response triage)

## Out of scope
- Indexing repos OUTSIDE jeff-corpus (separate concern)
- Modifying socraticode tool itself
- Changing watchtower skill (separate followup once index proves out)

## DOD
COMMIT must include `[jeff-corpus-socraticode-index]`. Bead closes when 170+/177 indexed AND smoke-test cross-corpus query returns results.

title: [ecosystem-polish] match zeststream-skillos repo polish: README/ARCHITECTURE/CONTRIBUTING/ROADMAP/SECURITY + .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE + PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE
description:
Joshua: 'I want this flywheel repo to match that overall look and feel - we should start applying this methodology to every git repo we have. Even while private, it should start building a super high quality signal to noise ecosystem.'

Reference: https://github.com/jyeswak/zeststream-skillos (and local /Users/josh/Developer/skillos for offline pattern probe)

GAP between flywheel and skillos at root:
- skillos has: README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, ROADMAP.md, SECURITY.md, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/, .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
- flywheel has: NONE of those at root, plus 30+ AGENTS.md.bak.* sediment + .beads.bak.* + .beads.failed.* version-controlled clutter

META-LEVERAGE: Joshua said 'every git repo we have'. This bead must produce a REUSABLE TEMPLATE/SCAFFOLD (skill or generator) that applies the same polish to any flywheel-managed repo, not a one-off flywheel-only fix.

Acceptance gates:
1. flywheel repo gets: README.md (project intro + quickstart, mission anchor reference), ARCHITECTURE.md (.flywheel substrate + lib/* split + recovery-system + dispatch-trust tripod), CONTRIBUTING.md (bead workflow, /flywheel:* commands, dispatch contract, DCG rules), ROADMAP.md (link to .flywheel/PLANS), SECURITY.md (DCG + token-echo doctrine + Infisical references)
2. .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug.md,feature.md,trauma.md} mirroring skillos shape
3. .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with bead-link + 4-lens checklist + DCG-clean verification box
4. AGENTS.md.bak.* sediment archived (tarball under .git-archive/ or moved to /tmp; do NOT git rm without backup)
5. .beads.bak.* and .beads.failed.* dirs gitignored AND archived (per memory: storage discipline)
6. NEW skill: ecosystem-polish-scaffold under ~/.claude/skills/ with template files + apply-to-repo.sh that produces the polish set against any target repo (parameterized by repo name + mission anchor + tech stack)
7. .gitignore tightened: AGENTS.md.bak.*, .beads.bak.*, .beads.failed.*, .ruff_cache, .pytest_cache, .continue-here.md
8. Three-judges (Jeff/Donella/Joshua) sniff on the produced README + ARCHITECTURE: composite >= 9.0/10
9. Test harness: tests/test_ecosystem_polish_scaffold.sh produces a clean dir from the skill, asserts all 5 doc files + .github/* exist + .gitignore patterns present

Out of scope: actually applying the skill to every other repo — that's a follow-up bead per repo. THIS bead delivers (a) flywheel polished + (b) the reusable skill that does the polish for any target.

Reference: zeststream-skillos github URL for canonical voice + structure. Joshua brand voice from skill 'zeststream-brand-voice'.

Doctrine link: doctrine_brand_voice + product_naming + signal-to-noise principle.

title: [beads-source-repo-basename-normalization] 286 JSONL rows have source_repo='flywheel' instead of absolute path — leakage_count=253, blocks doctor pass
description: Beads DB at /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.beads/beads.db has leakage_count=252-253 because 286 .beads/issues.jsonl rows have source_repo='flywheel' (basename) instead of source_repo='/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel' (canonical absolute path). These rows ARE legitimate flywheel beads — the field was incorrectly normalized at write time. Distribution: 1353 rows OK, 286 rows malformed. The leakage_count gate at lib/doctor.d/part-02-check_beads_db_health-to-detect_tests_json.sh:87 forces beads_db_health.status=fail when leakage>0, which forces flywheel-loop doctor top-level status=fail. title: [C] schedule tentacle drift sweep
description:
## Goal
Add recurring drift sweep that records Jeff checkout lag and alerts before drift becomes blocker.

## Context
ntm was 514 commits behind silently; source drift needs recurring measurement.

## Dependencies
Depends on C11.

## Inputs / Outputs
Files in scope: launchd/flywheel-weekly-refresh integration; tentacle-drift.jsonl; substrate registry
Output artifact: committed implementation and/or evidence artifact named in acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria
- drift sweep writes JSONL rows with repo, local_head, upstream_head, commits_behind, status; warns at threshold and routes via chosen alert path

## Testing Obligations
- Run sweep dry-run and verify JSONL append in temp or live-approved path; validate thresholds.

## Origin
00-MASTER-PLAN.md §IV.5 lines 330-337; §VI lines 456-457.

## Out of Scope
Do not auto-pull or mutate repos.

## Definition of Done

- Dependency graph remains acyclic after close.

title: [infra-codex] sidecar install codex-cli 0.129 + /goal canary on pane 4
description:
## Source

Joshua signal 2026-05-08: X (Twitter) feedback says GPT-5.5 + /goal mode "is insane". Asked for research on codex /goal compat.

## Findings

| Version | /goal | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 0.125.0 (current pin) | absent | no goal code |
| 0.128.0 | broken | issues #20548, #20591, #21477 |
| 0.129.0 | working | cut 2026-05-07 — discoverable, paused-across-resume, clearer validation |
| 0.130.0-alpha | unstable | dev only |

GPT-5.5 compat: ChatGPT signin still works in 0.129; API-key path still unsupported (unchanged from 5/5 baseline).

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Sidecar-install codex-cli 0.129 to second binary path (e.g. /Users/josh/.local/bin/codex-129) — does NOT replace /Users/josh/.local/bin/codex (0.125 pin stays canonical for 23 active panes during canary).

AG2: On flywheel:4 only — relaunch worker pane with sidecar binary. Verify chatgpt-signin works, GPT-5.5 reachable, no freeze regression in first 4h.

AG3: Enable features.goals=true in scoped ~/.codex/config.canary.toml. Issue /goal <objective> on pane 4 with a real bead-shaped objective.

AG4: 24h canary — track pane-4 throughput vs panes 2/3 on 0.125. Compare four_lens scores, callback latency, freeze events. Halt rollout if regressions.

AG5: If canary clean, document version-bump procedure in .flywheel/doctrine/codex-version-pin-canary.md + propagate to peer orchs (alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, skillos) via doctrine-sync 6h cadence. Roll fleet pin to 0.129 only after 48h aggregate canary.

## Cross-references

- Memory: reference_codex_gpt55_upgrade_findings_2026_05_05.md (5/5 baseline — needs update post-0.129 cut)
- Memory: reference_codex_0_129_goal_mode_2026_05_08.md (today's research)
- L142: CODEX-PREPARED-CHEVRON validator — 0.129 chevron behavior unverified
- L143: WORKER-CLOSE-REQUIRES-GIT-COMMIT
- Issue #20548 / #20591 / #21477 (0.128 /goal bugs)

## Anti-patterns (refused)

- DON'T fleet-rollout 0.128 (broken /goal, known regressions)
- DON'T conflate 0.129 with freeze fix — orthogonal; watchdog substrate is the freeze fix
- DON'T bump 23 panes simultaneously — canary one pane first
- DON'T migrate to paid-API path (~2142/mo extra) just to get /goal — 0.129 + chatgpt signin still works
close_reason: sidecar and goal config verified; gpt-5.5 reachable; 0.129 canary failed stability and rollout halted via flywheel-mspmr

comments.author: MagentaPond
comments.text: Canary closeout: AG1 sidecar verified (/Users/josh/.local/bin/codex-129 -> codex-cli 0.129.0) while canonical /Users/josh/.local/bin/codex remains codex-cli 0.125.0. AG3 scoped config has features.goals=true. 0.129 sidecar reached gpt-5.5 via ephemeral exec and returned OK_X2OKL_MODEL_REACHABLE. AG2/AG4 canary stability failed: flywheel-mspmr records two 0.129 /goal freeze occurrences, so rollout is halted and AG5 is not applicable until a clean future canary. Evidence pack: /tmp/flywheel-x2okl-compliance/flywheel-x2okl/; L112: bash /tmp/flywheel-x2okl-validation/l112-probe.sh.

title: [doctrine] replace four-lens self-grade with beads-compliance citation-bound rubric
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=doctrine-upgrade-from-jeff-skill-2026-05-07
joshua_directive=2026-05-07T~19:30Z "I want our four grade lens to be upgraded / replaced with this new skill"

## Why

The current four-lens (brand/sniff/jeff/public) is worker-claimed self-grading. Workers report `four_lens=brand:9,sniff:9,jeff:9,public:9` in callbacks; orch logs and trusts. That's a **claim**, not a fact. Jeff's `/beads-compliance-and-completion-verification` skill (1.2MB, 41 scripts, 23 subagents, 59 references) is the empirical-court replacement: spec.json + evidence.json + compliance.json + theater.json + test_depth.json + scorecard.md + REPORT.md per closed bead, with deterministic citation-bound rubric scoring + audit-of-the-audit phase 10.

GPT-5's framing (today): "closed is not truth, it is a claim." Same applies to four-lens at 9.0 self-graded.

## Surfaces to update

1. **close-handler skill** (`~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/_shared:close-handler` + agent-mail:_shared:close-handler):
   - Remove `four_lens=brand:N,sniff:N,jeff:N,public:N` field from required callback shape
   - Add `compliance_score=X/1000` (from beads-compliance evidence pack) + `compliance_pack_path=<audit-dir>/<bead-id>/`
   - Worker emits evidence pack at close time, NOT a self-grade

2. **flywheel-loop tick + worker-tick skill**:
   - Step that ran four-lens collection → run `bash ~/.claude/skills/beads-compliance-and-completion-verification/scripts/run-pass.sh --bead-id <id> --threshold 700` instead
   - On per-bead close: spec.json+evidence.json+compliance.json must exist before the close row hits dispatch-log.jsonl
   - Convergence check before close gate per skill's 10-phase loop

3. **dispatch-template + dispatch-tool-contracts skill**:
   - Update boilerplate so workers know to produce evidence packs not self-grades
   - Cite the compliance skill's EVIDENCE-SCHEMAS.md as the contract
   - Worker callback now includes evidence_pack_path instead of four_lens scores

4. **quality-bar-close-gate.sh** (`.flywheel/scripts/quality-bar-close-gate.sh`):
   - Replace four-lens-composite-≥9.0 check with compliance_score ≥ 700/1000 from skill output
   - Add convergence-streak field per skill (2 consecutive zero-finding rounds)

5. **STATE.json schema in /flywheel:plan**:
   - `quality_bar_evidence[]` field already exists; rename `jeff_score`/`donella_score`/`joshua_score` → `compliance_jeff_score`/etc OR just store a single `compliance_pack_path` pointer and compute everything from the pack
   - Schema migration on existing plans (`schema_version: 3 → 4`)

6. **AGENTS.md L-rule**:
   - File new L126 "EVIDENCE-PACK-REPLACES-SELF-GRADE" generalizing L91 (four-state receipt) into the wider compliance evidence-pack contract
   - Mark four-lens as **deprecated** (not removed; legacy beads keep it) but new closures must produce evidence pack
   - Cross-ref the compliance skill's "One Rule" + DESIGN-PHILOSOPHY.md

7. **memory updates**:
   - Add feedback memory: `feedback_evidence_pack_replaces_four_lens.md`
   - Update `worker-orchestration` skill cheat sheet
   - Update `orchestrator-validation-discipline` skill

## Hard rules

- **Backward compat:** legacy closed beads with four_lens entries are NOT migrated. The replacement is forward-only.
- **Threshold:** start at 700/1000 per skill default. Tunable via L-rule update later.
- **Tier routing:** per skill SKILL.md, < 20 closed → Solo (1 agent serial run-pass.sh). At our pace, every plan close-gate run uses Solo unless plan exceeds 20 beads.
- **Gradient:** start with new plans only. Existing in-flight plan (ntm-surface-validation-followup-2026-05-07) keeps four-lens through its close. Cutover at next /flywheel:plan invocation.

## Acceptance criteria

1. /flywheel:plan close-gate refuses plans without evidence packs
2. close-handler skill emits evidence_pack_path field; workers stop emitting four_lens
3. dispatch-template references EVIDENCE-SCHEMAS.md
4. quality-bar-close-gate.sh compliance_score ≥ 700 gate works
5. L126 landed in AGENTS.md + AGENTS-CANONICAL.md
6. canonical-meta-rules-sync watchdog propagates L126
7. fuckup-heuristics.json + INCIDENTS.md cross-references added
8. tests: at least 1 plan goes through full new flow end-to-end (ideally the *next* plan we author)

## Non-acceptance

- Do NOT modify any closed beads' four_lens fields
- Do NOT rerun beads-compliance against ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07 within this bead (that's the in-flight pane-3 work; doesn't block the doctrine swap)
- Do NOT adopt skill's full Mega-swarm tier (10-agent cap) prematurely; we'd hit that ~1500+ closed beads, not today

## Out of scope

- Multi-repo audit setup (skill MULTI-REPO-AUDIT.md) — fleet-wide rollout after flywheel-only validation
- Sample mode tuning (skill MODES-AND-TIERS.md) — defaults are fine until we hit 1500+ closed
- Bayesian verification (skill mentions it) — phase 2 once Solo tier is proven

## Mission anchor
