title: [josh-req-codex-parity] capture Codex-originated Joshua requests
description:
## Goal
Design and implement a Codex-runtime capture path for Joshua-originated requests so the Josh-request substrate is not Claude-Code-only.

## Context
flywheel-d62z closed the documented Claude hook, CLI, tick prelude, and forward-only schema v2 path. Gate 5 intentionally did not implement Codex parity because Codex CLI has no UserPromptSubmit-equivalent hook surface today. This bead owns that parity gap under the flywheel-2p25 runtime parity epic.

## Dependencies
Depends on flywheel-orx1 because Codex worker panes currently lack reliable ~/.local/bin PATH visibility for substrate tools. Tagged under flywheel-2p25.

## Options
A. Recommended: wrap `ntm send` for Codex-bound dispatches and append request-shaped human/orchestrator prompts into ~/.local/state/flywheel/josh-requests.jsonl with captured_via=ntm_send or the v2-compatible equivalent provenance fields.
B. Periodically tail Codex pane scrollback for new Joshua input. Rejected unless A is impossible because scrollback parsing is fragile.
C. Wait for Codex CLI to ship a hook surface upstream. Track as future simplification, not the operational fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Option A is implemented or superseded by a stronger non-fragile capture surface.
- Codex-originated Joshua request fixture writes a schema v2-compatible JSONL row.
- Duplicate dispatch/tick prompts do not create duplicate request rows.
- Parity probe under flywheel-2p25 reports Codex request capture as wired.

## Testing Obligations
- Run a Codex-pane or fixture smoke that exercises the ntm wrapper path.
- Validate JSONL row has the schema v2 required fields.
- Confirm old Claude UserPromptSubmit hook still passes.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(josh-requests): add Codex capture parity [josh-req-codex-parity]
- Evidence references flywheel-d62z Gate 5.
- AUTONOMY: autonomous

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[josh-req-codex-parity] capture Codex-originated Joshua requests` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-xap2` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.
close_reason: done

title: [gap-hunt-probe-faqj2] memory-without-cross-link class is name-grep-only — misses semantically embedded discipline (e.g., dispatch template's VERIFY-CALLBACK BLOCK)
description:
Discovered during flywheel-2xdi.109 (memory-without-cross-link wire-in for feedback_dispatch_post_send_verify_for_silent_deaf.md).

The probe's memory-without-cross-link class flags a memory as un-linked when its FILENAME doesn't appear in sampled commands/doctrine/incidents/plans. But the memory's DISCIPLINE can be deeply embedded behaviorally without the filename appearing.

Empirical example (this bead):
- Memory: feedback_dispatch_post_send_verify_for_silent_deaf.md
- Discipline: 'callback_delivery_verified' as required field; 'robot-tail polling' as verification primitive; 'silent-deaf' as failure class
- Behavioral embedding: dispatch-template.md has 6 mentions of 'callback_delivery_verified', the VERIFY-CALLBACK BLOCK enforces the contract — but the memory FILENAME isn't cited
- Probe flagged the memory because name-grep returned 0 hits

Net effect: probe over-files memory-without-cross-link beads even when the memory IS load-bearing in runtime artifacts. Workers correctly resolve by creating a doctrine doc citing the memory (this bead + 2xdi.93 pattern), but the underlying detection is myopic.

Fix shapes:
- Option A: scan for memory's key discipline tokens (extracted from frontmatter description + section headers), not just filename
- Option B: scan a wider corpus (dispatch templates, *_TEMPLATE.md, _shared/*.md files)
- Option C: define a 'semantic cross-link' metric: if memory's discipline tokens appear in N>=3 runtime artifact lines, treat as cross-linked
- Option D: accept the false-positive rate (cheap wire-in via doctrine doc; 2xdi.93 + 2xdi.109 resolved in <15min each)

Acceptance:
- [ ] Decide: tighten the class (A/B/C) or accept current behavior (D)
- [ ] If tightening: regression test asserting memory with embedded-but-not-named discipline doesn't flag
- [ ] If accepting: document the FP rate in probe's class taxonomy comment

Sister: flywheel-2xdi.93 + flywheel-2xdi.109 (both 'forward-link doctrine doc' wire-ins for memories that were already behaviorally embedded).

Parent: flywheel-2xdi.

Self-calibration hint cited by orch dispatch ('harvest any blind-spot finding into next-tick faqj2 self-calibration').
close_reason: resolved-upstream-via-flywheel-kwjja-Option-D-accept-current-FP-rate-document-3-revisit-triggers-the-mechanized-forward-link-recipe-auto-injection-from-pmg3c-makes-name-grep-only-class-resolution-cheap-enough-no-probe-tightening-needed

title: [skill-hygiene] agent-ergonomics SKILL.md should document operator-on-demand scripts to eliminate wired-but-cold FP cluster (10+ flagged)
description:
Follow-on to flywheel-2xdi.65 (wired-but-cold triage of audit-replay.sh in agent-ergonomics skill).

Investigation findings:
- Skill: ~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools
- Scripts dir has 47 scripts total
- SKILL.md (748 lines) references ~20 scripts by name (39 'scripts/' mentions, ~20 unique)
- Currently 10+ scripts from this skill flagged wired-but-cold by gap-hunt-probe (e.g., audit-replay.sh, cluster-recommendations.sh, diff_test.sh, dirty-surfaces.sh, log-provenance.sh, log-telemetry.sh, migrate-scores.sh, render_scorecard_html.sh, rubric-fitness.sh, scorer-prompt-fitness.sh, skill-self-test.sh)
- These scripts are operator-on-demand tools (self-documented usage patterns; not invoked by tick/cron/launchd)

Class identification:
- gap-hunt-probe's wired-but-cold class correctly identifies these as cold (no continuous wiring)
- BUT they're not dead code — they're "operator-on-demand by design" (analogous to flywheel-2xdi.58's tests/test_*.sh class but for skill-internal tools)
- The skill_md_corpus check in probe_wired_but_cold catches scripts mentioned in SKILL.md prose — these 10+ scripts just need SKILL.md mentions

Fix shape (skill-hygiene, not gap-hunt-probe calibration):
- Audit agent-ergonomics SKILL.md against scripts/ inventory
- For each script in scripts/ NOT mentioned in SKILL.md, add a 1-line reference in the appropriate section (e.g., "Operator tools: scripts/audit-replay.sh — replay old audit against current source")
- Once SKILL.md mentions all 47 scripts (or explicitly excludes deprecated ones), gap-hunt-probe's skill_md_corpus will recognize them as documented entry points

Boundary: ~/.claude/skills/ is SEPARATE REPO per project_skillos_separated.md. Skill-side change, not flywheel-repo change.

AG1: audit agent-ergonomics scripts/*.sh against SKILL.md mentions
AG2: add SKILL.md references for documented operator-on-demand scripts; mark deprecated/removed ones explicitly
AG3: re-run gap-hunt-probe; verify the 10+ flagged scripts no longer appear in wired-but-cold list (cap=20 freed for genuine other-skill gaps)
AG4: receipt at .flywheel/audit/<this-bead>/evidence.md with before/after counts

Sister patterns:
- flywheel-2xdi.58 (auto-allowlist tests/test_*.sh — same operator-on-demand class but for flywheel-repo tests)
- flywheel-e7lxv (launchd corpus extension)
- flywheel-kckw8 (probe-without-receiver 3-corpus extension)

Side observation: 47 scripts in 1 skill is unusual. If the skill is in maintenance mode, some scripts may be legitimately dead — those should be deleted (not documented). Audit may surface both new-SKILL.md-references AND deletion candidates.

This is a SKILL-WIDE hygiene fix not a per-script triage; resolving via 1 SKILL.md edit pass clears 10+ wired-but-cold FPs at once (Meadows #5 leverage).

title: [gap-hunt-probe-enhancement] cluster-detection — auto-emit single cluster bead for multi-script wired-but-cold in same skill substrate
description:
Sister to flywheel-r9pri (doctrine-promotion-N3 closed). r9pri shipped Option A (doctrine doc only); this bead is Option B (auto-detection in gap-hunt-probe).

## Current behavior

gap-hunt-probe.sh `probe_wired_but_cold()` files individual beads per script. When a skill substrate has multiple wired-but-cold scripts (e.g., 03yaj had 26 scripts in research-triad), each becomes its own auto-bead.

## Desired behavior

When the probe detects 2-or-more wired-but-cold scripts under the same `.claude/skills/<x>/` substrate, file ONE cluster-maintainer bead instead of N individual ones:

- Cluster bead title: `[<skill-name>-cluster-doc-completeness] N wired-but-cold scripts in .claude/skills/<x>/`
- Body cites .flywheel/doctrine/cluster-maintainer-pattern.md plus the recipe
- Body enumerates the N flagged script paths

## Implementation shape

In `probe_wired_but_cold`, after the gap loop:
1. Group gaps by skill-substrate dir (parse path for `.claude/skills/<x>/`)
2. If any group has 2 or more entries, replace individual gaps with one cluster gap

Then auto-bead-filer (create_bead) emits ONE bead per cluster group plus individual beads for non-clustered gaps.

## Acceptance

- Implement cluster-detection in probe_wired_but_cold
- Regression test: synthetic fixture with 3 wired-but-cold under same skill emits 1 cluster gap (not 3)
- Regression test: 2 wired-but-cold in DIFFERENT skills still emit 2 individual gaps (no false clustering)
- Cluster bead body cites .flywheel/doctrine/cluster-maintainer-pattern.md
- Sister probe metric: bead-generation rate drops when clusters exist (measured via tick-over-tick comparison)

## Why not in r9pri scope

r9pri shipped Option A (doctrine doc only) per kwjja Option D precedent (cheapest mechanization). This bead is the next-tier mechanization once the doctrine is sanctioned.

## Sister doctrine

- .flywheel/doctrine/cluster-maintainer-pattern.md (the doctrine this enhancement mechanizes)
- flywheel-r9pri (N=3 doctrine-promotion; this bead's parent)

Parent: flywheel-r9pri.
close_reason: done

title: [worker-stall-recovery] integrate-side detection of pane=ERROR-after-callback should escalate via L95
description:
## Gap surfaced by flywheel-ovd29 (2026-05-09)

12 fuckup-log rows on 2026-05-03 19:11-20:07Z (mobile-eats:0.1) under trauma_class `worker_capacity_gate_failed`. The pattern split into two sub-shapes:

**Sub-shape A (low severity, 6 events 19:11-19:36Z)**: pane 2 THINKING, INTEGRATE correctly defers. Already covered by parent class `integrate_worker_active` (INCIDENTS line 5945).

**Sub-shape B (medium severity, 6 events 19:41-20:07Z)**: pane 2 went to ERROR state. The first such event explicitly notes:
> 'INTEGRATE tick aborted because pane 2 robot-activity was ERROR, not WAITING, **despite mobile-eats-o73 callback being available**.'

The callback for `mobile-eats-o73` was DELIVERED. The worker reported DONE. But pane 2 ended up in ERROR state, and the INTEGRATE gate kept refusing for 6 cycles (~30 minutes) without escalating to worker-stall recovery (AGENTS.md L95). The canonical recovery path was never triggered.

## Why this is a substrate gap, not just doctrine drift

The parent class `integrate_worker_active` Forever-Rule says:
> 'continue only through the L91/L95 path: live activity/capture proof, dispatch-log callback state, output-advance or stall receipt, and the worker-stall recovery ladder if activity stops advancing.'

The ladder exists in doctrine (L95). But in practice, the INTEGRATE-side gate kept calling 'capacity gate failed' for 30 minutes without invoking it. That's a CALLER-SIDE gap: the integrate-prelude probe stops at 'pane not WAITING' and re-fires the same fuckup-log row instead of escalating.

## Definition of Done

- Locate the integrate-prelude code path that emits `worker_capacity_gate_failed` (likely .flywheel/scripts/ or ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/lib/).
- After N consecutive emissions of the trauma class on the same pane (suggested N=3), invoke the worker-stall escalation path (per L95 + worker-stall-alert-probe.sh).
- Regression test: pane stuck at ERROR after callback delivery for >15 minutes triggers the canonical recovery (respawn/escalation/Joshua notify per L95 ladder).

## Live evidence (re-runnable)

```bash
# Sub-shape B 6 events
grep worker_capacity_gate_failed ~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl | jq -r 'select(.severity == "medium") | [.ts, .what_happened] | @tsv'
# expected: 6 rows 2026-05-03T19:41:37Z onward, all 'pane 2 robot-activity was ERROR, not WAITING'

# Find emitter
grep -rn 'worker_capacity_gate_failed' /Users/josh/.claude/skills/.flywheel/ /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/ /Users/josh/Developer/mobile-eats/.flywheel/scripts/ 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Confirm worker-stall-alert-probe exists (canonical surface)
ls /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/worker-stall-alert-probe.sh
```

## Boundary

Read-only research bead. Does NOT mutate the integrate-prelude probe or worker-stall-alert-probe.sh — that's the closing worker's scope. Filed by flywheel-ovd29 close (MistyCliff, 2026-05-09) so the integrate-side N-strikes-then-escalate gap doesn't get re-discovered next time the trauma fires.
close_reason: done

title: [coord-bug] L107 release-then-git-add race bundles other panes' appends into wrong commit
description:
## Pattern observed (2026-05-09T20:30Z)

L107 shared-surface reservation does not extend to the `git add` step. When Pane A:
1. Acquires L107 reservation on INCIDENTS.md
2. Appends entry
3. Releases L107
4. Runs `git add INCIDENTS.md && git commit`

…and Pane B:
1. Acquires L107 reservation between Pane A's release (step 3) and Pane A's `git add` (step 4)
2. Appends ITS entry
3. Releases

…then Pane A's `git add` stages BOTH appends, and Pane A's commit message claims authorship of work it did not do.

Concrete instance today (2026-05-09T20:30:30Z):
- Pane 3 commit `37d0de7` claims `incidents(wwinm): cross-reference orch-punt-to-next-tick`. That commit's INCIDENTS.md diff also contains the `mobile-eats-dispatch-health-gate-fail` entry — appended by pane 2 (this dispatch, `flywheel-wb6oc`) AFTER pane 3 released L107 but BEFORE pane 3 staged + committed. The 5 occurrences of `mobile-eats-dispatch-health-gate-fail` in `git show 37d0de7` are this dispatch's entry, not pane 3's work.

## Root cause

L107 reservation lifecycle is reserve → write → release. The git-add+commit step is OUTSIDE the lifecycle. When concurrent appenders share the same file, releasing L107 before staging exposes a race window where another pane's append gets bundled into the wrong commit.

## Proposed fix (one of)

1. **Extend L107 lifecycle** — reservation must be held through `git add` AND `git commit`, not just through file write. Worker contract: `--reserve → write → git add+commit → --release`.
2. **Pathspec-scoped staging** — `git add INCIDENTS.md` is too broad. Use `git add --intent-to-add INCIDENTS.md` then a precise pathspec patch. But this is fiddly and doesn't really solve the issue.
3. **Worktree-isolated edits** — workers writing to shared markdown files use a per-task worktree, then merge. Heavy.
4. **Pre-stage append + atomic-add** — write the new content to a tmp file, `git add` the original, then concatenate + `git add` again with an explicit byte-range. Fragile.

Best option: (1) extend L107 lifecycle. Update `shared-surface-reservation-check.sh` worker docs and orchestrator dispatch contract to require reservation-held-through-commit.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] L107 contract clarified in `.flywheel/rules/L061-L107-shared-surface-writes-must-reserve-across-panes.md` to include git add+commit
- [ ] Worker dispatch packets cite the extended lifecycle in the SHARED-SURFACE RESERVATION BLOCK
- [ ] Regression test: spawn two concurrent appenders against a fixture file; assert each commit includes ONLY its own append
- [ ] No regression to existing reservation behavior (file lock still released after commit)

## Definition of Done

`git show <pane-A-commit>` and `git show <pane-B-commit>` each contain only their own append, even when both panes target the same shared file in the same minute.

title: [validation-e2e] ship end-to-end smoke harness and staged rollout gate
description:
### Title

`[validation-e2e] ship end-to-end smoke harness and staged rollout gate`

### Goal

Prove the validation system works end to end before declaring it shipped. This bead ties schema, dispatch block, callback validator, doctor signals, VALIDATE tick phase, auto-open/reopen, learn routing, no-punt chaining, doctrine wire-in, and Codex parity into one staged smoke harness.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`, `loop-driver-active-but-prompt-shell-executed`, `dispatch-acceptance-gate-incomplete-corpus`, and `documented-bug-not-actioned-self-recursion`. The plan exists because partial surface checks repeatedly shipped without end-to-end proof.

### Acceptance gates

1. End-to-end fixture dispatches a synthetic worker packet with validation block. Proof receipt: `B12_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Synthetic worker DONE claims a missing artifact; validator fails before summary/integration. Proof receipt: `B12_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Failed validation produces fix-bead dry-run payload and doctor `callbacks_validated_with_failures_count=1`. Proof receipt: `B12_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Tick enters VALIDATE and refuses INTEGRATE until remediation route exists. Proof receipt: `B12_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Learn routing sees exactly one failed validation event and does not duplicate it. Proof receipt: `B12_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. L70 fixture proves DISPATCH->BEADS->DISPATCH chaining or concrete blocker. Proof receipt: `B12_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Codex parity fixture proves agent-context callback path or records q03g blocker. Proof receipt: `B12_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
8. Rollout plan has feature flags or staged modes: schema-only, warn-only doctor, strict doctor, mutating remediation. Proof receipt: `B12_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
9. Final receipt records every changed surface: command, template, doctor, tick, learn, docs, skill, tests. Proof receipt: `B12_AG9=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `e2e_cmd=<cmd>:PASS rollout_mode=<mode> receipts=<dir> doctor=<cmd>:PASS tick=<cmd>:PASS commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[validation-e2e]`.

### Doctrine references

L50, L52, L53, L56, L60, L61, L69, L70; `flywheel-1z65`; `flywheel-7lby`; `flywheel-2p25`; `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md`.

### Out of scope

No new feature scope beyond proving already implemented beads; no retroactive validation of every historical closed bead unless Joshua explicitly approves.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B05, B08, B09, B10, B11.  
Blocks: final plan closeout and implementation rollout.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings and Lane B-prime if final rollout must adopt Jeff pattern names or additional upstream-derived gates.

### Phase 3 audit amendments before creation

Audit sources: `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:38`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:40`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:51`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:79`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:82`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:84`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:119`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:120`.

- Add paired `claude_agent_context_fixture` and `codex_agent_context_fixture` for the same validation primitive.
- Add negative raw-shell/agent drift fixture.
- Add closeout-integrity e2e cases for invalid no-bead reason, `BLOCKED` without `fuckups_logged`, closed bead artifact missing, and tick punt.
- Final receipt must prove every B04 signal has producer+fixture or explicit no-signal/no-bead reason.
- B12 depends on B13 and B14 in addition to the B01-B12 pre-draft graph.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B12_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B12_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B12_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B12_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B12_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B12_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B12_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG8` closes with `B12_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG9` closes with `B12_AG9=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [fleet-skill-discovery-substrate] implement skill-discoveries JSONL schema and append helper
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/fleet-skill-reporting-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-xbuh

## Goal

Define and implement the append-only fleet skill discovery substrate at `~/.local/state/flywheel/skill-discoveries.jsonl` so workers across sessions can report reusable patterns with stable `sd-*` IDs.

## Scope

- Add a schema or schema documentation for `skill-discovery/v1`.
- Add a small append/validate helper under the existing flywheel script or `flywheel-loop` surface.
- Support discovery kinds from plan lines 63-74: `pattern-emerged`, `pattern-recurrence`, `skill-search-miss`, `skill-found-but-incomplete`, `skill-broken-yaml`, `cross-repo-shared-pattern`, `anti-pattern`.
- Enforce required fields: `ts`, `discovery_id`, `session`, `worker_pane`, `worker_kind`, `task_context`, `discovery_kind`, `candidate_skill_name`, `evidence`, `promotion_signal`, `should_become`, `blocking_current_work`.
- Use lock-safe append semantics for multi-session writes.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `test -f ~/.local/state/flywheel/skill-discoveries.jsonl` passes after init/apply, and the file is appendable without destructive truncation.
2. `flywheel-loop skill-discovery --schema --json | jq -e '.schema_version == "skill-discovery/v1" and (.discovery_kinds | length == 7)'` passes, or equivalent script schema command passes.
3. `flywheel-loop skill-discovery append --candidate-skill-name fleet-liveness-watchdog --discovery-kind pattern-emerged --session fixture --worker-pane 2 --worker-kind codex --task-context fixture --evidence-json '{}' --dry-run --json | jq -e '.dry_run == true and .row.discovery_id | startswith("sd-")'` passes.
4. Apply-mode append writes exactly one JSONL row and `tail -1 ~/.local/state/flywheel/skill-discoveries.jsonl | jq -e '.discovery_id and .candidate_skill_name and .evidence and .promotion_signal'` passes.
5. Invalid `discovery_kind` fails with non-zero exit and a machine-readable error naming allowed kinds.
6. Concurrent append fixture with at least 5 background writers produces 5 valid unique `sd-*` rows and no partial JSON lines.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: schema, dry-run, apply, invalid-kind, and concurrent append tests run locally.
- DOCUMENTED: schema fields and discovery kinds are documented in the command/help or schema file.
- SURFACED: rows land in the global JSONL where skillos coordinator, doctor, and Agent Mail consumers can read them.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L50, L51, L52, L56, L61, L71, L80.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No skillos promotion logic.
- No Agent Mail thread creation.
- No dispatch template changes.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented skill discovery JSONL substrate; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[fleet-skill-discovery-substrate]`.

Codex watchtower 2026-05-08T03:00Z fired CODEX_WATCHTOWER_HIGH with 62 new issues / 23 relevant. Pinned at codex-cli 0.125.0.

## Triaged impact

**ACTIONED THIS TICK:**
- #21660 (rollout JSONLs world-readable) — confirmed all 1194 of our rollouts at ~/.codex/sessions/ were mode 0644. Mass chmod 0600 applied; new janitor plist ai.zeststream.codex-rollout-permission-janitor loaded (15m cadence) for forward-fix. Defense-in-depth per L125 ENV-FILE-IS-SEALED-SUBSTRATE class.

**REAL IMPACT — workaround needed:**
- #21620 (workspace-write cannot write inside repo subdirectory agents/) — ALPS uses `agents/` extensively (alps repo + 8+ worktrees). Codex workers dispatched to alps that try to write inside agents/ will fail. Workaround: use absolute paths or alternate subdirectory until upstream fix. Confirm impact with alps:1 / CoralRaven before changing dispatch contracts.

**POSSIBLY RELEVANT (no action this tick):**
- #21654 (MCP server tool subset issue) — could explain weird worker behavior; needs reproducer
- #21699 (Shift+Enter not in tmux due to no modifyOtherKeys / Ext 2) — affects keyboard ergonomics in tmux codex panes; not blocking
- #21615 (IDE/wrapper hook trust) — could affect claude-code wrapping codex, low priority
- #21673 (compact handoff command request) — feature request, observe

**NOT RELEVANT TO US:**
- #21605, #21607, #21635, #21641, #21664, #21692, #21693, #21703, #21705 — UI/Desktop/Windows/macOS-Settings/Chrome-plugin issues
- #21672, #21681, #21704 — Chrome plugin variants
- #21639 — Codex Desktop hooks (we use CLI)
- #21640 — image_gen tool (not used)
- #21666 — Neovim cursor (not us)
- #21606 — Windows sandbox (not us)
- #21653 — multi-line status line (feature request)

## References

- Watchtower ledger: /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/codex-watchtower/daily-2026-05-08.jsonl
- Janitor: /Users/josh/.local/bin/codex-rollout-permission-janitor
- Janitor plist: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.codex-rollout-permission-janitor.plist
- Janitor ledger: /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/codex-rollout-permission-ledger.jsonl

## Acceptance gates

AG1: rollout permission janitor plist loaded and healthy (verified ts=2026-05-08T09:03:30Z, fixed_count=0 after mass-fix).
AG2: alps:1 ACK on #21620 risk class with workaround plan.
AG3: Re-triage on next CODEX_WATCHTOWER_HIGH; close this bead when codex-cli upgrades past the relevant fix versions.
close_reason: 2026-05-09 Codex watchtower re-triage complete; AG1 janitor healthy; AG2 ALPS ACK captured; AG3 21 relevant issues triaged; follow-up flywheel-ie2en filed for #21869.

title: [jeff-stack J4] dedupe-existing-19-repo-clones
description:
Dedupe the 19 existing Jeff repo clones at ~/Developer/<name>/ vs the new canonical at ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<name>/.

DEPENDS ON: J3 (canonical clones complete).

PROBLEM: Joshua already had 19 Jeff repos cloned at ~/Developer/<name> (e.g., ~/Developer/ChatTTS, ~/Developer/vibe_cockpit, ~/Developer/wezterm). After J3, these duplicate canonical clones at ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<name>. Need exactly one source-of-truth per repo.

DECISION (per Joshua earlier): canonical = ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/. Existing 19 should become symlinks to canonical or be removed.

PROCEDURE:
1. List all ~/Developer/* git repos with Dicklesworthstone origin (use git remote get-url):
   `find ~/Developer -maxdepth 2 -name .git -type d | xargs -I{} dirname {} | xargs -I{} sh -c 'cd "{}" && url=$(git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null); case "$url" in *Dicklesworthstone*|*jeffreyemanuel*) echo "{}";; esac'`
2. For each existing flat-path clone:
   - If working tree has uncommitted changes: STOP and report to orchestrator (Joshua needs to decide). Never auto-delete uncommitted work.
   - If clean: rename existing to ~/Developer/<name>.bak.<ts>, then symlink ~/Developer/<name> -> ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<name>
   - Verify symlink resolves and `cd ~/Developer/<name> && git status` works
3. Write summary to /tmp/jeff-dedupe-report.md: {repo, action: symlinked|preserved-uncommitted|skipped}

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. All 19 originally-flat clones are now symlinks to canonical (or preserved as .bak with reason)
2. No git operations broken: spot-check 5 symlinks with `git -C ~/Developer/<name> status`
3. No data loss: any preserved .bak documented with diff summary
4. Tools that referenced old paths still work (frankensqlite skill, vibe_cockpit skill, etc.)

ROLLBACK:
- Each symlink action logs the original path. To rollback: rm symlink, mv .bak.<ts> back to original name.
close_reason: superseded by flywheel-ys7em [jeff-daily-corpus-diff] per Joshua signoff 2026-05-10. Original J-chain assumed local-clone substrate; replacement uses GitHub API daily activity poll. Spec at .flywheel/audit/jeff-daily-corpus-diff/apply-spec.md

title: Implement L66 data-backed deferral guard
description:
## Goal
Implement the data-backed dispatch deferral guard designed by flywheel-28v so orchestrators stop asking Joshua when evidence already selects a safe action.

## Context
PHASE: L66 implementation follow-up
CRITERION: autonomy-guardrail
Doctor in /Users/josh/Developer/skillos surfaced trauma_class=orchestrator-asked-instead-of-decided at /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl#L185. The row says Phase 2 REFINE r1 had 5 Joshua-disposes, all recommended yes, with 3552 lines / 3 lanes / 5+ alignments, but the orchestrator asked for blanket approval instead of declaring the decisions and proceeding. L66 is already canonical and points to flywheel-28v as the durable target; flywheel-28v closed with plan-space design only, so this bead is the implementation owner.

## Dependencies
Depends on: flywheel-28v closed design artifact /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md. Related: flywheel-8i5 dispatch enforcement design.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: flywheel-28v design, AGENTS.md L66, INCIDENTS.md meat-puppet/orchestrator decision incidents, fuckup-log row 185, dispatch-log/session topology/ready-work signals.
OUTPUTS: hook, tick check, or dispatch-skill guard that detects question-shaped deferral when data supports a concrete next action; fixtures proving allowed ambiguity remains allowed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detector catches question-shaped deferral phrasing when the L66 four-check gate passes: idle capacity, ready work, prior wave data points one way, and action is in doctrine.
- Detector emits structured violation class orch_meat_puppet_question_with_sufficient_data or a compatible L66 class with the missing evidence fields included.
- Genuine ambiguity or missing data must pass only when the output names the specific missing datum or tie.
- Regression fixture covers the 2026-05-02 row: 5 Joshua-disposes, all recommended yes, cross-lane convergence, and an ask-for-blanket-approval output.
- No raw pane transport is introduced; any pane probes use ntm surfaces.

## Testing Obligations
- Unit tests for pass/fail L66 four-check gate fixtures.
- Smoke or dry-run that shows the guard emits a SOFT violation without mutating source state.
- br dep cycles --json remains empty.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(flywheel): implement L66 deferral guard [flywheel-XXXX]
- Tests green
- AUTONOMY: autonomous
close_reason: Implemented L66 data-backed deferral guard as flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check. Fixtures catch row185 and ntm#116; allow named tie and missing datum; tests/data-backed-deferral-guard.sh 5/5; br dep cycles empty. confidence=HIGH

title: [convergence-autoloop-diagnose-repair] repair before negative-cache skip
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/convergence-bead-plan-2026-05-01.md`

## Scope

Patch `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-autoloop` so failed/warn/interrupted repos are diagnosed and repaired before they are skipped or negative-cached.

## Required Behavior

- Widen candidate selection from only `status=="ready"` to `status IN ("ready","warn","fail","interrupt")`.
- Score candidates deterministically: `fail=100`, `interrupt=90`, `warn=70`, `ready=50`.
- Replace raw `doctor_failed` negative-cache writes with `diagnose_and_repair_doctor_failure()`.
- Map doctor action `docs_state=drift_detected` to the lock-repair tool.
- Retry doctor after a repair attempt.
- Cache only `repair_failed` or `repair_not_available` when repair cannot recover.
- Replace raw `tick_failed` cache path with `diagnose_and_repair_tick_failure()` for missing dirs, malformed overrides, and permission issues.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash -n ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-autoloop` passes.
2. Dry-run fixture with a failing repo selects that repo before ready-only candidates.
3. Doctor-failure fixture calls lock-repair for `docs_state=drift_detected`.
4. Successful repair fixture retries doctor and avoids negative-cache `doctor_failed`.
5. Failed repair fixture writes `repair_failed` or `repair_not_available`, never raw `doctor_failed`.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented autoloop diagnose-then-repair; tests=<cmd>; fixture=<path>; cache_reason=<value>`.

## Dependencies

Can start independently, but full docs-state repair requires the lock-repair implementation bead.

## Out Of Scope

Agent wakeups, model calls, launchd interval changes, and arbitrary source edits.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: syntax and fixture gates prove behavior.
- DOCUMENTED: repair reasons and cache states are captured in script/help or README.
- SURFACED: repair failures must be visible in autoloop status/log JSON.
notes: VALIDATOR BLOCK_CLOSE 2026-05-04: open_child=flywheel-ahlv OPEN; jeff_lens=FAIL (contract_without_version); public_lens=FAIL (no_bar_self_grade). Three structural fails — cannot close.

title: [doctrine-prop] broadcast doctrine-update-2026-05-07.L126 to peer orchs (alps mobile-eats zesttube picoz)
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07
parent=flywheel-owirq

## Why
Today's apply pass stamped 52 L-rule appends across 4 fleet repos but the running peer orchestrators (alps:1, mobile-eats:1, zesttube:1, picoz:1) were not informed. Their AGENTS.md files were updated on disk; their tick-loops will discover the new rules whenever they next read AGENTS.md, but that's passive discovery — silent until tick fires. Per Joshua 2026-05-07T~20:30Z directive: peer orchs need active notification so they can update local processes (close-handler emit shape, evidence-pack contract, fuckup-heuristics).

This is the structural gap that the broadcast-template surface (templates/peer-orch-broadcasts/doctrine-update-template.md) was built for but I didn't actually use.

## Acceptance
1. Author 4 broadcast messages (one per peer orch) using doctrine-update-template.md as the boilerplate
2. Each broadcast cites:
   - L-rules added (specific list per repo)
   - Provenance: flywheel commit 11d3153 + doctrine_version=2026-05-07.L126
   - Action items: "L126 evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade — your callbacks should now emit compliance_score+compliance_pack_path instead of four_lens=brand:N,sniff:N,jeff:N,public:N"
   - Reference: this flywheel commit + receipt path in their .flywheel/receipts/doctrine-sync/
3. Send via Agent Mail through `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__send_message` to each peer orch's registered identity
4. Verify delivery via inbox-check on each peer's project_key
5. Skillos: separate broadcast NOT sent; deferred to flywheel-rdqc7 cohort plan
6. Compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract

## Hard rules
- Token-handle safety per L58 (Agent Mail send-redacted; no tokens in plaintext)
- Anonymized broadcast bodies — no josh/flywheel paths, only repo-relative references
- L107: write broadcast drafts to /tmp/ first, send only after lsof check
- One broadcast per peer orch; do NOT bundle into a multi-recipient message

## Cross-refs
- templates/peer-orch-broadcasts/doctrine-update-template.md (the template surface)
- .flywheel/PLANS/doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07/01-VERIFY-PASS.json (per-repo missing lists pre-apply)
- flywheel commit 11d3153 (doctrine-sync infrastructure)
- L58 token-handle safety
- L126 evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade (the load-bearing rule peer orchs must absorb)
close_reason: BLOCKED-then-resolved: agent-mail contact handshake gap. Drafts written + anonymized clean. Resolution: rely on tick-time passive discovery (already wired in Step 1a awareness) for tonight; structural fix queued as new bead to wire contact-handshake into /flywheel:onboard so future doctrine broadcasts work.

title: [orch-no-punt-chain] implement L70 same-tick phase chaining and ticks_punted_count gate
description:
### Title

`[orch-no-punt-chain] implement L70 same-tick phase chaining and ticks_punted_count gate`

### Goal

Implement the mechanical gate for L70: when DISPATCH names BEADS, BEADS names DISPATCH, INTEGRATE names LEARN, or any worker callback names a next phase and capacity exists, the tick chains that phase in the same tick or records a concrete blocker.

### Why now

Trauma class: `orch-punt-to-next-tick-instead-of-next-actionable`. This pre-draft task itself exists because L70 was applied immediately after Lane C instead of waiting for Lane B-prime. `flywheel-7lby` is P0 and requires the chain gate.

### Acceptance gates

1. Tick driver detects `next_phase=BEADS` from DISPATCH with `br_ready=0` and plan artifacts present, then runs BEADS same tick. Proof receipt: `B08_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Tick driver detects new ready beads after BEADS and chains DISPATCH same tick. Proof receipt: `B08_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Tick driver detects `next_phase=LEARN` and unprocessed fuckup-log rows after INTEGRATE and chains LEARN same tick. Proof receipt: `B08_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. If capacity is exhausted or safety gates fail, receipt includes `chain_blocked_reason=<concrete cause>`. Proof receipt: `B08_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Missing chain and missing blocker increments `ticks_punted_count`. Proof receipt: `B08_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Dispatch packets include worker `chain_if_capacity` block from B02. Proof receipt: `B08_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Tests include DISPATCH->BEADS->DISPATCH, INTEGRATE->LEARN, worker callback `next_phase=X`, and legitimate chain-blocked cases. Proof receipt: `B08_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `chain_cmd=<cmd> fixtures=<cmd>:PASS ticks_punted_probe=<cmd>:PASS receipt=<path> commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[orch-no-punt-chain]`.

### Doctrine references

L60, L70; `flywheel-7lby`; `feedback_orchestrator_must_dispatch.md`; `feedback_flywheel_never_idles.md`; `feedback_data_guides_decisions_not_human_judgment.md`.

### Out of scope

No new bead creation from plans beyond invoking existing BEADS phase; no cross-session dispatch into mobile-eats; no bypass of safety/capacity gates.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B02, B04, B05.  
Blocks: B10, B12.  
Related existing bead: `flywheel-7lby`.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings if `ticks_punted_count>=1` threshold is changed from fail to rollout warn.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B08_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B08_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B08_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B08_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B08_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B08_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B08_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [doctor-validation-signals] wire callback validation signals into flywheel-loop doctor
description:
### Title

`[doctor-validation-signals] wire callback validation signals into flywheel-loop doctor`

### Goal

Add doctor signals for validation discipline using the L60 producer/measurement/consumer/promotion pattern. This bead makes unvalidated callbacks, validation failures, tick punts, unwired surfaces, missing closed-bead artifacts, invalid receipts, context drift, and unrouted validation events visible to the orchestrator.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`, `orchestrator-idle-with-actionable-work`, `documented-bug-not-actioned-self-recursion`, and `skill-substrate-validation-drift`. Lane A found doctor signals are partial and callback validation is not yet a doctor-gated state.

### Acceptance gates

1. Doctor emits `callbacks_unvalidated_count`. Proof receipt: `B04_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Doctor emits `callbacks_validated_with_failures_count`. Proof receipt: `B04_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Doctor emits `ticks_punted_count`. Proof receipt: `B04_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Doctor emits `surfaces_unwired_count`. Proof receipt: `B04_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Doctor emits `closed_bead_artifact_missing_count`. Proof receipt: `B04_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Doctor emits `validation_receipts_schema_invalid_count`, `agent_context_probe_drift_count`, and `validation_events_unrouted_count` if those producers exist. Proof receipt: `B04_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Every signal documents producer, measurement, consumer, and promotion behavior. Proof receipt: `B04_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
8. Strict mode fails on `callbacks_unvalidated_count>=1` after consumer proof; rollout mode can warn until B12 passes. Proof receipt: `B04_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
9. Doctor output includes JSON fields stable enough for tick receipts and `/flywheel:status`. Proof receipt: `B04_AG9=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `doctor_cmd=<cmd> signals=<list> fixture_cmd=<cmd>:PASS strict_behavior=<warn|fail> commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[doctor-validation-signals]`.

### Doctrine references

L60, L68, L70; `flywheel-doctor-author`; `flywheel-1z65`; `flywheel-7lby`; `feedback_substrate_watchtower_must_be_wired.md`.

### Out of scope

No tick phase insertion, no auto-open mutator, no parity probe implementation.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B03.  
Blocks: B05, B08, B09, B11, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings if thresholds change, especially whether `ticks_punted_count>=1` is immediate fail or rollout warn.

### Phase 3 audit amendments before creation

Audit sources: `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:37`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:43`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:51`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:71`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:87`.

- Add `orchs_with_capture_gap_count` after B13 defines capture-parity producer output.
- Add `surfaces_unwired_count` after B14 defines surface-registry producer output.
- `agent_context_probe_drift_count>=1` must block strict parity rollout and emit stable doctor JSON.
- Replace conditional silent producer wording with per-signal rows: source, producer, measurement, consumer, threshold, gate behavior, promotion path.
- B12 final receipt must prove every B04 signal has a producer+fixture or explicit `no_signal_reason` / `no_bead_reason`.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B04_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B04_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B04_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B04_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B04_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B04_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B04_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG8` closes with `B04_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG9` closes with `B04_AG9=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [gap-hunt-probe] skill_md_corpus per-file 4KB cap truncates large SKILL.md files — flagged scripts in Scripts table past byte-4096 stay cold
description:
Regression introduced by flywheel-2xdi.66 (broadened SKILL.md → all *.md corpus with per-file cap 4KB to fit ~5500 files in 32MB budget).

Trade-off: when a SKILL.md is >4KB and references scripts in its `## Scripts` table past byte-4096, those scripts are NOT captured by skill_md_corpus, so they're falsely flagged as wired-but-cold.

Empirical example: `~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools/SKILL.md` is 748 lines. The Scripts table starts at line 596. With 4KB cap, only the first ~100 lines are corpus-scanned. Scripts mentioned ONLY in the Scripts table (after line 596) appear cold even though they're well-documented.

Currently affected (live probe):
- audit-readme-vs-help.sh (mentioned in SKILL.md line 630)
- build-canonical-tasks.sh (line 622)
- measure-help-readtime.sh (line 629)
- run_simulation.sh (line 615)
- sw-self-audit.sh (line 628)
- verify-determinism.sh (line 626)
- verify-non-tty-discipline.sh (line 627)
- (+ others)

These all show in gap_ids as wired-but-cold even though they are explicitly documented in SKILL.md.

Proposed fix: SKILL.md is the canonical entry-point doc; treat it as a privileged corpus source that bypasses the per-file cap. Other markdown (references/**.md, assets/**.md) keeps the cap. Pseudocode:

```python
for path in candidates:
    if used >= overall_cap: break
    cap = per_file_cap if path.name != "SKILL.md" else overall_cap - used
    text = read_text(path, cap)
    ...
```

Or: a separate skill_md_only_corpus() function (no cap) called alongside the broader skill_doc_corpus() (with cap). The probe's wired-but-cold check then ORs both.

Acceptance:
- [ ] gap-hunt-probe captures FULL SKILL.md content per skill (no per-file truncation)
- [ ] other in-tree *.md still capped to prevent budget starvation
- [ ] regression test asserts: a SKILL.md >10KB with a script name at byte 8000 is captured
- [ ] live probe re-run: the 7+ scripts listed above unflag without any SKILL.md edits

title: [skillos-fleet-mail-notify-gap] Wire routed decision notices
description:
## Goal
Wire skillos routed-decision notifications so `needs_fleet_mail` rows reach flywheel through L61/L65 dual-channel delivery.

## Context
PHASE: skillos goal-rotation substrate repair
CRITERION: flagship-anchor-skillos
Dispatch `/tmp/dispatch_skillos-meadows-goal-rotation_2026_05_03.md` found 5 rows in `~/.local/state/flywheel/skillos-routed.jsonl`, all stuck at `notification_status=needs_fleet_mail` since 2026-05-03T03:44:34Z. Topology has skillos `fleet_mail_identity=FoggyBear` and flywheel `fleet_mail_identity=LavenderGlen`; LavenderGlen has a token in the fleet-mail vault, but FoggyBear currently has metadata only and no sender token vaulted. Previous LavenderGlen -> FoggyBear intake worked; the missing path is skillos -> flywheel.

## Dependencies
None. Must precede claims that routed-decision notification loop is healthy.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: `/tmp/skillos-meadows-rotation_findings.md`, `/tmp/skillos-state-v2-update-draft.md`, `~/.local/state/flywheel/skillos-routed.jsonl`, `~/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl`, `~/.local/state/flywheel/fleet-mail-tokens/`, L61/L65 doctrine.
OUTPUTS: token-safe sender auth or relay primitive, paired fleet-mail + ntm poke delivery, updated notification statuses or blocked-with-owner receipts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Authenticate or relay as skillos fleet-mail identity without exposing tokens in pane text.
- Send notification for all 5 current routed rows, grouped if appropriate.
- Pair every fleet-mail send with an ntm poke containing message id and project key.
- Update each routed row to `notified` or `blocked_with_owner` with evidence.
- Add a regression guard so future `needs_fleet_mail` rows cannot linger silently beyond one cadence window.

## Testing Obligations
- Run token-vault doctor or equivalent redacted probe.
- Prove recipient fetch path works without echoing tokens.
- Validate JSONL after status updates.
- SKILLS: agent-mail, agent-security, beads-workflow.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: feat(skillos): wire routed-decision fleet-mail notifications [bead-id]
- Routed rows drained or explicitly blocked.
- L61/L65 dual-channel evidence captured.
- AUTONOMY: autonomous, except any Joshua-required token surfacing is explicit.
close_reason: FoggyBear canonical vault file created with 0600 perms; token-safe smoke message id 41 validated; 5 routed decisions notified in fleet-mail msg id 42 and paired with ntm poke; append-only status rows written to skillos-routed.jsonl.
